description: A silvery-leaved perennial sacred to the Moon goddess Artemis, long revered as the premier dreaming herb across European, Chinese, and Indigenous traditions. Mugwort bridges the waking and dreaming worlds, opening the gates of nocturnal vision while simultaneously grounding the digestive and menstrual systems in healthy rhythm.
habitat: Roadsides, riverbanks, waste ground, and disturbed soils throughout temperate regions. Thrives in moist, nitrogen-rich soil near waterways.
energetic_quality: Warming, drying, opening. Moves stagnant energy downward and outward while opening the upper gates of perception.
doctrine_of_signatures: The silver-white undersides of the leaves reflect moonlight, marking this as a lunar plant. Its preference for growing near waterways echoes the Moon's governance of tides and bodily fluids.
safety_notes: Avoid during pregnancy as it is a uterine stimulant. Not recommended for extended continuous use. Contains thujone, which in large doses can be neurotoxic. May cause allergic reactions in individuals sensitive to Asteraceae family plants.

parts.typical_compounds: Thujone, 1,8-cineole, camphor, linalool, flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones
parts.therapeutic_properties: Digestive bitter, emmenagogue, nervine, oneirogen (dream-enhancing), mild sedative, antispasmodic
parts.notes: The primary medicinal part. Used as tea for digestive and menstrual support, smoked or placed under the pillow for dream enhancement, and bundled as smudge for energetic cleansing.

parts.typical_compounds: Inulin, volatile oils, tannins, sesquiterpene lactones
parts.therapeutic_properties: Tonifying, grounding, emmenagogue, digestive tonic
parts.notes: The root is more grounding and tonifying than the leaf. Used as a decoction for deep menstrual and digestive support. Harvested in autumn when energy has descended.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Subtle nervine, energetic opening, vibrational medicine
parts.notes: Used primarily as a flower essence for opening psychic sensitivity and dream recall. The flowering tops can also be tinctured for a lighter, more ethereal preparation.

planet_associations.notes: Named for Artemis, goddess of the Moon. Governs dreams, the menstrual cycle, and the rhythmic tides of the body. The silver-backed leaves are a direct lunar signature.

zodiac_associations.notes: Cancer is ruled by the Moon and governs the stomach and womb, both primary targets of mugwort's medicine.
Pisces governs the dream state, the pineal gland, and spiritual receptivity, all of which mugwort opens and enhances.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Mild sedative action promotes sleep onset. Best used in combination with dream intention for restorative, visionary sleep.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried leaf per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Drink 30-60 minutes before bed.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Emmenagogue action promotes menstrual flow and regulates cycle timing. Traditionally used for delayed or scanty menses.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 30-60 drops of tincture, 2-3 times daily in the luteal phase. Discontinue once menstruation begins.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Bitter principles stimulate digestive secretions and promote peristalsis, relieving bloating and sluggish digestion.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1 teaspoon dried leaf per cup, steeped 10 minutes. Drink before meals.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Thujone and other volatile compounds promote vivid, lucid dreaming when smoked in small amounts before sleep or placed in a dream pillow.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Small pinch smoked in a pipe or rolled blend before bed, or 1-2 tablespoons dried leaf in a dream pillow placed near the head.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Flower essence opens receptivity to subtle perception and reconnects the individual to the dreaming and intuitive faculties.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 4 drops under the tongue, 4 times daily, or as intuitively guided.

contraindication_associations.reason: Contains thujone which acts as a uterine stimulant and emmenagogue, potentially inducing contractions and increasing miscarriage risk
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during all trimesters; traditionally used specifically to induce menstruation and promote delayed menses

contraindication_associations.reason: Thujone and other volatile constituents pass into breast milk and may be neurotoxic to infants
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid internal use entirely while nursing; even topical moxa use should be discussed with a practitioner

contraindication_associations.reason: Contains coumarin derivatives that may potentiate anticoagulant effects and inhibit platelet aggregation
contraindication_associations.notes: Use caution with warfarin, heparin, or other blood-thinning medications; monitor INR if combining

contraindication_associations.reason: Thujone is a GABA-A receptor antagonist and convulsant at higher doses, lowering the seizure threshold
contraindication_associations.notes: Contraindicated in epilepsy and other seizure disorders; may interfere with anticonvulsant medications

contraindication_associations.reason: Member of the Asteraceae/Compositae family; cross-reactivity common in individuals allergic to ragweed, chrysanthemums, or marigolds
contraindication_associations.notes: Perform a small patch test before topical use; discontinue immediately if allergic reaction occurs

description: The preeminent adaptogenic root of Ayurvedic medicine, known as 'Indian Ginseng' for its ability to restore depleted vitality and resilience. Ashwagandha uniquely combines energizing and calming qualities, rebuilding the adrenal reserves while simultaneously quieting an overactive stress response.
habitat: Dry, stony soils in arid and semi-arid regions. Grows as a small woody shrub in grasslands and open forests.
energetic_quality: Warming, moistening, grounding. Builds deep reserves of vital energy while calming the nervous system.
doctrine_of_signatures: The root system is strong, deep, and tenacious, reflecting the grounding and foundational strength this plant imparts. The species name 'somnifera' (sleep-inducing) points to its calming, restorative nature.
safety_notes: Generally well-tolerated. Avoid during pregnancy. May potentiate thyroid medications due to thyroid-stimulating activity. Caution with immunosuppressants as it modulates immune function. Nightshade family member; rare sensitivity possible.

parts.typical_compounds: Withanolides (withaferin A, withanolide D), alkaloids, steroidal lactones, iron
parts.therapeutic_properties: Adaptogenic, anxiolytic, anti-inflammatory, immunomodulating, thyroid-supporting, neuroprotective
parts.notes: The primary medicinal part. Dried root is used as powder, capsule, or tincture. Traditional Ayurvedic preparation involves simmering the powdered root in milk with ghee and honey.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, insecticidal
parts.notes: Leaves contain higher concentrations of withaferin A. Used topically for wounds and swelling in traditional practice. Less commonly used internally than the root.

planet_associations.notes: Mars governs vitality, muscular strength, and adrenal function. Ashwagandha rebuilds the martial reserves of energy, endurance, and resilience that chronic stress depletes.

zodiac_associations.notes: Aries rules the head and the initiating force of the body. Ashwagandha restores the primal vitality and courage associated with Aries energy.
Scorpio governs regeneration and deep transformation. Ashwagandha supports the body's regenerative capacity at the cellular level through its adaptogenic action.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Modulates the HPA axis and reduces cortisol levels. Multiple clinical trials demonstrate significant reduction in perceived stress and cortisol biomarkers.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300-600mg standardized extract (root), twice daily with meals. Full adaptogenic effects develop over 4-8 weeks of consistent use.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Rebuilds depleted adrenal reserves and enhances mitochondrial function. Improves energy, endurance, and recovery from exertion without stimulant effects.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300-600mg standardized root extract, twice daily. Traditional Ayurvedic dose: 3-6g powdered root simmered in warm milk before bed.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: GABAergic activity of withanolides produces anxiolytic effects comparable to lorazepam in animal studies. Clinical trials show significant reduction in anxiety scores.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 30-60 drops of tincture (1:3 ratio), 2-3 times daily. Effects are cumulative; allow 2-4 weeks for full anxiolytic benefit.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Withaferin A inhibits NF-kB signaling and reduces inflammatory cytokines. Supports joint health and systemic inflammation reduction.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300-600mg standardized root extract, twice daily with food. Combine with anti-inflammatory diet for best results.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Withanolides provide neuroprotective and immunomodulatory effects, helping regulate the overactive immune response characteristic of MS.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-4 ml tincture or 300-600 mg extract, twice daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Clinical studies show ashwagandha normalizes TSH and T4 levels in subclinical hypothyroidism, supporting thyroid function in Hashimoto's.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300-600 mg root extract twice daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Stimulates thyroid hormone production by enhancing T4 to T3 conversion. Clinical trials show significant improvements in TSH and T4 levels.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300-600 mg root extract twice daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory effects address the HPA axis dysfunction and widespread pain characteristic of fibromyalgia.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300-600 mg root extract twice daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Adaptogenic support for HPA axis dysfunction and mitochondrial energy production, addressing core fatigue mechanisms in CFS.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300-600 mg root extract twice daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Premier adaptogen for restoring HPA axis function and normalizing cortisol rhythms disrupted by chronic stress.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300-600 mg root extract twice daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Immunomodulatory withanolides help regulate overactive immune responses while supporting overall vitality.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300-600 mg root extract twice daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Clinical evidence for reducing anxiety and stress-related psychiatric symptoms through GABAergic and serotonergic modulation.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300-600 mg root extract twice daily. Use as adjunct under psychiatric supervision.

contraindication_associations.reason: Classified as an abortifacient in Ayurvedic texts; withanolides may induce uterine contractions and have shown spermatotoxic and embryotoxic effects in animal studies
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during all trimesters; large doses have traditionally been used to terminate pregnancy

contraindication_associations.reason: Potent immunostimulatory activity upregulates Th1 cytokines and natural killer cell activity, potentially exacerbating autoimmune flares
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid or use with medical supervision in lupus, rheumatoid arthritis, multiple sclerosis, and Hashimoto's thyroiditis

contraindication_associations.reason: Increases thyroid hormone production (T3 and T4) via stimulation of the hypothalamic-pituitary-thyroid axis; may also modulate estrogen receptor activity
contraindication_associations.notes: Contraindicated in hyperthyroidism; use caution with thyroid cancer or estrogen-receptor-positive cancers

contraindication_associations.reason: Case reports of clinically apparent liver injury with hepatocellular pattern; withanolides undergo extensive hepatic metabolism
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor liver enzymes if using with pre-existing liver conditions; discontinue if jaundice or elevated ALT/AST occurs

contraindication_associations.reason: Withanolides demonstrate significant hypoglycemic activity by enhancing insulin secretion and improving insulin sensitivity
contraindication_associations.notes: May potentiate diabetes medications; monitor blood glucose closely and adjust medication doses with practitioner guidance

contraindication_associations.reason: May increase gastric acid secretion and cause gastrointestinal irritation, particularly in concentrated extract form
contraindication_associations.notes: Take with food to reduce GI irritation; avoid high-dose extracts if active peptic ulcers are present

description: The sacred flower of ancient Egypt, depicted throughout temple art and funerary texts as a gateway between worlds. Blue Lotus produces a gentle euphoria and dreamy, contemplative state that enhances meditation, sensual appreciation, and visionary experience without the intensity of stronger entheogens.
habitat: Still and slow-moving freshwater bodies including ponds, lakes, and marshes. Roots in muddy substrate with leaves and flowers floating on the surface.
native_region: East Africa, Nile River region, Egypt
energetic_quality: Cooling, moistening, opening. Gently dissolves mental rigidity and opens the doors of subtle perception.
doctrine_of_signatures: The flower opens at dawn and closes at dusk, mirroring the cycles of consciousness between waking and dreaming. Rising from muddy waters to bloom in pristine beauty, it embodies spiritual emergence from material conditions.
safety_notes: Mild psychoactive effects. May potentiate sedatives and alcohol. Not recommended during pregnancy or nursing. Legal status varies by jurisdiction. Use with intention and in a safe setting.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Mild euphoric, anxiolytic, oneirogen, aphrodisiac, sedative, antispasmodic
parts.notes: The primary and most sacred part. Traditionally steeped in wine or tea. The petals and stamens contain the highest concentration of active alkaloids. Blue and purple petals are most prized.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Astringent, nutritive, grounding
parts.notes: The rhizome was consumed as food in ancient Egypt. Less psychoactive than the flower but provides grounding nutritive support.

planet_associations.notes: Neptune governs visionary states, the dissolution of boundaries, and transcendent experience. Blue Lotus gently opens the Neptunian gates of dreaming and spiritual perception.
Uranus governs awakening and the liberation of consciousness from habitual patterns. Blue Lotus catalyzes subtle shifts in awareness that break through ordinary perception.

zodiac_associations.notes: Pisces rules the dream state, the pineal gland, and spiritual dissolution. Blue Lotus is the quintessential Piscean flower, dissolving boundaries between self and cosmos.
As an aquatic plant governed by lunar rhythms of opening and closing, Blue Lotus resonates with Cancer's watery, nurturing, and intuitive nature.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Nuciferine acts as a dopamine receptor modulator producing gentle anxiolysis and relaxation without heavy sedation.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 grams dried flower petals steeped in hot water for 10-15 minutes. Drink 1 cup in the evening.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Mild sedative properties promote sleep onset and deepen sleep quality. Works synergistically with dream-enhancing effects.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 30-60 drops of tincture in warm water, 30-60 minutes before bed.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Ancient Egyptian use centered on visionary dreaming. The alkaloid profile promotes vivid, memorable dreams and lucid awareness during sleep.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 grams dried flowers steeped in hot water or warm wine. Consume 1-2 hours before sleep with clear dream intention.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Opens the heart and third eye to subtle perception and reconnection with the sacred dimension of existence.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 4 drops under the tongue, 4 times daily, or added to bath water for an immersive vibrational treatment.

contraindication_associations.reason: Contains aporphine alkaloids (nuciferine, apomorphine) with dopaminergic activity that may affect uterine tone and fetal neurological development
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid entirely during pregnancy; psychoactive alkaloids pose unknown risks to fetal development

contraindication_associations.reason: Apomorphine and nuciferine act on dopamine D1/D2 receptors and serotonin receptors, potentially destabilizing mood and perception in vulnerable individuals
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid in schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, and psychotic disorders; may trigger dissociative or hallucinatory episodes

contraindication_associations.reason: Nuciferine has antiarrhythmic and hypotensive properties; apomorphine can cause QT prolongation and cardiovascular depression
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid with cardiac medications, particularly antiarrhythmics and beta-blockers; monitor blood pressure

contraindication_associations.reason: Psychoactive alkaloids including nuciferine and apomorphine are lipophilic and likely pass into breast milk, posing neurodevelopmental risks to infants
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid entirely while breastfeeding; no safety data exists for infant exposure

contraindication_associations.reason: Nuciferine has demonstrated hypoglycemic effects in animal models by modulating insulin signaling pathways
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor blood glucose if diabetic; may require adjustment of hypoglycemic medication dosing

description: Among the most extensively researched entheogens in modern clinical science, psilocybin mushrooms have demonstrated remarkable efficacy for treatment-resistant depression, end-of-life anxiety, and addiction. These fungi represent an ancient lineage of consciousness-expanding organisms with deep roots in Mesoamerican ceremonial tradition.
habitat: Subtropical and tropical regions, growing on cattle dung and enriched soils in pastures and meadows. Requires warm, humid conditions with indirect light.
native_region: Central America, South America, Southeast Asia, subtropical regions worldwide
energetic_quality: Expansive, dissolving, illuminating. Opens the psyche to vast inner landscapes while dissolving rigid patterns of thought and identity.
doctrine_of_signatures: Emerging from decay and dung, the mushroom transforms waste into wisdom, reflecting the alchemical principle that the greatest medicine arises from the lowest material. The blue bruising upon handling reveals its psychoactive nature.
safety_notes: Powerful psychoactive. Controlled substance in most jurisdictions. Contraindicated with lithium and in individuals with personal or family history of psychotic disorders. Requires careful set and setting. Should only be used under experienced guidance. Physical toxicity is extremely low but psychological risks exist without proper preparation.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Serotonergic psychedelic, neuroplasticity promoter, anxiolytic (at sub-perceptual doses), antidepressant, consciousness-expanding
parts.notes: The entire fruiting body is consumed. Psilocybin content varies by strain and growing conditions. Both caps and stems are active, with caps generally containing slightly higher concentrations. Can be consumed fresh, dried, as tea, or in capsule form.

planet_associations.notes: Neptune dissolves the boundaries of ordinary perception and opens the gates to visionary experience. Psilocybin mushrooms are the quintessential Neptunian medicine, facilitating direct encounter with the transpersonal and the numinous.

zodiac_associations.notes: Pisces governs transcendence, spiritual dissolution, and the oceanic feeling of cosmic unity that characterizes the psilocybin experience.
Scorpio rules death-rebirth transformation. The psilocybin experience often involves ego dissolution and psychological rebirth, core Scorpionic themes.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Multiple clinical trials at Johns Hopkins and Imperial College London demonstrate rapid, sustained antidepressant effects from 1-2 sessions, including in treatment-resistant cases. Promotes neuroplasticity and breaks ruminative thought patterns.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Clinical protocols use 25mg synthetic psilocybin (equivalent to approximately 3.5g dried mushroom) in supervised therapeutic sessions. Sub-perceptual microdoses of 50-200mg are used for ongoing support.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Reliably produces mystical-type experiences rated among the most meaningful of participants' lives in clinical research. Opens perception to non-ordinary dimensions of awareness.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Full ceremonial dose: 2-5g dried mushroom brewed as tea with lemon juice. Requires experienced facilitation and careful set and setting.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: NYU and Johns Hopkins trials demonstrate significant and sustained reduction in death anxiety and existential distress in patients with life-threatening diagnoses.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Clinical protocols use supervised single high-dose sessions (25mg psilocybin) with psychotherapeutic integration before and after.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Sub-perceptual microdosing protocols show promise for generalized anxiety. Full-dose sessions can produce lasting anxiety reduction through confrontation and resolution of core fears.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Microdose protocol: 50-200mg dried mushroom, every 3rd day for 4-8 weeks. Full dose: supervised clinical setting only.

contraindication_associations.reason: Psilocybin and psilocin cross the placental barrier; serotonergic activity during fetal development may disrupt critical neurodevelopmental processes including neural tube formation
contraindication_associations.notes: Absolute contraindication during all trimesters; no safe dose has been established for pregnancy

contraindication_associations.reason: Potent 5-HT2A receptor agonist that profoundly alters perception and cognition; can trigger persistent psychosis, severe anxiety, or depersonalization in predisposed individuals
contraindication_associations.notes: Contraindicated in personal or family history of schizophrenia, bipolar I disorder, or psychotic disorders; may precipitate first psychotic break

contraindication_associations.reason: Serotonergic activation via 5-HT2A receptors can lower seizure threshold; intense neurological arousal during psychedelic experience may trigger seizures
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid entirely with epilepsy or history of seizures; may interact with anticonvulsant medications

contraindication_associations.reason: Psilocybin increases sympathetic nervous system activation, raising heart rate and blood pressure acutely; 5-HT2B receptor agonism poses theoretical risk of valvulopathy with chronic use
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid with severe cardiovascular disease, uncontrolled hypertension, or history of cardiac arrhythmias

contraindication_associations.reason: Psilocin is a small lipophilic molecule likely to pass into breast milk; serotonergic compounds may affect infant neurodevelopment and sleep regulation
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid entirely while breastfeeding; pump and discard milk for at least 24 hours after any exposure

contraindication_associations.reason: Psilocybin undergoes hepatic dephosphorylation to psilocin via alkaline phosphatase; impaired liver function may alter metabolism and increase duration and intensity of effects unpredictably
contraindication_associations.notes: Compromised hepatic metabolism may lead to prolonged psychoactive effects; avoid with severe liver impairment

description: One of humanity's oldest cultivated plants, used for fiber, food, medicine, and sacrament for over 10,000 years. Cannabis produces a complex entourage of cannabinoids and terpenes that interact with the body's endocannabinoid system, offering a remarkably versatile therapeutic profile spanning pain, anxiety, nausea, and neurological conditions.
habitat: Highly adaptable annual herb that thrives in temperate to tropical climates with well-drained, nitrogen-rich soil and abundant sunlight.
native_region: Central Asia, likely the Hindu Kush region
energetic_quality: Complex and strain-dependent. Sativa-leaning varieties are warming and uplifting; indica-leaning varieties are cooling and sedating. Overall, cannabis opens and relaxes.
doctrine_of_signatures: The resinous trichomes that coat the female flowers glisten like tiny crystalline structures, suggesting the plant's affinity for the nervous system and crystalline clarity of perception. The palmate leaf shape resembles an open hand, offering its gifts freely.
safety_notes: Psychoactive in THC-dominant preparations. Legal status varies widely. May exacerbate anxiety or psychosis in susceptible individuals. Avoid smoking during pregnancy. CBD-dominant preparations have minimal psychoactive risk. Adolescent use may affect brain development.

parts.typical_compounds: THC, CBD, CBN, CBG, beta-caryophyllene, myrcene, linalool, limonene, pinene
parts.therapeutic_properties: Analgesic, anti-emetic, anxiolytic (CBD-dominant), appetite stimulant, muscle relaxant, anti-inflammatory, bronchodilator
parts.notes: The unfertilized female flower (sinsemilla) contains the highest concentration of cannabinoids and terpenes. Consumed smoked, vaporized, or extracted. The specific terpene and cannabinoid profile determines the therapeutic effect.

parts.typical_compounds: Lower concentrations of cannabinoids, chlorophyll, flavonoids
parts.therapeutic_properties: Mild anti-inflammatory, nutritive, antioxidant
parts.notes: Fan leaves contain minimal THC but provide nutritive value when juiced raw. Sugar leaves near the flowers contain moderate cannabinoid levels and are used for extractions.

parts.typical_compounds: Essential fatty acids (omega-3, omega-6), complete protein, vitamin E, minerals
parts.therapeutic_properties: Nutritive, anti-inflammatory, cardiovascular support, skin-nourishing
parts.notes: Hemp seeds contain no psychoactive compounds. An exceptional food source with ideal omega fatty acid ratios. Used as whole seeds, hulled hearts, oil, or protein powder.

planet_associations.notes: Uranus governs liberation, altered states, and the breaking of rigid patterns. Cannabis shifts perception, liberates the mind from habitual thought patterns, and catalyzes creative and unconventional thinking.

Saturn's association reflects cannabis's grounding, sedating qualities (particularly indica varieties) and its structural role as a fiber plant (hemp). Saturn also governs restriction, reflecting the plant's complex legal history.

zodiac_associations.notes: Ruled by Uranus, Aquarius governs innovation, unconventional approaches, and collective consciousness. Cannabis has been central to countercultural and consciousness movements.
The dreamy, dissolving, and spiritually opening qualities of cannabis resonate with Pisces' transcendent and boundary-dissolving nature.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Cannabinoids modulate pain signaling through CB1 and CB2 receptors. Substantial clinical evidence supports efficacy for neuropathic and chronic pain conditions.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: CBD:THC tincture, start low (2.5-5mg THC) and titrate slowly. CBD-dominant ratios (20:1 or higher) for daytime use; balanced ratios for severe pain.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: CBD demonstrates significant anxiolytic effects in clinical trials. THC can be anxiolytic at low doses but may worsen anxiety at higher doses. CBD-dominant preparations are preferred.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: CBD tincture: 15-50mg CBD, 1-3 times daily. Avoid high-THC preparations for anxiety-prone individuals.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: THC is a potent anti-emetic, particularly effective for chemotherapy-induced nausea. Synthetic THC (dronabinol) is FDA-approved for this indication.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Small inhalation via vaporizer for rapid onset. Oral: 2.5-5mg THC as needed, up to 3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Indica-leaning cultivars and CBN-rich preparations promote sleep onset and duration. Most effective for pain-related or anxiety-related insomnia.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 5-15mg THC (indica-dominant tincture) or 5mg CBN, taken 1 hour before bed. Tolerance can develop with nightly use.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Cannabinoids reduce spasticity, neuropathic pain, and neuroinflammation. Sativex (THC:CBD) is approved for MS spasticity in many countries.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Dose varies; start low with 2.5-5 mg THC:CBD, titrate gradually under guidance.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Cannabinoids modulate pain signaling and improve sleep quality, with growing clinical evidence for fibromyalgia symptom relief.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Dose varies; start low with 2.5-5 mg, titrate under guidance.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: CBD (cannabidiol) has FDA-approved clinical evidence for reducing seizure frequency in treatment-resistant epilepsy.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: CBD: dosing varies, typically 5-20 mg/kg/day. Must work with prescribing physician.

contraindication_associations.reason: THC crosses the placental barrier and disrupts the endocannabinoid system critical for fetal neurodevelopment, placental function, and implantation; associated with low birth weight and preterm delivery
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid all forms including CBD-dominant products during pregnancy; endocannabinoid signaling is essential for normal embryonic development

contraindication_associations.reason: THC is a CB1 receptor partial agonist that disrupts dopaminergic signaling in the mesolimbic pathway; strong association with triggering psychotic episodes and worsening schizophrenia symptoms
contraindication_associations.notes: Contraindicated in schizophrenia and psychotic disorders; high-THC strains pose greatest risk, especially in adolescents with genetic predisposition

contraindication_associations.reason: Acute cannabis use increases heart rate by 20-100%, causes orthostatic hypotension, and raises myocardial oxygen demand; associated with increased risk of myocardial infarction in the hour following use
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid with unstable angina, recent MI, arrhythmias, or heart failure; smoking route adds additional cardiovascular burden

contraindication_associations.reason: THC is highly lipophilic and concentrates in breast milk at approximately 8:1 ratio to maternal plasma; detectable in infant plasma and stool for weeks
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid entirely while breastfeeding; THC accumulates in infant brain tissue and may impair neurodevelopment

contraindication_associations.reason: THC and CBD undergo extensive first-pass hepatic metabolism via CYP2C9 and CYP3A4; CB1 receptor activation promotes hepatic steatosis and fibrogenesis
contraindication_associations.notes: Use caution with hepatic impairment; CBD in particular can elevate liver enzymes and may interact with hepatically metabolized medications

contraindication_associations.reason: THC may lower seizure threshold via CB1-mediated glutamate modulation, despite CBD having anticonvulsant properties; whole-plant preparations have unpredictable THC:CBD ratios
contraindication_associations.notes: Isolated CBD has FDA approval for certain epilepsies, but THC-containing products require careful medical supervision in seizure disorders

description: The gentle healer of the herb garden, chamomile is perhaps the most universally beloved medicinal plant. Its sunny, apple-scented flowers calm the nervous system, soothe inflamed digestive tissue, and ease the transition into restful sleep, making it safe and effective for all ages from infants to elders.
habitat: Fields, roadsides, cultivated gardens, and disturbed ground throughout temperate regions. Prefers well-drained soil with full sun.
native_region: Southern and Eastern Europe, Western Asia
energetic_quality: Cooling, drying, calming. Gently disperses heat and tension, bringing peaceful equilibrium to agitated states of body and mind.
doctrine_of_signatures: The golden-rayed flowers resemble miniature suns, connecting chamomile to solar vitality and warmth. The genus name Matricaria derives from 'matrix' (womb), revealing its ancient use as a women's herb for soothing menstrual and maternal complaints.
safety_notes: Generally recognized as safe. Rare allergic reactions possible in individuals sensitive to Asteraceae family. May potentiate anticoagulant medications. Safe for children and during pregnancy in moderate amounts as tea.

parts.typical_compounds: Chamazulene, alpha-bisabolol, apigenin, luteolin, quercetin, volatile oils
parts.therapeutic_properties: Anxiolytic, sedative, anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, carminative, vulnerary, bitter tonic
parts.notes: The flower heads are the sole medicinal part. Harvested at full bloom when essential oil content peaks. Used as tea, tincture, essential oil, or topical preparation. The blue essential oil (from chamazulene) indicates potent anti-inflammatory activity.

planet_associations.notes: The golden, radiant flower heads reflect solar energy. Chamomile warms and vitalizes the digestive system while bringing the Sun's gentle, nurturing aspect to the nervous system.
The calming, nurturing, and sleep-promoting qualities connect chamomile to the Moon's domain. Its traditional use as a children's and women's herb further strengthens this lunar association.

zodiac_associations.notes: Ruled by the Sun, Leo governs the heart and vital warmth. Chamomile's sunny flower and heart-soothing nature align with Leo's generous, warming energy.
Chamomile's nurturing, maternal quality and digestive soothing action connect it to Cancer, the sign of the stomach and womb.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Apigenin binds benzodiazepine receptors, producing clinically demonstrated anxiolytic effects. A 2009 clinical trial showed chamomile extract significantly reduced generalized anxiety disorder symptoms.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-3 teaspoons dried flowers per cup, steeped 5-7 minutes covered. Drink 2-4 cups daily. Extract: 220-1100mg daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Apigenin's mild sedative action promotes sleep onset. Clinical studies show improved sleep quality scores in postnatal women and elderly populations.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Strong infusion (3 teaspoons per cup, steeped 10 minutes covered) 30-45 minutes before bed.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Antispasmodic and carminative actions relieve bloating, gas, and cramping. Bisabolol soothes inflamed gastric and intestinal mucosa.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried flower per cup, steeped 5-10 minutes. Drink after meals, 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Regular chamomile use reduces cortisol and promotes parasympathetic nervous system activation. The ritual of tea preparation itself contributes to stress reduction.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 30-60 drops tincture in warm water, 2-3 times daily. Or 2-4 cups of tea throughout the day.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties soothe intestinal inflammation and reduce cramping associated with celiac flares.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-3 teaspoons dried flowers per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Drink 3-4 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties soothe intestinal inflammation and reduce cramping.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-3 teaspoons dried flowers per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Drink 3-4 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties soothe colonic inflammation and reduce bloody diarrhea frequency.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-3 teaspoons dried flowers per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Drink 3-4 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Antispasmodic and carminative properties reduce intestinal cramping, bloating, and gas characteristic of IBS.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-3 teaspoons dried flowers per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Drink 3-4 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Calming nervine that reduces irritability and emotional reactivity through gentle sedative and anti-inflammatory effects.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-3 teaspoons dried flowers per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Drink 2-4 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Gentle calming herb suitable for all ages that promotes restful sleep and reduces nighttime anxiety.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-3 teaspoons dried flowers per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Drink before bed.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Gentle nervine and digestive aid that soothes pregnancy-related anxiety, insomnia, and digestive discomfort.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried flowers per cup, steeped 10 minutes. Drink 1-2 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Gentle calming herb safe during breastfeeding that reduces stress and may help with infant colic through breast milk.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried flowers per cup, steeped 10 minutes. Drink 1-3 cups daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Contains apigenin and bisabolol which have mild uterine-stimulating and emmenagogue properties; high doses may increase risk of miscarriage
contraindication_associations.notes: Occasional mild tea is generally considered safe; avoid concentrated extracts, tinctures, and essential oil internally during pregnancy

contraindication_associations.reason: Member of Asteraceae family with significant cross-reactivity to ragweed, chrysanthemum, and other Compositae plants; contains allergenic sesquiterpene lactones
contraindication_associations.notes: Individuals with ragweed allergy have elevated risk of anaphylaxis; perform patch test before first use and avoid if any Asteraceae allergy is known

contraindication_associations.reason: Contains coumarin constituents that may potentiate anticoagulant effects; apigenin inhibits platelet aggregation through thromboxane A2 pathway suppression
contraindication_associations.notes: Use caution with warfarin and other anticoagulants; unlikely to cause issues at normal tea doses but concentrated extracts may be significant

contraindication_associations.reason: Apigenin exhibits weak estrogenic activity through estrogen receptor beta binding, potentially stimulating estrogen-dependent tissue growth
contraindication_associations.notes: Normal tea consumption unlikely to be problematic; use caution with concentrated supplements in estrogen-receptor-positive breast cancer

description: An aromatic evergreen shrub revered since antiquity as the herb of remembrance and mental clarity. Rosemary stimulates cerebral circulation, sharpens memory, and lifts the spirits with its invigorating camphoraceous fragrance, while its potent antioxidant compounds protect the brain and body from oxidative damage.
habitat: Rocky, calcareous hillsides and coastal cliffs in Mediterranean climates. Thrives in full sun with well-drained, alkaline soil and tolerates drought.
energetic_quality: Warming, drying, stimulating. Moves stagnant energy upward to the head, clearing fog and igniting mental fire.
doctrine_of_signatures: Its evergreen nature reflects the Sun's constancy and unwavering vitality. The needle-like leaves suggest focus and pointed clarity of thought, while the plant's ability to thrive on rocky, sun-baked hillsides demonstrates resilience and solar strength.
safety_notes: Generally safe in culinary and moderate medicinal amounts. Essential oil should not be taken internally. Avoid therapeutic doses during pregnancy (emmenagogue). High doses of camphor can cause seizures. Caution with epilepsy.

parts.typical_compounds: Rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid, 1,8-cineole, camphor, ursolic acid, carnosol
parts.therapeutic_properties: Cerebral stimulant, circulatory tonic, antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, carminative, hepatoprotective, memory-enhancing
parts.notes: The primary medicinal part. Used fresh or dried as tea, tincture, culinary herb, or essential oil (topical/aromatic). The fresh sprig retains more volatile oils than the dried leaf.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Uplifting, clarifying, mildly stimulating
parts.notes: The small blue-purple flowers are edible and can be used in flower essences for mental clarity and remembrance. They contain a lighter, more refined version of the leaf's aromatic compounds.

planet_associations.notes: The Sun governs vitality, the heart, and conscious awareness. Rosemary is a quintessential solar herb, warming the blood, strengthening the heart, and illuminating the mind with clarity and purpose.

zodiac_associations.notes: Leo's solar fire and association with the heart, spine, and vital warmth align with rosemary's warming, invigorating, and heart-strengthening properties.
Aries rules the head and the blood. Rosemary drives circulation upward to the brain, stimulating the Aries-governed regions with clarity and alertness.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Stimulates cerebral and peripheral circulation, combating mental and physical fatigue. Aromatic inhalation alone improves alertness and cognitive performance.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried rosemary per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Drink 1-3 cups daily, preferably in the morning and afternoon.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Enhances memory and cognitive performance. A 2012 study demonstrated that rosemary aroma alone significantly improved memory task speed and accuracy.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 30-60 drops tincture, 2-3 times daily. Or inhale fresh rosemary essential oil from the sprig for immediate cognitive boost.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Improves cerebral circulation and relieves tension headaches. The anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties address the vascular component of headaches.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Strong infusion (2 teaspoons per cup), sipped at onset of headache. Topical: diluted rosemary essential oil massaged into temples.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Stimulates bile production and digestive secretions. Carminative action relieves gas and bloating. Traditionally used as a culinary herb to aid digestion of rich foods.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1 teaspoon dried rosemary per cup, steeped 10 minutes. Drink after meals.

contraindication_associations.reason: Contains camphor and rosmarinic acid with emmenagogue and abortifacient properties; stimulates uterine contractions at medicinal doses
contraindication_associations.notes: Culinary amounts are safe; avoid therapeutic doses, essential oil internally, and concentrated extracts during pregnancy

contraindication_associations.reason: Camphor and eucalyptol components are CNS stimulants that can lower the seizure threshold, particularly in essential oil form or at high doses
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid rosemary essential oil and high-dose extracts entirely; culinary use in food preparation is generally safe

contraindication_associations.reason: Rosemary extract can raise blood pressure through sympathomimetic activity and inhibition of ACE; rosmarinic acid affects vascular tone
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor blood pressure if using therapeutic doses; may counteract antihypertensive medications

contraindication_associations.reason: High doses may inhibit platelet aggregation and potentiate anticoagulant medications through interaction with CYP enzymes involved in warfarin metabolism
contraindication_associations.notes: Culinary use is safe; caution with concentrated extracts if taking anticoagulant or antiplatelet drugs

description: The great solar antidepressant of Western herbalism, St. John's Wort blooms at midsummer when the Sun reaches its zenith. Its bright yellow flowers, which bleed a deep red oil when crushed, have been clinically validated for mild to moderate depression, rivaling the efficacy of standard pharmaceutical antidepressants with fewer side effects.
habitat: Meadows, roadsides, woodland edges, and dry grasslands. Prefers well-drained soil with full sun to partial shade.
energetic_quality: Warming, drying, uplifting. Brings solar light into dark, contracted emotional states and repairs damaged nerve tissue.
doctrine_of_signatures: The perforated leaves (visible when held to light) reveal the plant's ability to let light pass through darkness, directly reflecting its antidepressant action. The blood-red oil released from crushed flowers suggests its affinity for wound healing and nerve repair.
safety_notes: Causes photosensitivity; avoid excessive sun exposure during use. Significant drug interactions with SSRIs (serotonin syndrome risk), oral contraceptives, anticoagulants, cyclosporine, and many other medications due to CYP450 enzyme induction. Consult a practitioner before combining with any pharmaceutical.

parts.typical_compounds: Hypericin, hyperforin, pseudohypericin, flavonoids (quercetin, rutin), volatile oils
parts.therapeutic_properties: Antidepressant, anxiolytic, nervine, anti-inflammatory, vulnerary, antiviral
parts.notes: The flowering tops are harvested at peak bloom (around the summer solstice) when hypericin content is highest. The flowers and buds are used for tincture, infused oil, and tea. The deep red oil produced by macerating flowers in olive oil is used topically for nerve pain and wound healing.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Mild nervine, astringent, anti-inflammatory
parts.notes: The leaves contain hyperforin and flavonoids but lower hypericin levels. Often harvested together with the flowering tops. The perforated appearance is the key identification feature.

planet_associations.notes: St. John's Wort is the solar herb par excellence, blooming at the summer solstice and bringing the light of the Sun into states of darkness and depression. Its golden flowers and red-staining oil embody solar vitality and healing warmth.

zodiac_associations.notes: Leo's solar rulership, association with the heart and joy, and generous vital warmth align perfectly with St. John's Wort's antidepressant, heart-opening action.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Extensively studied. Multiple meta-analyses confirm efficacy equivalent to standard SSRIs for mild to moderate depression, with fewer side effects. Inhibits reuptake of serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300mg standardized extract (0.3% hypericin) three times daily. Or 30-60 drops tincture, 3 times daily. Full effects require 4-6 weeks of consistent use.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Clinical trials demonstrate anxiolytic effects alongside antidepressant action. Particularly effective for the anxiety that accompanies depression.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300mg standardized extract, 3 times daily. Or 30-60 drops tincture, 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Infused oil applied topically accelerates wound closure, reduces inflammation, and supports nerve repair. Particularly effective for burns, bruises, and nerve-rich areas.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Apply St. John's Wort infused oil directly to clean wounds, burns, or areas of nerve pain 2-3 times daily. Avoid sun exposure on treated areas.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Addresses the depression and nerve pain components of fibromyalgia through serotonin modulation and anti-inflammatory effects.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-4 ml tincture or 300 mg extract standardized to 0.3% hypericin, 3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Well-established clinical evidence for mild to moderate depression, with efficacy comparable to SSRIs in trials.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300 mg extract standardized to 0.3% hypericin, 3 times daily. Monitor drug interactions carefully.

contraindication_associations.reason: Hyperforin and hypericin cross the placental barrier; animal studies show reduced fetal weight and delayed ossification; uterotonic effects observed in vitro
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during pregnancy; also a potent CYP3A4 inducer that may reduce efficacy of prenatal medications

contraindication_associations.reason: Inhibits reuptake of serotonin, norepinephrine, and dopamine; combining with SSRIs, SNRIs, or MAOIs can precipitate life-threatening serotonin syndrome
contraindication_associations.notes: Absolute contraindication with serotonergic medications; allow 2-week washout before starting antidepressants; may trigger mania in bipolar disorder

contraindication_associations.reason: Potent inducer of CYP3A4, CYP2C9, CYP1A2, and P-glycoprotein, dramatically altering metabolism of hepatically cleared drugs; may reduce drug levels by 50% or more
contraindication_associations.notes: Contraindicated with cyclosporine, HIV protease inhibitors, oral contraceptives, warfarin, and many other medications metabolized by the liver

contraindication_associations.reason: CYP2C9 induction accelerates warfarin metabolism, reducing INR and anticoagulant efficacy; abrupt discontinuation can cause dangerous INR rebound
contraindication_associations.notes: Do not combine with warfarin or other coumarin anticoagulants; if stopping St. John's Wort, monitor INR closely for rebound effect

contraindication_associations.reason: Induces CYP3A4 which accelerates metabolism of tamoxifen, aromatase inhibitors, and hormonal therapies, potentially rendering cancer treatment ineffective
contraindication_associations.notes: Absolutely contraindicated during hormonal cancer treatment; drug interaction risk is clinically severe and well-documented

contraindication_associations.reason: CYP3A4 induction reduces plasma levels of digoxin, calcium channel blockers, statins, and antiarrhythmic drugs, potentially causing treatment failure
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid with all cardiac medications; enzyme induction effects persist for 1-2 weeks after discontinuation

description: The quintessential calming herb, lavender's aromatic flowers have been used for millennia to soothe anxiety, promote restful sleep, and ease tension headaches. Modern research has validated its anxiolytic effects, with lavender essential oil demonstrating clinical efficacy comparable to benzodiazepines for generalized anxiety.
habitat: Rocky, calcareous slopes and dry meadows in Mediterranean climates. Thrives in full sun with poor, well-drained, alkaline soil.
native_region: Mediterranean region, particularly Provence (France), Spain, and Italy
energetic_quality: Cooling, drying, balancing. Disperses excess heat and nervous tension, bringing calm equilibrium to overwrought states.
doctrine_of_signatures: The blue-violet color of the flowers connects lavender to the higher centers of perception and spiritual calm. The intensely aromatic nature reveals its affinity for the nervous system and the breath, entering through the olfactory pathway to directly calm the mind.
safety_notes: Generally recognized as safe. Lavender essential oil is one of few safe for direct skin application in small amounts. May cause drowsiness; use caution when driving. Rare estrogenic effects reported with prepubescent topical use. Internal use of essential oil only with qualified guidance.

parts.typical_compounds: Linalool, linalyl acetate, 1,8-cineole, camphor, lavandulol, tannins, coumarins
parts.therapeutic_properties: Anxiolytic, sedative, analgesic, antispasmodic, antidepressant, antimicrobial, carminative, vulnerary
parts.notes: The flower spikes are the primary medicinal part, harvested just before full bloom when essential oil content peaks. Used as tea, tincture, essential oil, sachet, and in baths. The essential oil is the most clinically studied preparation.

planet_associations.notes: Mercury rules the nervous system, communication, and the respiratory pathways through which lavender's aromatic compounds enter the body. Lavender calms Mercurial over-stimulation and brings clarity to scattered nervous energy.

zodiac_associations.notes: Ruled by Mercury, Gemini governs the lungs, nervous system, and mental processes. Lavender's calming effect on nervous anxiety and respiratory support align with Gemini's domain.
Also ruled by Mercury, Virgo's analytical, sometimes anxious energy is soothed by lavender. Its purifying and orderly nature resonates with Virgo's desire for cleanliness and refinement.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Silexan (lavender oil capsule) demonstrated efficacy equal to lorazepam for generalized anxiety in a randomized controlled trial. Linalool modulates GABA receptors without benzodiazepine side effects.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 80mg lavender oil capsule (Silexan) daily. Tincture: 30-60 drops, 2-3 times daily. Aromatic: 3-5 drops essential oil in a diffuser.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Lavender aroma significantly improves sleep quality scores in clinical trials. The sedative effect of linalool is well-documented through inhalation studies.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2 teaspoons dried lavender flowers per cup, steeped 5-7 minutes. Drink before bed. Or place sachets near pillow. Aromatic: 3-5 drops on pillowcase.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: A clinical trial demonstrated that inhalation of lavender essential oil significantly reduced migraine severity within 15 minutes of onset.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Apply 2-3 drops essential oil to temples and inhale deeply. Or take 30 drops tincture at onset of headache.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: The aromatic and energetic qualities of lavender reduce cortisol and promote parasympathetic activation. Flower essence works on the subtle body to release tension patterns.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 4 drops flower essence under the tongue, 4 times daily. Or add 5-10 drops essential oil to a warm bath for full-body stress relief.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Anxiolytic and calming effects reduce emotional volatility and promote a sense of peace and equanimity.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried flowers per cup, steeped 10 minutes. Drink 2-3 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Calming aromatherapy and internal use promotes peaceful sleep and reduces the nervous system hyperarousal behind night terrors.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried flowers per cup, steeped 10 minutes. Also effective as pillow sachet or essential oil.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Clinical trials show Silexan (lavender oil capsules) reduces anxiety comparable to lorazepam without sedation or dependence.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 80-160 mg lavender oil capsules daily, or 1-2 cups lavender tea daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Linalool and linalyl acetate have mild smooth muscle relaxant and emmenagogue properties; high-dose internal use may stimulate uterine activity
contraindication_associations.notes: Mild aromatherapy and culinary use generally considered safe; avoid internal use of essential oil and concentrated extracts during pregnancy

contraindication_associations.reason: Lavender oil demonstrates estrogenic activity and anti-androgenic effects in vitro; associated with prepubertal gynecomastia in case reports of topical use
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid prolonged topical application of undiluted essential oil in estrogen-sensitive conditions; occasional aromatherapy use is low risk

contraindication_associations.reason: Linalool may potentiate anticoagulant effects; lavender oil has demonstrated antiplatelet activity in laboratory studies
contraindication_associations.notes: Unlikely to be clinically significant at normal aromatherapy doses; exercise caution with internal use alongside anticoagulants

contraindication_associations.reason: Volatile terpenes including linalool are lipophilic and may pass into breast milk; estrogenic properties raise theoretical concerns for infant hormonal development
contraindication_associations.notes: Topical and aromatherapy use likely safe in moderation; avoid ingesting lavender essential oil or concentrated supplements while nursing

description: A fiercely nutritive wild green that stings upon contact yet yields one of the most deeply nourishing medicines in the herbal pharmacopoeia. Nettle is a mineral-rich tonic that rebuilds depleted blood, quells inflammation, supports immune resilience, and clears waste from the tissues with the martial vigor that its sting implies.
habitat: Rich, moist soils along riverbanks, forest edges, farm margins, and disturbed nitrogen-rich ground. Often indicates fertile soil.
native_region: Europe, Asia, Northern Africa, North America
energetic_quality: Drying, astringent, nourishing. Mars-like in its protective sting but deeply building and mineralizing in its inner nature.
doctrine_of_signatures: The stinging hairs that defend the plant reflect Mars' protective and activating energy. The sting itself contains formic acid, histamine, and serotonin, revealing the plant's capacity to modulate the very inflammatory pathways it initially provokes.
safety_notes: Fresh plant stings on contact; wear gloves when harvesting. Cooking or drying neutralizes the sting. May affect blood sugar and blood pressure. Caution with kidney disease (diuretic). May interact with blood thinners, diabetes medications, and lithium.

parts.typical_compounds: Quercetin, chlorophyll, iron, calcium, magnesium, silica, vitamin K, formic acid, serotonin, histamine
parts.therapeutic_properties: Nutritive tonic, antihistamine, anti-inflammatory, diuretic, iron-rich blood builder, astringent, galactagogue
parts.notes: Harvest young spring leaves before flowering for maximum nutritive value. Cooking or drying neutralizes the sting. Used as tea, tincture, food (like spinach), or freeze-dried capsules for allergies.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Prostate-supporting (BPH), anti-inflammatory, diuretic, 5-alpha-reductase inhibitor
parts.notes: The root has distinct therapeutic applications from the leaf, primarily used for benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH). Harvested in autumn. Used as decoction or tincture.

parts.typical_compounds: Essential fatty acids, vitamin E, carotenoids
parts.therapeutic_properties: Kidney tonic, adrenal restorative, adaptogenic
parts.notes: Nettle seeds are an underappreciated adrenal and kidney tonic. They provide gentle, sustained energy support without stimulation. Eaten raw, sprinkled on food, or tinctured.

planet_associations.notes: Mars rules the blood, muscles, adrenals, and the inflammatory response. Nettle's stinging defense, iron-rich blood-building capacity, and anti-inflammatory action are quintessentially martial.

zodiac_associations.notes: Aries rules the head and the blood. Nettle's iron-rich, blood-building nature and its fierce, protective sting reflect the Ram's assertive, pioneering energy.
Scorpio's co-rulership by Mars and its association with elimination and regeneration align with nettle's diuretic and deeply restorative properties.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Quercetin and other flavonoids inhibit inflammatory pathways including NF-kB and COX-2. Long infusions extract the full mineral and anti-inflammatory profile.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Nourishing infusion: 1 ounce dried nettle leaf in 1 quart boiling water, steeped 4-8 hours (overnight). Strain and drink 1-3 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Dense mineral and vitamin content rebuilds the nutritive foundation of immune function. Supports healthy white blood cell production through iron and chlorophyll content.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Nourishing infusion daily for 4-12 weeks to rebuild depleted mineral stores and immune reserves.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Highly bioavailable iron, along with chlorophyll, calcium, and magnesium, addresses nutritive depletion that underlies many cases of chronic fatigue.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1 quart nourishing nettle infusion daily. Effects are cumulative over weeks of consistent use.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Iron and vitamin K content support healthy menstrual flow. Astringent action helps reduce excessive bleeding while minerals replenish what is lost.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-3 cups nettle infusion daily throughout the menstrual cycle. Especially important during and after menstruation to replenish iron stores.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Anti-inflammatory lectins and minerals support joint health and reduce inflammatory markers. Topical sting therapy also used traditionally.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-3 teaspoons dried leaf per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Drink 3-4 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Gentle diuretic and kidney tonic that supports renal function without overstressing damaged kidneys.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-3 teaspoons dried leaf per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Drink 2-3 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Rich in vitamin K and iron, supports healthy blood clotting and rebuilds blood after hemorrhagic episodes.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-3 teaspoons dried leaf per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Drink 3-4 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Nutrient-dense pregnancy tonic rich in iron, calcium, and vitamin K, supporting blood building and fetal development.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried leaf per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Drink 1-2 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Nutrient-dense herb rich in iron and minerals that supports milk production and maternal nutrition during lactation.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-3 teaspoons dried leaf per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Drink 2-3 cups daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Nettle leaf is a uterine stimulant that may induce contractions; root extracts affect hormonal pathways including testosterone and estrogen binding
contraindication_associations.notes: Some midwives use nettle leaf tea in late pregnancy for its nutritive value, but concentrated extracts and root preparations should be avoided; consult practitioner

contraindication_associations.reason: Strong diuretic effect increases renal excretion of water and electrolytes; high oxalate content may contribute to kidney stone formation in susceptible individuals
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid in acute kidney inflammation; stay well hydrated; use caution with existing kidney stones or renal insufficiency

contraindication_associations.reason: Nettle lectins and polysaccharides lower blood glucose through enhanced pancreatic insulin secretion and improved peripheral glucose uptake
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor blood glucose when combining with diabetes medications; dose adjustments may be needed to prevent hypoglycemia

contraindication_associations.reason: High vitamin K content may interfere with warfarin anticoagulation by promoting hepatic synthesis of clotting factors II, VII, IX, and X
contraindication_associations.notes: Maintain consistent intake if on warfarin; sudden increases in nettle consumption can significantly alter INR values

contraindication_associations.reason: Diuretic and hypotensive properties may potentiate antihypertensive medications, causing excessive blood pressure reduction
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor blood pressure if combining with antihypertensive drugs; beneficial effect in mild hypertension but requires dose coordination

description: The universal flower of love and beauty, rose has been used medicinally for at least 5,000 years across every major healing tradition. Beyond its celebrated fragrance, rose is a profound medicine for the heart, both physical and emotional, gently opening closed hearts, cooling inflammation, and nourishing the skin with its astringent, antioxidant-rich chemistry.
habitat: Temperate gardens, hedgerows, and hillsides worldwide. Wild species prefer well-drained soil with full to partial sun. Highly cultivated with thousands of varieties.
energetic_quality: Cooling, moistening, astringent. Opens the heart while gently containing and cooling excessive emotional heat. Profoundly harmonizing.
doctrine_of_signatures: The beauty and thorns of the rose reflect Venus's dual nature: the exquisite vulnerability of the open heart and the necessary boundaries that protect it. The layered petals unfurl from a tight bud, mirroring the gradual opening of a guarded heart.
safety_notes: Generally recognized as safe for all ages. Ensure roses used medicinally are organically grown and free of pesticides. Rose essential oil is extremely concentrated; use sparingly. Rose hip seeds contain fine hairs that can irritate the throat if not properly strained.

parts.typical_compounds: Quercetin, kaempferol, eugenol, geraniol, citronellol, gallic acid, tannins
parts.therapeutic_properties: Heart tonic (emotional), astringent, anti-inflammatory, cooling, antidepressant, nervine, skin-nourishing, aphrodisiac
parts.notes: Rose petals are used fresh or dried as tea, tincture, hydrosol (rose water), essential oil, and flower essence. The most fragrant, deeply colored varieties are preferred medicinally. Damask rose (Rosa damascena) is considered the finest for distillation.

parts.typical_compounds: Vitamin C (high), carotenoids, flavonoids, essential fatty acids, lycopene
parts.therapeutic_properties: Immune-supportive, antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, nutritive, joint-supporting
parts.notes: Rose hips (the fruit) are among the richest plant sources of vitamin C. Harvested after the first frost when they soften and sweeten. Used as tea, syrup, jam, or powder. Must be deseeded and strained to remove irritating hairs.

planet_associations.notes: Venus rules beauty, love, the heart, and sensual pleasure. Rose is the supreme Venusian plant, embodying beauty, opening the heart, nourishing the skin, and harmonizing relationships.

zodiac_associations.notes: Ruled by Venus, Taurus governs the throat, the senses, and earthly pleasure. Rose's exquisite fragrance and beautifying, throat-soothing properties resonate with Taurus's sensual nature.
Also ruled by Venus, Libra seeks harmony, beauty, and balance. Rose's cooling, harmonizing action and its association with love and relationship align with Libra's quest for equilibrium.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Rose flower essence is the premier remedy for heartbreak, loss, and closed-heartedness. It gently opens the heart to process grief while maintaining the protective boundary of self-compassion.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 4 drops under the tongue, 4 times daily. Or add rose hydrosol to drinking water throughout the day.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Rose tea reduces cortisol and promotes parasympathetic activation. Rose essential oil inhalation has demonstrated anxiolytic effects in clinical settings.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 tablespoons dried rose petals per cup, steeped 5-7 minutes. Drink 2-3 cups daily. Add rose water to bath for full-body stress relief.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Astringent tannins tighten pores and reduce redness. Anti-inflammatory flavonoids soothe irritated skin. Rose water has been used for centuries as a facial tonic.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Apply rose hydrosol directly to skin as toner. Internal: 30-40 drops rose petal tincture, 2 times daily. Rose hip oil applied topically for scarring.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Rose hips provide exceptionally high vitamin C content, supporting immune function and collagen production. Antioxidant carotenoids further bolster immune resilience.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons crushed dried rose hips per cup, simmered gently 10-15 minutes. Strain carefully. Drink 2-3 cups daily during cold season.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Opens the heart center and softens emotional reactivity, transforming anger into compassion through subtle vibrational medicine.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried rose petals per cup, steeped 10 minutes. Drink mindfully.

contraindication_associations.reason: Rose essential oil contains geraniol and citronellol which have mild uterotonic properties; rosehip preparations may have vitamin A levels that are teratogenic at high doses
contraindication_associations.notes: Mild rose petal tea is generally considered safe; avoid internal use of essential oil and high-dose rosehip supplements during pregnancy

contraindication_associations.reason: Rose pollen and volatile compounds can trigger allergic rhinitis and contact dermatitis in sensitive individuals; cross-reactivity with other Rosaceae family plants
contraindication_associations.notes: Perform patch test before topical use of rose preparations; discontinue if irritation or allergic symptoms develop

contraindication_associations.reason: Rosehip contains high vitamin C levels that may enhance iron absorption and interact with anticoagulants; some rose species contain mild antiplatelet compounds
contraindication_associations.notes: Normal tea consumption is generally safe; monitor if consuming high-dose rosehip supplements alongside anticoagulant therapy

description: The most ubiquitous and underestimated medicinal plant on earth, dandelion is a complete pharmacy growing freely in every lawn and meadow. Its root cleanses and regenerates the liver, its leaf is a potassium-sparing diuretic, and its flower provides antioxidant nourishment, making every part of this generous plant a powerful ally for health.
habitat: Lawns, meadows, roadsides, fields, waste ground, and cracks in pavement worldwide. Thrives in virtually any soil and conditions, reflecting its Jupiterian abundance.
energetic_quality: Cooling, drying, cleansing. Moves energy downward through the liver and kidneys, clearing stagnation and promoting the free flow of bile and urine.
doctrine_of_signatures: Dandelion proliferates with irrepressible abundance, reflecting Jupiter's expansive, generous nature. The bright yellow flower resembling a miniature sun reveals its affinity for the liver (the body's internal sun), while the milky latex in the stem suggests its role in promoting the flow of bodily fluids.
safety_notes: Generally recognized as safe. Avoid if allergic to Asteraceae family. May interact with diuretic medications, lithium, and antibiotics (quinolones). Those with bile duct obstruction or gallstones should consult a practitioner before using the root. Harvest only from unsprayed areas.

parts.typical_compounds: Inulin, taraxacin (bitter glycoside), taraxasterol, beta-sitosterol, pectin, minerals
parts.therapeutic_properties: Hepatic tonic, cholagogue, digestive bitter, prebiotic, gentle laxative, blood sugar modulator, detoxificant
parts.notes: Harvested in autumn when inulin content is highest. Used as decoction, tincture, roasted root coffee substitute, or dried in capsules. The primary liver and digestive herb.

parts.typical_compounds: Potassium, iron, calcium, vitamins A, C, K, luteolin, chicoric acid
parts.therapeutic_properties: Potassium-sparing diuretic, nutritive, anti-edemic, kidney supportive, blood pressure supportive
parts.notes: Unlike pharmaceutical diuretics, dandelion leaf replaces the potassium it helps excrete. Young leaves are eaten as salad greens. Used as tea or tincture for urinary support.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, heart-supportive, skin-nourishing
parts.notes: Flowers are used to make dandelion wine, infused oil (for sore muscles), and flower essence. Rich in antioxidant flavonoids. Edible and nutritive.

planet_associations.notes: Jupiter rules the liver, expansion, and abundance. Dandelion is the quintessential Jupiterian herb, growing with unstoppable proliferation while detoxifying and regenerating the liver, Jupiter's primary organ.

zodiac_associations.notes: Ruled by Jupiter, Sagittarius governs the liver and thighs. Dandelion's liver-cleansing action and its proliferative, optimistic nature align with the Archer's expansive spirit.
Co-ruled by Jupiter, Pisces governs the lymphatic system and bodily fluids. Dandelion's diuretic and lymph-supporting properties serve the Piscean domain.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Taraxacin and other bitter compounds stimulate bile production and flow, decongest the liver, and promote gentle hepatic detoxification. Traditional cornerstone of spring liver cleansing protocols.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 tablespoons dried root simmered in 2 cups water for 20-30 minutes. Drink 1-3 cups daily. Best taken before meals to stimulate digestive secretions.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Bitter principles stimulate the entire digestive cascade from salivation through bile release to peristalsis. Inulin content acts as a prebiotic, feeding beneficial gut flora.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 30-60 drops dandelion root tincture in a small amount of water, 15-20 minutes before meals, 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Exceptionally nutrient-dense leaves provide vitamins A, C, and K along with iron and calcium. Prebiotic inulin in the root supports gut-mediated immunity.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Young leaves eaten fresh in salads, 1-2 handfuls daily in spring. Year-round: 2-3 cups dandelion leaf tea daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: By decongestanting the liver and improving nutrient assimilation, dandelion root addresses a common root cause of chronic fatigue. The roasted root also serves as a nourishing caffeine-free coffee alternative.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Roasted dandelion root decoction: 1-2 tablespoons per cup, simmered 10-15 minutes. Drink 1-2 cups daily as a coffee substitute.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Supports liver detoxification, increases bile production, and provides gentle hepatoprotective effects.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried root simmered 10 minutes, 2-3 cups daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Taraxasterol and other triterpenoids have mild uterine-stimulating properties; strong diuretic effect may alter electrolyte balance during pregnancy
contraindication_associations.notes: Occasional tea use is likely safe but avoid concentrated root extracts and tinctures; diuretic effect may exacerbate pregnancy-related dehydration

contraindication_associations.reason: Potent diuretic action increases renal workload and potassium excretion; high oxalate content may promote calcium oxalate stone formation
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid with acute renal inflammation or renal insufficiency; maintain adequate hydration and monitor electrolytes

contraindication_associations.reason: Contains coumarin derivatives that may potentiate anticoagulant effects; high vitamin K content in leaves may also interfere with warfarin dosing
contraindication_associations.notes: Maintain consistent intake if on warfarin to keep vitamin K levels stable; avoid combining concentrated extracts with anticoagulants

contraindication_associations.reason: Member of the Asteraceae family with sesquiterpene lactone content; cross-reactivity with ragweed, chamomile, and other Compositae plants is well-documented
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid if known Asteraceae allergy; may cause contact dermatitis with topical use of latex from fresh stems

contraindication_associations.reason: Bitter constituents (taraxacin, taraxacerin) stimulate gastric acid secretion through the bitter taste receptor-vagal nerve pathway, potentially aggravating peptic ulcers
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid concentrated root preparations with active ulcers; mild leaf tea may be better tolerated than root decoctions

description: Known for centuries as 'knitbone' for its remarkable ability to accelerate the healing of fractures, sprains, and wounds, comfrey contains allantoin, a cell-proliferant compound that stimulates tissue regeneration at an extraordinary rate. It is primarily used topically in modern herbalism due to concerns about internal use of its pyrrolizidine alkaloids.
habitat: Moist, rich soils along riverbanks, ditches, and damp meadows. Grows vigorously with large, rough-textured leaves and drooping bell-shaped flowers.
energetic_quality: Cooling, moistening, consolidating. Draws tissues together and promotes rapid structural repair. Deeply demulcent and tissue-knitting.
doctrine_of_signatures: Known as 'knitbone,' comfrey's ability to mend broken bones reflects Saturn's rulership of the skeletal structure. The rough, hairy leaves and thick, mucilaginous root suggest the plant's capacity to bind, consolidate, and rebuild damaged structural tissue.
safety_notes: Contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids (PAs) which are hepatotoxic with prolonged internal use. Modern practice restricts comfrey primarily to topical use. Do not apply to deep puncture wounds (can seal infection inside). Internal use only under expert guidance with PA-free cultivars. Avoid during pregnancy.

parts.typical_compounds: Allantoin, mucilage, tannins, rosmarinic acid, pyrrolizidine alkaloids, asparagine
parts.therapeutic_properties: Vulnerary, cell-proliferant, demulcent, anti-inflammatory, bone-knitting, astringent
parts.notes: The root contains the highest concentration of allantoin and mucilage but also the most pyrrolizidine alkaloids. Primarily used topically as poultice, salve, or infused oil. Harvested in autumn.

parts.typical_compounds: Allantoin (lower than root), mucilage, tannins, silica, vitamins A and C
parts.therapeutic_properties: Vulnerary, demulcent, anti-inflammatory, mild astringent, nutritive (topical)
parts.notes: Leaves contain lower PA levels than root and are sometimes used as poultice for bruises, sprains, and inflammation. Large leaves can be wrapped around injured limbs as a natural splint and healing compress.

planet_associations.notes: Saturn governs bones, joints, teeth, and the structural framework of the body. Comfrey's bone-knitting, tissue-consolidating properties are the direct expression of Saturn's rulership of form and structure.

zodiac_associations.notes: Ruled by Saturn, Capricorn governs bones, joints, and knees. Comfrey's primary use for skeletal and structural repair directly serves the Capricorn domain.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Allantoin stimulates cell proliferation and accelerates wound closure. Clinical studies on comfrey ointments demonstrate significantly faster healing of abrasions and surgical wounds.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Apply comfrey leaf poultice or comfrey-infused oil/salve to clean wounds 2-3 times daily. Do not apply to deep puncture wounds.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Topical comfrey root extract significantly reduces pain, inflammation, and improves mobility in osteoarthritis of the knee. Multiple clinical trials confirm efficacy.
