ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Apply comfrey root ointment or salve to affected joints 2-3 times daily. Clinical studies used preparations containing 35% comfrey root extract.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Rosmarinic acid and allantoin together reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair. Topical application targets localized inflammation without systemic PA exposure.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Apply comfrey poultice or salve to inflamed areas as needed. Combine with internal anti-inflammatory herbs for comprehensive support.

contraindication_associations.reason: Contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids (PAs) including symphytine and echimidine that are hepatotoxic and genotoxic; PAs cross the placental barrier and are teratogenic in animal studies
contraindication_associations.notes: Absolute contraindication; avoid all internal use during pregnancy; even topical use on broken skin allows PA absorption

contraindication_associations.reason: Pyrrolizidine alkaloids undergo hepatic bioactivation to highly reactive pyrrolic metabolites that cause sinusoidal obstruction syndrome (hepatic veno-occlusive disease)
contraindication_associations.notes: Absolute contraindication for internal use; comfrey-induced hepatotoxicity can be fatal; many countries have banned internal comfrey products

contraindication_associations.reason: Pyrrolizidine alkaloids and their toxic metabolites are secreted into breast milk, posing hepatotoxic and carcinogenic risk to the nursing infant
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid all internal use and limit topical application while breastfeeding; infant liver is especially vulnerable to PA toxicity

contraindication_associations.reason: Pyrrolizidine alkaloid metabolites are partly excreted renally and may cause nephrotoxicity; impaired renal clearance increases systemic PA exposure
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid internal use entirely with kidney disease; reduced renal clearance increases toxic metabolite accumulation

contraindication_associations.reason: Pyrrolizidine alkaloids are classified as genotoxic carcinogens; their reactive pyrrolic metabolites form DNA adducts and cross-links that promote mutagenesis
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid internal use entirely; PA-induced DNA damage may promote tumor initiation and progression

description: A strikingly beautiful climbing vine whose intricate, otherworldly flowers produce a powerful calming medicine for the overactive mind. Passionflower quiets circular, anxious thinking and nervous tension without heavy sedation, making it one of the most valued herbs for anxiety and insomnia in both European and American herbal traditions.
habitat: Open woods, thickets, roadsides, and fence rows in warm temperate to subtropical regions. Climbs with tendrils and prefers well-drained soil with full sun.
native_region: Southeastern United States, Central and South America
energetic_quality: Cooling, moistening, calming. Quiets the overactive, spinning mind and invites surrender into stillness and rest.
doctrine_of_signatures: The complex, mandala-like structure of the flower suggests a meditation object, reflecting the plant's capacity to focus and calm the scattered mind. The spiraling tendrils that the vine uses to climb suggest the unwinding of tension and anxiety.
safety_notes: Generally well-tolerated. May potentiate sedative medications and alcohol. Avoid during pregnancy (uterine stimulant). May cause drowsiness; use caution when driving. Rare cases of altered consciousness at very high doses.

parts.typical_compounds: Apigenin, chrysin, vitexin, isovitexin, harman alkaloids, GABA
parts.therapeutic_properties: Anxiolytic, sedative, antispasmodic, nervine, hypotensive, analgesic
parts.notes: The leaves and stems are the primary medicinal parts, harvested during flowering. Used as tea, tincture, or capsule. Often combined with valerian and hops for enhanced sleep support.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Anxiolytic, calming, mildly sedative, heart-opening
parts.notes: The flowers are included with the leaves in most preparations. They contribute additional flavonoids and a subtle uplifting quality that distinguishes passionflower from heavier sedatives.

planet_associations.notes: Uranus governs the nervous system and bioelectric field. Passionflower works on the electrical over-stimulation of the nervous system, calming the Uranian lightning of anxiety and racing thoughts into a state of peaceful alertness.

zodiac_associations.notes: Ruled by Uranus, Aquarius governs the circulatory system and nervous system. Passionflower calms the nervous hyperactivity that can accompany the Aquarian temperament.
Passionflower's ability to dissolve mental tension and invite surrender aligns with Pisces' capacity for release, flow, and spiritual openness.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Clinical trials demonstrate anxiolytic efficacy comparable to oxazepam for generalized anxiety, with fewer side effects. Chrysin and apigenin modulate GABA-A receptors.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 30-60 drops tincture, 2-3 times daily as needed. Or 500mg dried herb capsule, 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: A clinical trial demonstrated significant improvement in sleep quality when passionflower tea was consumed before bed. Particularly effective for insomnia driven by anxious, racing thoughts.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried herb per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Drink 1 cup 30-60 minutes before bed. Combine with valerian for enhanced effect.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Calms the sympathetic nervous system overdrive characteristic of chronic stress. Reduces muscle tension and nervous agitation without causing grogginess.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 30-60 drops tincture in water, 2-3 times daily during periods of heightened stress.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Calming nervine that addresses the anxiety, insomnia, and nervous tension characteristic of hyperthyroid states.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried herb per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Drink 2-3 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Calming nervine that improves sleep quality and reduces the anxiety that amplifies pain perception in fibromyalgia.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried herb per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Drink before bed.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: GABAergic action calms nervous agitation and reduces the physiological arousal underlying anger responses.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-4 ml tincture or 1-2 cups tea, 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: GABAergic action promotes deeper, more stable sleep patterns and reduces the arousal disturbances underlying night terrors.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-4 ml tincture or 500 mg extract, taken 30-60 minutes before bed.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Chrysin and other flavonoids enhance GABA activity, raising the seizure threshold and calming neuronal hyperexcitability.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-4 ml tincture 2-3 times daily. Use as adjunct under medical supervision.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: GABAergic anxiolytic with clinical evidence for generalized anxiety disorder, comparable to oxazepam in one trial.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried herb per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Drink 2-3 cups daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Contains harman and harmine alkaloids with uterotonic activity; chrysin and other flavonoids may stimulate uterine contractions and have shown teratogenic effects in animal models
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during all trimesters; harmala alkaloids may also affect fetal neurological development through MAO inhibition

contraindication_associations.reason: Harmala alkaloids (harman, harmine, harmaline) are reversible MAO-A inhibitors; combining with serotonergic medications may precipitate serotonin syndrome
contraindication_associations.notes: Use caution with SSRIs, SNRIs, and other serotonergic drugs; monitor for symptoms of serotonin syndrome including agitation, hyperthermia, and myoclonus

contraindication_associations.reason: Harmala alkaloids and sedative flavonoids including chrysin pass into breast milk and may cause excessive sedation and CNS depression in nursing infants
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during breastfeeding; infant metabolic capacity for alkaloid clearance is immature

contraindication_associations.reason: Rare cases of hepatotoxicity reported with passionflower supplements; harmala alkaloids undergo hepatic metabolism and may cause cholestatic liver injury
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor liver function if using with pre-existing hepatic conditions; discontinue if signs of liver injury appear

contraindication_associations.reason: May potentiate anticoagulant effects through CYP3A4 interactions affecting warfarin metabolism; chrysin has demonstrated antiplatelet activity in vitro
contraindication_associations.notes: Exercise caution when combining with anticoagulants or before surgical procedures; monitor coagulation parameters

description: Named for the warrior Achilles who used it to staunch battlefield wounds, yarrow is a master wound herb and blood mover with an affinity for the circulatory system. It simultaneously stops bleeding, moves stagnant blood, opens the pores to release fever, and regulates menstrual flow, demonstrating a rare intelligent adaptability in its therapeutic action.
habitat: Meadows, roadsides, grasslands, and waste ground from sea level to alpine zones. Extremely hardy and widely distributed across temperate regions.
energetic_quality: Drying, astringent, moving. Has an amphoteric quality with blood, both stopping hemorrhage and moving stagnation as needed. Opens and closes with intelligence.
doctrine_of_signatures: The finely divided, feathery leaves ('millefolium' means thousand-leaved) suggest the intricate branching of the capillary system, revealing yarrow's deep affinity for the circulation and its ability to work at the finest level of the vascular network.
safety_notes: Avoid during pregnancy (emmenagogue and uterine stimulant). May cause allergic reactions in individuals sensitive to Asteraceae family. May potentiate anticoagulant medications. Prolonged topical use may cause photosensitivity in some individuals.

parts.typical_compounds: Chamazulene, achillein, camphor, 1,8-cineole, flavonoids, tannins, salicylic acid
parts.therapeutic_properties: Hemostatic, vulnerary, diaphoretic, anti-inflammatory, astringent, emmenagogue, febrifuge, circulatory tonic
parts.notes: The flowering tops are the primary medicinal part, harvested at peak bloom. White-flowered varieties are preferred medicinally. Used as tea, tincture, poultice, or essential oil.

parts.typical_compounds: Achillein, tannins, flavonoids, volatile oils, salicylic acid
parts.therapeutic_properties: Hemostatic, astringent, vulnerary, digestive bitter
parts.notes: The feathery leaves are chewed directly and applied to wounds for first aid hemostasis. Also used in tea alongside the flowers. The fresh leaf is traditionally placed on cuts and nosebleeds.

planet_associations.notes: Venus governs the venous circulation, the kidneys, and female reproductive health. Yarrow's ability to regulate menstrual flow and harmonize the circulation reflects Venus's balancing, harmonizing influence.

zodiac_associations.notes: Ruled by Venus, Libra seeks balance and equilibrium. Yarrow's amphoteric action on blood flow, simultaneously stopping hemorrhage and moving stagnation, exemplifies Libran balance.
Aries rules the blood and the warrior archetype. Named for Achilles, yarrow is the warrior's herb, used on the battlefield to staunch wounds, reflecting Aries' martial connection.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Achillein promotes blood clotting while tannins and anti-inflammatory compounds speed wound closure. Applied directly to wounds as a field poultice, this is one of the oldest wound remedies known.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Fresh leaves chewed and applied directly as a poultice to clean cuts and abrasions. Or apply yarrow-infused oil or salve 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Regulates menstrual flow with amphoteric action: reduces excessive bleeding through astringency while promoting flow in cases of stagnation. Relieves menstrual cramps through antispasmodic action.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 30-60 drops tincture, 3 times daily during menstruation. For regulation, take throughout the cycle.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Chamazulene provides anti-inflammatory action similar to chamomile. The diaphoretic quality helps resolve inflammatory processes by opening the channels of elimination.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried flowering tops per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Drink 2-3 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Premier hemostatic herb that both stops bleeding and supports healthy blood clotting through styptic and astringent actions.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried herb per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Drink 2-3 cups daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Contains thujone and camphor which are uterine stimulants; traditionally used as an emmenagogue to promote menstruation and induce labor
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during all trimesters; both internal use and essential oil application contraindicated in pregnancy

contraindication_associations.reason: Paradoxical effects on coagulation: achilleine promotes clotting topically while coumarin derivatives may thin blood systemically, making dosing unpredictable with anticoagulants
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid combining with warfarin or antiplatelet drugs due to unpredictable interaction; traditionally used both to stop bleeding and promote blood flow

contraindication_associations.reason: Member of the Asteraceae family containing sesquiterpene lactones that are potent contact allergens; cross-reactivity with ragweed, chamomile, and chrysanthemum
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid if known Asteraceae allergy; may cause allergic contact dermatitis with topical application

contraindication_associations.reason: Thujone content acts as a GABA-A receptor antagonist, potentially lowering seizure threshold at higher doses or with concentrated preparations
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid concentrated extracts and essential oil with epilepsy; mild tea use poses lower risk but should be discussed with a neurologist

contraindication_associations.reason: Thujone and other volatile terpenoids pass into breast milk and may be neurotoxic to infants; bitter constituents may also alter milk flavor and reduce feeding
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid therapeutic doses while breastfeeding; occasional culinary use in small amounts is likely acceptable

description: A warming, pungent rhizome that has been central to both cuisine and medicine across Asia for over 5,000 years. Ginger is unmatched in its ability to warm the core, stimulate digestion, resolve nausea, and move stagnant inflammatory conditions with its fiery, circulation-promoting chemistry.
habitat: Tropical and subtropical regions with warm, humid climates, rich soil, and partial shade. Cultivated extensively; rarely found in the wild.
native_region: Southeast Asia, likely southern China or the Malay Archipelago
energetic_quality: Warming, drying, stimulating. Ignites digestive fire, drives circulation to the periphery, and disperses cold, damp stagnation.
doctrine_of_signatures: The knobby, branching rhizome resembles joints and intestinal structures, suggesting ginger's affinity for joint inflammation and digestive function. Its fiery pungency reveals its capacity to warm cold, stagnant conditions and move blocked energy.
safety_notes: Generally recognized as safe in culinary and moderate medicinal amounts. High doses (above 6g daily) may cause heartburn or digestive irritation. May interact with anticoagulant medications due to mild blood-thinning effects. Use cautiously with gallstones. Safe in pregnancy for morning sickness at recommended doses.

parts.typical_compounds: Gingerols (6-gingerol primary), shogaols, zingerone, zingiberene, bisabolene, volatile oils
parts.therapeutic_properties: Anti-emetic, carminative, anti-inflammatory, circulatory stimulant, analgesic, diaphoretic, expectorant, digestive tonic
parts.notes: The rhizome (underground stem) is the sole medicinal part. Fresh ginger is richer in gingerols (anti-inflammatory); dried ginger is richer in shogaols (more warming, more pungent). Used fresh, dried, powdered, juiced, as tea, tincture, capsule, or topical compress.

planet_associations.notes: Mars rules heat, inflammation, and the activating force in the body. Ginger's fiery pungency, circulation-stimulating action, and warming therapeutic profile are quintessentially martial.

zodiac_associations.notes: Aries initiates with heat and force. Ginger's ability to ignite digestive fire and drive warming energy through the body reflects Aries' hot, initiating nature.
Co-ruled by Mars, Scorpio governs elimination and transformation. Ginger's capacity to transform stagnation through heat and promote elimination of waste aligns with Scorpionic processes.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Extensively validated for nausea of multiple origins including pregnancy, chemotherapy, and post-surgical. Multiple clinical trials demonstrate efficacy comparable to metoclopramide.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons fresh grated ginger per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Or 250mg dried ginger capsule, up to 4 times daily. For pregnancy nausea: 250mg 4 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: 6-Gingerol and 6-shogaol inhibit COX-2 and prostaglandin synthesis. Clinical trials demonstrate significant reduction in osteoarthritis pain and inflammatory markers.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 500-1000mg dried ginger powder, 2-3 times daily with food. Or 1-2 inches fresh ginger juiced or grated into food daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Stimulates gastric motility, bile secretion, and pancreatic enzyme activity. Relieves bloating, gas, and sluggish digestion through warming carminative action.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Fresh ginger tea before or with meals. 1-2 inches fresh ginger sliced and simmered 10-15 minutes. Drink 1-3 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties address both the inflammatory and pain-signaling components of chronic pain. Particularly effective for musculoskeletal and arthritic pain.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 30-60 drops tincture, 2-3 times daily. Topical: apply ginger compress (cloth soaked in strong ginger decoction) to painful areas for 20 minutes.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Warming circulatory stimulant that promotes peripheral blood flow and reduces platelet aggregation.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 inches fresh root simmered for 10 minutes, or 500 mg dried ginger, 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Anti-inflammatory and prokinetic effects reduce nausea and intestinal inflammation while supporting nutrient absorption.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 inches fresh root steeped in hot water for 10 minutes, or 500 mg dried ginger. 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Reduces prostaglandin-mediated pain and inflammation. Clinical studies show efficacy comparable to NSAIDs for menstrual pain.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 g fresh or dried ginger in tea, 2-3 times daily, especially during painful episodes.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Inhibits prostaglandins and leukotrienes, reducing joint inflammation and pain. Multiple trials confirm benefit in inflammatory arthritis.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 g dried ginger daily, or 250 mg extract 4 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Anti-inflammatory action with emerging research showing benefit in reducing neutrophil extracellular trap formation in lupus.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 inches fresh root steeped for 10 minutes, 2-3 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Warming herb that supports thyroid function and improves the sluggish metabolism characteristic of hypothyroidism.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 inches fresh root steeped 10 minutes, 2-3 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties help reduce widespread muscular pain and stiffness.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 g dried ginger daily, or fresh ginger tea 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Prokinetic and anti-nausea effects support healthy gut motility and reduce IBS-related nausea and discomfort.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 inches fresh root steeped 10 minutes, 2-3 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Clinically proven to reduce pregnancy-related nausea and vomiting (morning sickness) safely in the first trimester.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1 g fresh ginger or 250 mg extract, up to 4 times daily. Do not exceed 1-2 g dried ginger daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Gingerols and shogaols may affect sex steroid differentiation in the fetal brain at high doses; conflicting data on mutagenicity though moderate doses are generally considered safe for nausea
contraindication_associations.notes: Up to 1g dried ginger daily is generally accepted for pregnancy nausea; avoid high-dose supplements and essential oil internally; consult practitioner

contraindication_associations.reason: Gingerols and shogaols inhibit thromboxane synthetase and platelet aggregation; 6-shogaol is a more potent antiplatelet agent than aspirin in vitro
contraindication_associations.notes: Discontinue high-dose supplements 1-2 weeks before surgery; use caution with warfarin, aspirin, and other anticoagulant or antiplatelet drugs

contraindication_associations.reason: While gastroprotective at low doses, high concentrations of gingerols and shogaols can irritate gastric mucosa and stimulate hydrochloric acid secretion
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid consuming on an empty stomach with active ulcers; moderate dietary amounts are typically well tolerated and may actually be protective

contraindication_associations.reason: Positive inotropic and chronotropic effects may increase cardiac workload; high doses can cause palpitations and may potentiate or antagonize cardiac glycosides and antiarrhythmics
contraindication_associations.notes: Culinary amounts are safe; high-dose extracts may interact with calcium channel blockers and antiarrhythmic medications

contraindication_associations.reason: Gingerols enhance insulin sensitivity and secretion, demonstrating significant hypoglycemic effects in clinical studies that may potentiate diabetes medications
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor blood glucose when using therapeutic doses alongside diabetes medications; dietary amounts are generally not a concern

description: The elder tree has been called 'the medicine chest of the common people' for its wide-ranging healing gifts. The fragrant flowers open the pores and break fevers while the dark purple berries are among the most potent natural antivirals known, making elder an indispensable ally for respiratory health and immune resilience across all seasons.
habitat: Hedgerows, woodland edges, riverbanks, and waste ground throughout temperate regions. Prefers moist, nitrogen-rich soil with partial shade to full sun.
energetic_quality: Cooling (flowers), warming (berries). The flowers open and release; the berries build and protect. Together they represent the elder tree's complete cycle of healing.
doctrine_of_signatures: The umbrella-like clusters of tiny white flowers suggest the plant's protective, sheltering nature over the immune system. The dark purple-black berries, rich in anthocyanins, reflect the deep, immune-building potency contained within.
safety_notes: Raw elderberries, leaves, bark, and seeds contain cyanogenic glycosides and must be cooked before consumption. Flowers are safe raw. Ensure correct identification; do not confuse with water elder or other toxic Sambucus species. May stimulate the immune system; use cautiously with autoimmune conditions.

parts.typical_compounds: Flavonoids (quercetin, rutin, kaempferol), chlorogenic acid, volatile oils, mucilage
parts.therapeutic_properties: Diaphoretic, febrifuge, anti-catarrhal, anti-inflammatory, mild diuretic, expectorant, relaxant
parts.notes: Harvested in late spring when fully open and fragrant. Used as tea, tincture, cordial, or topical preparation. The premier diaphoretic herb for breaking fevers and resolving upper respiratory catarrh.

parts.typical_compounds: Anthocyanins, flavonoids (quercetin, rutin), vitamin C, vitamin A, iron, lectins (destroyed by cooking)
parts.therapeutic_properties: Antiviral, immune-stimulating, antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, diaphoretic
parts.notes: The berries must always be cooked before consumption. Used as syrup, wine, tincture, or gummy. Elderberry syrup is the most popular preparation for immune support. Clinical evidence supports antiviral activity against influenza.

planet_associations.notes: Venus governs beauty, nourishment, and the protective, nurturing aspect of nature. Elder's beautifying flowers, nourishing berries, and protective immune-building properties align with Venus's domain of care and sustenance.

zodiac_associations.notes: Ruled by Venus, Libra governs balance and equilibrium. Elder works to restore immune balance, cooling fevers (flowers) while building immune strength (berries).
Also ruled by Venus, Taurus governs the throat. Elderflower and elderberry preparations directly soothe and protect the throat and upper respiratory tract.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Diaphoretic and anti-catarrhal action opens the sinuses, thins mucus, and promotes sweating to resolve upper respiratory infections and hay fever.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-3 teaspoons dried elderflowers per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes covered. Drink 3-4 cups daily during acute respiratory illness. Combine with yarrow and peppermint for fever management.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Elderberry extract demonstrates direct antiviral activity against influenza strains in clinical studies. A 2004 randomized trial showed significant reduction in flu duration and severity with elderberry syrup.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Elderberry syrup: 1 tablespoon daily for prevention; 1 tablespoon every 2-3 hours at onset of illness. Children: half the adult dose. Continue for 5-7 days during acute infection.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Rich anthocyanin and vitamin C content supports adrenal function and combats the oxidative stress that underlies fatigue. Immune-building action prevents the energy drain of subclinical infections.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1 tablespoon elderberry syrup daily as a tonic. Or prepare elderberry-infused honey as a daily nutritive supplement.

contraindication_associations.reason: Contains cyanogenic glycosides (sambunigrin) that release hydrogen cyanide during metabolism; insufficiently processed preparations pose toxicity risk to the fetus
contraindication_associations.notes: Properly prepared elderflower tea from dried flowers is generally considered safe in moderation; avoid raw or unprocessed plant material and elderberry bark, leaves, or roots

contraindication_associations.reason: Potent immunostimulatory properties increase pro-inflammatory cytokine production (IL-1, IL-6, TNF-alpha), potentially exacerbating autoimmune inflammatory cascades
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid with lupus, rheumatoid arthritis, and multiple sclerosis; immune-stimulating effect is therapeutic in colds but harmful in autoimmune disorders

contraindication_associations.reason: Elderflower exhibits insulin-secretagogue activity and enhances glucose uptake in peripheral tissues, potentially causing additive hypoglycemia with diabetes medications
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor blood glucose if diabetic; may require adjustment of insulin or oral hypoglycemic drug dosages

contraindication_associations.reason: Diuretic properties increase renal fluid excretion and may alter electrolyte balance; can increase the excretion of lithium and other renally cleared medications
contraindication_associations.notes: Stay well hydrated; use caution if on lithium therapy as diuretic effect may increase lithium levels to toxic range

description: Its Latin name Salvia means 'to save' or 'to heal,' and sage has been considered a panacea across Mediterranean cultures for millennia. A powerful aromatic herb that clears the mind, dries excessive secretions, and purifies the respiratory tract, sage embodies the wisdom-granting, clarifying energy that its name implies.
habitat: Dry, rocky, calcareous hillsides in Mediterranean climates. Thrives in full sun with well-drained, alkaline soil. Highly drought-tolerant once established.
energetic_quality: Warming, drying, astringent. Strongly drying to excessive secretions (sweat, mucus, saliva). Brings focused, clear energy to foggy, damp conditions.
doctrine_of_signatures: The velvety, grey-green leaves with their pebbly texture suggest the surface of the brain, hinting at sage's well-documented cognitive-enhancing and memory-supporting properties. The plant's extreme longevity and resilience reflect its association with wisdom and the preservation of life.
safety_notes: Contains thujone, which in large doses or prolonged use can be neurotoxic. Avoid therapeutic doses during pregnancy and breastfeeding (reduces milk supply due to drying action). Culinary use is safe. Avoid large doses with epilepsy. May interact with diabetes and sedative medications.

parts.typical_compounds: Thujone, 1,8-cineole, camphor, ursolic acid, rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid, flavonoids
parts.therapeutic_properties: Astringent, antiseptic, carminative, cognitive-enhancing, antiperspirant, anti-inflammatory, expectorant, estrogenic
parts.notes: The leaves are the primary medicinal part, harvested before flowering for maximum essential oil content. Used as tea, tincture, culinary herb, gargle, or smudge. Fresh sage retains more volatile oils; dried sage is more concentrated.

planet_associations.notes: Jupiter rules wisdom, expansion of consciousness, and the liver. Sage's name means 'to save,' and its association with wisdom, longevity, and mental clarity reflects Jupiter's philosophical and life-preserving influence.

zodiac_associations.notes: Ruled by Jupiter, Sagittarius seeks wisdom and higher understanding. Sage's cognitive-enhancing properties and its very name (sage = wise one) align with Sagittarian ideals.
Virgo's association with healing, purification, and discernment resonates with sage's purifying, clarifying, and antiseptic properties.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Antiseptic volatile oils cleanse the respiratory tract. Astringent and expectorant actions dry excessive mucus while loosening congestion. Excellent as a gargle for sore throats.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried sage per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes covered. Drink 2-3 cups daily. For sore throat: use cooled tea as gargle, 3-4 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: A clinical trial showed sage extract significantly improved memory and attention in healthy adults. Inhibits acetylcholinesterase, preserving the neurotransmitter acetylcholine for enhanced cognitive function.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 30-60 drops sage tincture, 1-2 times daily. Or 300-600mg dried sage capsule daily. Effects on cognition may be noticeable within hours.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: The aromatic compounds calm the nervous system while the bitter principles ground scattered energy. Sage's drying quality addresses the nervous sweating that accompanies stress.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1 teaspoon dried sage per cup, steeped 10 minutes. Drink 1-2 cups daily. Sage smudging is traditionally used for energetic cleansing and stress clearing.

contraindication_associations.reason: Contains thujone which is a potent uterine stimulant and emmenagogue; traditionally used to dry up breast milk, indicating hormonal activity incompatible with pregnancy
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid therapeutic doses during all trimesters; culinary amounts in cooking are generally safe but avoid sage tea, tinctures, and essential oil

contraindication_associations.reason: Alpha- and beta-thujone are GABA-A receptor antagonists and potent convulsants; sage essential oil has caused seizures in documented case reports
contraindication_associations.notes: Contraindicated in epilepsy; never use sage essential oil internally; even culinary sage in large quantities may pose risk

contraindication_associations.reason: Traditionally used as a galactofuge (milk-drying agent); thujone and other constituents reduce prolactin secretion and suppress lactation
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid unless intentionally weaning; even moderate sage tea consumption may noticeably reduce milk supply

contraindication_associations.reason: Contains phytoestrogens and compounds that modulate estrogen receptor activity; may promote growth of estrogen-sensitive tumors
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid therapeutic doses with estrogen-receptor-positive cancers; culinary use in small amounts is likely acceptable

contraindication_associations.reason: Rosmarinic acid and other polyphenols in sage significantly lower blood glucose by enhancing insulin action and inhibiting gluconeogenesis
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor blood sugar closely if diabetic; therapeutic sage preparations may potentiate hypoglycemic medications

description: Known as the 'Mushroom of Immortality' in Chinese medicine, reishi has been the supreme tonic for longevity, spiritual cultivation, and immune resilience for over 2,000 years. This woody bracket fungus modulates rather than stimulates the immune system, making it appropriate for both immune deficiency and autoimmune conditions, while its triterpenes calm the spirit and promote deep, restorative sleep.
habitat: Dead or dying hardwood trees, particularly oaks and maples, in temperate and subtropical forests. Grows as a shelf-like bracket fungus on tree trunks and stumps.
energetic_quality: Neutral to slightly warming, grounding, harmonizing. Tonifies the deep reserves of vital energy while calming the spirit and bringing the organism into balanced equilibrium.
doctrine_of_signatures: The lacquered, kidney-shaped cap with its concentric growth rings suggests longevity, structural integrity, and the slow accumulation of wisdom. Its woody, enduring nature and growth on ancient trees reflect Saturn's association with time, structure, and the deepest levels of constitutional strength.
safety_notes: Generally well-tolerated with a long history of safe use. May potentiate anticoagulant and immunosuppressant medications. Rare cases of digestive upset or skin sensitivity. Discontinue 2 weeks before surgery. Start with low doses in those with autoimmune conditions.

parts.typical_compounds: Beta-glucans (polysaccharides), ganoderic acids (triterpenes), sterols, adenosine, germanium
parts.therapeutic_properties: Immunomodulating, adaptogenic, hepatoprotective, cardioprotective, nervine, anti-inflammatory, anti-tumor, spirit-calming
parts.notes: The fruiting body is extremely tough and woody, requiring prolonged decoction or dual-extraction (water + alcohol) to release its medicinal compounds. Beta-glucans are water-soluble; triterpenes require alcohol extraction. Available as decoction, dual-extract tincture, powder, or capsule.

planet_associations.notes: Saturn governs longevity, structural integrity, and deep constitutional strength. Reishi, the Mushroom of Immortality, embodies Saturn's domain through its ability to fortify the body's deepest reserves and promote extraordinary lifespan.

Neptune governs the subtle, spiritual dimensions of healing. Reishi's traditional classification as a Shen (spirit) tonic that calms the mind, deepens meditation, and opens spiritual sensitivity connects it to Neptune's transcendent influence.

zodiac_associations.notes: Ruled by Saturn, Capricorn represents enduring structure and longevity. Reishi's capacity to extend life, build constitutional resilience, and strengthen the body's framework aligns with Capricorn's structural archetype.

Co-ruled by Neptune, Pisces governs spiritual sensitivity and the pineal gland. Reishi's Shen-calming properties and its traditional use as a meditation aid serve the Piscean path of spiritual cultivation.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Beta-glucans activate NK cells, macrophages, and T-lymphocytes while modulating overactive immune responses. Clinical evidence supports use as adjunct therapy in cancer treatment for immune recovery.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Sliced dried reishi simmered for 2-4 hours in water (1-2 liters). Drink 1-3 cups daily. Or dual-extract tincture: 60-90 drops, 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Ganoderic acids modulate the HPA axis and reduce cortisol. Adaptogenic action normalizes the stress response over time. The Shen-calming effect quiets mental restlessness.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Dual-extract tincture: 60-90 drops, 2-3 times daily. Full adaptogenic effects develop over 2-3 months of consistent use.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Addresses fatigue at the deepest constitutional level rather than through stimulation. Improves oxygen utilization, supports mitochondrial function, and rebuilds depleted reserves.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-3g reishi extract powder or capsules daily. Or decoction: 3-5g dried slices simmered 2+ hours, drunk daily. Allow 4-8 weeks for full tonic effect.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Classified as a superior Shen tonic in Chinese medicine, reishi calms the spirit and opens the heart to receive higher wisdom. Traditional use in Taoist spiritual cultivation spans millennia.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Long-simmered decoction taken daily as part of a meditation or spiritual practice. 3-5g dried reishi in 1 liter water, simmered 2-4 hours.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Beta-glucans and triterpenes modulate immune function and reduce neuroinflammation, supporting balanced immune activity.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 g dried slices simmered for 30 minutes, or 1-2 g extract daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Triterpenes and polysaccharides modulate immune function, reducing autoantibody production while supporting overall immune balance.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 g dried slices simmered 30 minutes, or 1-2 g extract daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Immune-modulating triterpenes help rebalance the autoimmune response that drives thyroid tissue destruction.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 g dried slices simmered 30 minutes, or 1-2 g extract daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Immune-modulating and anti-fatigue effects support recovery from the persistent immune activation seen in CFS.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 g dried slices simmered 30 minutes, or 1-2 g extract daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Immune-modulating triterpenes help rebalance the autoimmune response attacking salivary and lacrimal glands.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 g dried slices simmered 30 minutes, or 1-2 g extract daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Immune-modulating effects may help slow the autoimmune destruction of pancreatic beta cells.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 g dried slices simmered 30 minutes, or 1-2 g extract daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Beta-glucans and triterpenes rebalance immune function, reducing autoimmune overactivity without suppressing necessary immune defenses.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 g dried slices simmered 30 minutes, or 1-2 g extract daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Triterpenes inhibit 5-alpha reductase and aromatase, with immunomodulatory effects supporting anti-cancer immunity.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 g dried slices simmered 30 minutes, or 1-2 g extract daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Ganoderic acids and other triterpenoids may inhibit 5-alpha reductase and modulate steroid hormone metabolism; insufficient safety data for use during pregnancy
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during pregnancy due to lack of adequate safety studies; hormonal modulation effects pose theoretical risks to fetal development

contraindication_associations.reason: Ganoderic acids inhibit platelet aggregation by suppressing thromboxane A2 formation; adenosine content provides additional antiplatelet and vasodilatory effects
contraindication_associations.notes: Discontinue 2 weeks before surgery; avoid combining with warfarin, aspirin, or other anticoagulant and antiplatelet medications

contraindication_associations.reason: Beta-glucan polysaccharides are potent immunomodulators that activate macrophages, dendritic cells, and natural killer cells, potentially triggering autoimmune flares
contraindication_associations.notes: Use with caution in lupus, rheumatoid arthritis, and other autoimmune conditions; immune activation may worsen disease activity

contraindication_associations.reason: Case reports of hepatotoxicity with powdered reishi preparations; ganoderic acids undergo hepatic metabolism and may cause liver injury in susceptible individuals
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor liver enzymes periodically with long-term use; discontinue immediately if signs of liver injury develop

contraindication_associations.reason: Triterpenes and adenosine in reishi exhibit ACE-inhibitory and hypotensive activity, potentially causing additive blood pressure lowering with antihypertensive medications
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor blood pressure if combining with antihypertensive drugs; may be beneficial in mild hypertension but requires dose coordination

description: The sacred 'vine of the soul' from the Amazon basin, ayahuasca is traditionally prepared as a ceremonial brew combining Banisteriopsis caapi vine with DMT-containing leaves of Psychotria viridis. This synergistic preparation has been central to Amazonian shamanic healing for centuries and is now the subject of growing clinical interest for treatment-resistant depression and addiction.
habitat: Tropical rainforest of the Amazon basin, growing as a large woody liana (climbing vine) in the forest canopy. Requires warm, humid conditions with rich, well-drained soil.
native_region: Amazon basin: Peru, Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, Bolivia
energetic_quality: Purgative, visionary, transformative. Works on every level simultaneously, cleansing the physical body while opening the doors of visionary perception and catalyzing profound psychological and spiritual transformation.
doctrine_of_signatures: As a vine that spirals upward through the forest canopy toward the light, ayahuasca reflects the soul's journey from darkness toward illumination. The intertwining of two distinct plant species into a single medicine mirrors the union of body and spirit required for healing.
safety_notes: Powerful psychoactive brew. Controlled or restricted in many jurisdictions (though religious exemptions exist). Contains MAOIs requiring strict dietary restrictions (tyramine-containing foods must be avoided). Contraindicated with SSRIs, SNRIs, and many medications due to serotonin syndrome risk. Cardiovascular risks for those with heart conditions. Should only be used in traditional ceremonial context with experienced facilitators. Purging (vomiting, diarrhea) is common and considered part of the healing process.

parts.typical_compounds: Harmine, harmaline, tetrahydroharmine (beta-carboline alkaloids, reversible MAO-A inhibitors)
parts.therapeutic_properties: MAO inhibition (enables oral DMT activity), antidepressant, anti-tremor, neurogenic, purgative, anthelmintic
parts.notes: The bark of Banisteriopsis caapi vine is the foundational component. The beta-carboline alkaloids serve as MAO inhibitors that enable the oral activity of DMT from the admixture plant. The vine itself is considered the teacher; the admixture provides the vision.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Visionary, consciousness-expanding, serotonergic psychedelic
parts.notes: The leaves of Psychotria viridis (chacruna) or Diplopterys cabrerana (chaliponga) provide DMT, which is normally inactive orally but becomes active due to MAO inhibition from B. caapi. This synergistic combination is the basis of the ayahuasca brew.

planet_associations.notes: Neptune governs the dissolution of ordinary boundaries, visionary perception, and the encounter with the transpersonal. Ayahuasca is perhaps the most profoundly Neptunian medicine, opening the doors between worlds and dissolving the barriers between self and cosmos.

zodiac_associations.notes: Ruled by Neptune, Pisces embodies transcendence, spiritual dissolution, and compassionate unity with all life. The ayahuasca experience frequently involves oceanic feelings of cosmic interconnection that are quintessentially Piscean.

Scorpio rules death-rebirth transformation, the underworld journey, and the confrontation with shadow. Ayahuasca ceremonies characteristically involve ego death, purgation, and profound psychological rebirth.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Emerging clinical research demonstrates rapid and sustained antidepressant effects. Harmine alone shows antidepressant and neurogenic properties. The full brew facilitates psychological processing of the root causes of depression.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Administered only in traditional ceremonial context by experienced curanderos or in supervised clinical settings. Typical ceremony involves 1-3 doses of brewed tea over 4-8 hours. Dietary restrictions required for minimum 24 hours before and after.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Produces profound visionary states and non-ordinary consciousness characterized by vivid imagery, encounter with archetypal beings, and experiences of cosmic interconnection. Considered the master plant teacher in Amazonian shamanic traditions.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Ceremonial context only. Preparation and dosing are the domain of the experienced ayahuascero. Integration support before and after ceremony is essential.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: The death-rebirth experience facilitated by ayahuasca directly confronts existential fears and often resolves them through direct experiential encounter with transpersonal dimensions of consciousness.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Ceremonial context with experienced facilitation. Multiple ceremonies over weeks or months may be needed for deep existential work. Integration is as important as the ceremony itself.

contraindication_associations.reason: DMT crosses the placental barrier and activates sigma-1 receptors involved in fetal neurodevelopment; harmine and harmaline are teratogenic in animal studies and have uterotonic activity
contraindication_associations.notes: Absolute contraindication during all trimesters; both DMT and beta-carboline alkaloids pose severe risks to fetal development

contraindication_associations.reason: DMT is a potent 5-HT2A agonist causing intense hallucinations; beta-carboline alkaloids (harmine, harmaline, tetrahydroharmine) are reversible MAO-A inhibitors creating life-threatening serotonin syndrome risk with serotonergic medications
contraindication_associations.notes: Absolute contraindication with SSRIs, SNRIs, MAOIs, and tricyclic antidepressants; contraindicated in schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, and psychotic disorders; requires minimum 2-6 week washout from serotonergic medications

contraindication_associations.reason: MAO inhibition causes acute hypertensive crisis when combined with tyramine-containing foods; DMT increases sympathetic activation, heart rate, and blood pressure; harmine has cardiovascular effects through serotonergic and adrenergic pathways
contraindication_associations.notes: Contraindicated with cardiovascular disease, uncontrolled hypertension, and cardiac arrhythmias; strict dietary restrictions for tyramine required

contraindication_associations.reason: Both DMT and beta-carboline alkaloids significantly lower seizure threshold through intense serotonergic and neurological activation; convulsions documented in adverse event reports
contraindication_associations.notes: Absolute contraindication with epilepsy or history of seizures; may interact dangerously with anticonvulsant medications

contraindication_associations.reason: Harmine and harmaline are potent MAO-A inhibitors that alter hepatic monoamine metabolism; impaired liver function dramatically slows clearance of both beta-carbolines and DMT, prolonging and intensifying effects unpredictably
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid with any hepatic impairment; MAO inhibition combined with reduced metabolic clearance creates dangerous accumulation of biogenic amines

contraindication_associations.reason: MAO-A inhibition prevents breakdown of tyramine and other pressor amines, causing potentially fatal hypertensive crisis; DMT activates trace amine-associated receptors that raise blood pressure
contraindication_associations.notes: Contraindicated with hypertension; strict tyramine-free diet required for at least 24 hours before and after; avoid aged cheeses, fermented foods, cured meats

description: A small, spineless cactus revered for millennia as a sacramental doorway to the divine, its unassuming button form belying the vast inner landscapes it reveals. Peyote is the heart-medicine of the Huichol and Native American Church, a teacher of patience and radical presence.
habitat: Arid limestone deserts and scrublands of the Chihuahuan Desert, growing slowly in rocky, alkaline soils under the shelter of nurse plants.
energetic_quality: Cooling and expanding, with a bitter, earth-rooted quality that opens the heart center and dissolves egoic rigidity.
doctrine_of_signatures: Its round, button-like form resembles a closed eye that, when ingested, opens the inner eye of vision. The cactus grows partially buried like a secret waiting to be unearthed, mirroring how it reveals hidden truths within the psyche.
safety_notes: Produces intense nausea and vomiting in early stages. Contraindicated in heart conditions and psychotic disorders. Legally protected and sacred to indigenous peoples; ethical sourcing is critical. Extremely slow-growing and endangered in the wild.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Visionary, entheogenic, anti-addictive, emetic
parts.notes: The entire above-ground button is traditionally consumed fresh or dried. Bitter alkaloid content increases with age of the plant.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Grounding, sedative in low doses
parts.notes: The taproot is not traditionally consumed but contains lower concentrations of alkaloids. Harvesting the root kills the plant and is discouraged.

planet_associations.notes: Neptune governs visionary states, dissolution of boundaries, and communion with the divine—all hallmarks of the peyote experience.
Saturn reflects peyote's extreme patience in growth, its lessons of endurance, and the structured ceremonial discipline required for its use.

zodiac_associations.notes: Pisces rules transcendence, spiritual surrender, and the dissolution of the ego—the core teachings of peyote ceremony.
Capricorn resonates with peyote's desert endurance, its slow maturation, and the disciplined ceremonial framework surrounding its use.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Used for thousands of years in indigenous ceremony to facilitate direct encounter with the sacred and expanded states of awareness.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Traditionally 4-12 dried buttons consumed in ceremonial context under experienced guidance.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Native American Church ceremonies with peyote have shown significant efficacy in treating alcoholism among indigenous populations.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Administered within structured ceremonial context; not used as a standalone pharmaceutical intervention.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: The profound mystical experiences facilitated by mescaline can catalyze lasting shifts in outlook, meaning, and emotional well-being.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Ceremonial doses only; not recommended for self-administration.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Peyote ceremony provides a framework for confronting mortality, purpose, and one's relationship to the greater web of existence.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Used within traditional all-night ceremony with experienced roadman or medicine person.

contraindication_associations.reason: Mescaline crosses the placental barrier and may cause uterine contractions; associated with teratogenic risk in animal studies
contraindication_associations.notes: Absolutely avoid during all trimesters; mescaline exposure during fetal development poses significant risk

contraindication_associations.reason: Mescaline is a potent serotonin 5-HT2A agonist that can trigger psychotic episodes, severe anxiety, and dissociation in vulnerable individuals
contraindication_associations.notes: Contraindicated in schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, psychosis history, and severe anxiety disorders

contraindication_associations.reason: Mescaline causes sympathomimetic effects including tachycardia, increased cardiac output, and vasoconstriction
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor cardiovascular function; avoid with arrhythmias, heart failure, or recent cardiac events

contraindication_associations.reason: Mescaline lowers the seizure threshold through serotonergic and dopaminergic overstimulation
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid in epilepsy or any seizure history; may interfere with anticonvulsant medications

contraindication_associations.reason: Mescaline undergoes hepatic metabolism via CYP450 enzymes; impaired clearance increases toxicity risk and prolongs effects
contraindication_associations.notes: Compromised liver function may lead to dangerously elevated plasma levels

description: A towering columnar cactus of the Andes, San Pedro is the grandfather medicine of South American curanderismo, offering expansive heart-opening visions beneath open skies. Unlike the introspective depth of peyote, San Pedro radiates outward, connecting the seeker to the living world.
habitat: Andean mountain slopes at 2,000-3,000 meters elevation, thriving in well-drained soils with cool nights and bright sun.
native_region: Peru, Ecuador, Bolivia, and surrounding Andean regions
energetic_quality: Warming and expansive, with a heart-centered radiance that dissolves interpersonal barriers and fosters empathic connection to nature.
doctrine_of_signatures: Its tall, ribbed columnar form reaches skyward like a cathedral spire, reflecting its role as a bridge between earth and heaven. The star-shaped cross-section embodies the four directions, anchoring the seeker in sacred space.
safety_notes: Causes significant nausea and purging in early stages. Contraindicated in cardiac conditions and severe mental health disorders. Should be used with experienced guidance. Legal status varies by jurisdiction.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Visionary, heart-opening, empathogenic, purgative
parts.notes: The outer green flesh beneath the skin contains the highest alkaloid concentration. Traditionally prepared as a slow-cooked decoction.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Entheogenic, anti-inflammatory
parts.notes: The mucilaginous sap is sometimes extracted and concentrated for ceremonial preparation.

planet_associations.notes: Neptune governs the visionary and boundary-dissolving qualities central to the San Pedro experience.
The Sun reflects San Pedro's heart-opening warmth, its daytime ceremonial use, and its radiant expansive energy.

zodiac_associations.notes: Pisces mirrors San Pedro's capacity to dissolve the boundary between self and world, fostering oceanic unity consciousness.
Leo resonates with San Pedro's solar, heart-centered generosity and its tendency to open the seeker to joy and creative vitality.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Andean curanderos have used San Pedro for over 3,000 years to facilitate visionary healing ceremonies and direct spiritual experience.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: A section of cactus (typically 12-18 inches) is slow-cooked into a concentrated decoction for ceremonial use.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: The empathogenic and heart-opening qualities restore a felt sense of connection to nature, community, and purpose.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Ceremonial doses under guidance of experienced curandero.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: San Pedro's heart-opening action allows suppressed grief to surface and be processed within a supportive ceremonial container.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Single ceremonial dose in supported setting; integration work is essential.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: The expanded empathic awareness can allow re-processing of traumatic memories without overwhelming re-traumatization.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Used in guided ceremonial context with experienced practitioner and aftercare support.

contraindication_associations.reason: Contains mescaline which crosses the placental barrier; alkaloid content may stimulate uterine smooth muscle
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during pregnancy; mescaline poses teratogenic and abortifacient risk

contraindication_associations.reason: Mescaline acts as a 5-HT2A receptor agonist producing profound psychoactive effects that can destabilize fragile mental states
contraindication_associations.notes: High risk of triggering psychosis, panic attacks, or PTSD flashbacks in predisposed individuals

contraindication_associations.reason: Mescaline and related alkaloids in San Pedro produce tachycardia, hypertension, and increased myocardial oxygen demand
contraindication_associations.notes: Cardiovascular stress may be dangerous with coronary artery disease or cardiomyopathy

contraindication_associations.reason: Serotonergic overstimulation from mescaline can lower seizure threshold
contraindication_associations.notes: Contraindicated with epilepsy; may reduce efficacy of anticonvulsant therapy

contraindication_associations.reason: Mescaline and its metabolites are excreted in breast milk, exposing the nursing infant to psychoactive compounds
contraindication_associations.notes: Discontinue nursing if exposure occurs; alkaloids may affect infant neurological development

description: The sacred root bark of the Bwiti tradition, iboga is a fierce and uncompromising teacher that takes the seeker on a panoramic life review, confronting unresolved wounds with surgical precision. It is among the most potent and physically demanding of all plant medicines.
habitat: Dense tropical rainforest understory in equatorial West-Central Africa, thriving in humid, shaded conditions with rich laterite soils.
energetic_quality: Intensely stimulating yet deeply introspective, with a bitter, penetrating quality that roots into the nervous system and demands complete surrender.
doctrine_of_signatures: The medicine resides in the roots, the hidden underground network, reflecting iboga's power to unearth buried memories and unconscious patterns. Its bitter taste mirrors the difficult truths it brings to light.
safety_notes: Potentially fatal due to cardiac effects; requires full medical screening including EKG before use. Contraindicated with heart conditions, liver disease, and many medications. Must only be administered by experienced providers in medically supervised settings. Deaths have occurred from unsupervised use.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Anti-addictive, visionary, psychospiritual healing, stimulant
parts.notes: The root bark is the primary medicine, traditionally scraped and consumed in large quantities during Bwiti initiation. Contains the highest concentration of ibogaine.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Stimulant, entheogenic, appetite suppressant
parts.notes: Stem bark contains lower alkaloid concentrations and is sometimes used in smaller doses as a stimulant and hunger suppressant during forest work.

planet_associations.notes: Pluto governs death-and-rebirth transformation, the confrontation with shadow material, and the radical metamorphosis iboga demands.
Saturn reflects iboga's unflinching confrontation with consequences, its demand for accountability, and the structured discipline of the Bwiti tradition.

zodiac_associations.notes: Scorpio embodies iboga's descent into the underworld of the psyche, its power to transform through unflinching encounter with death and rebirth.
Capricorn mirrors the disciplined, structured nature of Bwiti initiation and iboga's demand for personal responsibility and maturation.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Ibogaine has demonstrated remarkable ability to interrupt opioid, cocaine, and alcohol addiction by resetting receptor sites and providing psychospiritual insight into the roots of addictive behavior.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Flood doses of 15-25 mg/kg ibogaine HCl administered in clinical settings with cardiac monitoring. Followed by integration support.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: The panoramic life-review experience allows systematic re-processing of traumatic memories from a detached, witnessing perspective.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Full flood dose in medically supervised setting; multi-day observation period required.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Ibogaine's action on multiple neurotransmitter systems and its capacity to generate meaningful autobiographical insight can produce lasting shifts in depressive patterns.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Clinical doses under medical supervision; lower sub-perceptual doses also under investigation.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: The Bwiti initiation with iboga is considered a complete confrontation with one's life narrative, death, and spiritual origin.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Traditional initiation doses are large and administered over 24-72 hours under continuous elder supervision.

contraindication_associations.reason: Ibogaine is a potent alkaloid that crosses the placenta; associated with fetal neurotoxicity and cardiac effects in animal models
contraindication_associations.notes: Absolutely contraindicated; ibogaine carries severe teratogenic and embryotoxic risk

contraindication_associations.reason: Ibogaine prolongs the QT interval by blocking hERG potassium channels, creating risk of fatal cardiac arrhythmias including torsades de pointes
contraindication_associations.notes: Multiple fatalities documented from ibogaine-induced cardiac arrest; full cardiac screening required before any use

contraindication_associations.reason: Ibogaine produces intense and prolonged psychoactive effects lasting 24-48 hours via action on multiple receptor systems including NMDA, opioid, and serotonin receptors
contraindication_associations.notes: Severe risk of psychotic breaks, dissociative crisis, and retraumatization; contraindicated in psychosis and bipolar disorder

contraindication_associations.reason: Ibogaine undergoes extensive first-pass hepatic metabolism via CYP2D6 to noribogaine; hepatic impairment drastically alters pharmacokinetics and increases toxicity
contraindication_associations.notes: Impaired hepatic clearance leads to dangerous accumulation; liver function tests mandatory before use

contraindication_associations.reason: Ibogaine exhibits proconvulsant activity through NMDA receptor antagonism and serotonergic stimulation
contraindication_associations.notes: Documented seizure cases in clinical settings; avoid with any seizure history

contraindication_associations.reason: Noribogaine, the primary active metabolite, is renally excreted; impaired renal function prolongs exposure and increases toxicity
contraindication_associations.notes: Renal function should be assessed before use; dose adjustment is not feasible due to unpredictable metabolism

description: The sage of the diviners, a mint-family oracle cultivated by Mazatec healers in the cloud forests of Oaxaca for centuries of quiet, introspective communion. Salvia offers brief but extraordinarily powerful journeys into alien geometries of consciousness unlike any other known entheogen.
habitat: Humid cloud forests at 300-1,800 meters elevation, growing in shaded ravines with rich, moist soil and high humidity.
energetic_quality: Profoundly cooling and dissociative, with a quality of dimensional displacement that shifts the felt sense of gravity, identity, and spatial orientation.
doctrine_of_signatures: As a member of the sage family, it belongs to the lineage of wisdom plants. Its preference for dark, hidden ravines mirrors its capacity to reveal hidden dimensions of reality normally concealed from ordinary perception.
safety_notes: Produces extremely intense, disorienting experiences that can cause falls and injury. Must never be used alone. Effects are brief (5-20 minutes when smoked) but can be terrifying. Contraindicated in psychotic disorders. A sitter is absolutely essential.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Entheogenic, dissociative, analgesic, diaphoretic
parts.notes: Fresh leaves are traditionally rolled into a quid and chewed for a gentler, longer experience. Dried leaves may be smoked for intense but brief effects.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Divinatory, visionary, consciousness-altering
parts.notes: The Mazatec traditionally use the whole fresh plant in darkness and silence, emphasizing the plant's preference for quiet, introspective settings.

planet_associations.notes: Neptune governs the dissolution of consensus reality and entry into wholly other dimensions of perception—the hallmark of salvia's action.
Uranus reflects the sudden, shocking, and utterly unexpected nature of the salvia experience, which ruptures all assumptions about the nature of reality.

zodiac_associations.notes: Pisces governs the dissolution of ordinary boundaries and surrender to transpersonal currents, echoing salvia's radical ego dissolution.
Aquarius reflects the alien, futuristic, and geometrically abstract quality of salvia visions, which often feel more technological than organic.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Produces rapid access to non-ordinary states of consciousness that fundamentally challenge assumptions about the nature of self and reality.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Mazatec method: quid of 8-20 fresh leaves chewed in darkness. Smoked: plain leaf or low-concentration extract with a sitter present.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Kappa-opioid receptor agonism may produce antidepressant effects; research is preliminary but promising.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Sub-breakthrough doses used experimentally; formal clinical protocols not yet established.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Preliminary evidence suggests kappa-opioid activity may help interrupt stimulant addiction patterns.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Research-stage dosing only; self-treatment not recommended.

contraindication_associations.reason: Salvinorin A is an extremely potent kappa-opioid receptor agonist; effects on fetal development are unknown but kappa-opioid activation may disrupt placental function
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during pregnancy; no safety data exists for prenatal exposure

contraindication_associations.reason: Salvinorin A produces intense dissociative and dysphoric states through selective kappa-opioid agonism, which can trigger psychotic episodes and severe depersonalization
contraindication_associations.notes: High risk of dissociative crisis and panic; contraindicated in psychotic disorders, dissociative disorders, and severe depression

contraindication_associations.reason: Kappa-opioid receptor activation can modulate GABAergic neurotransmission and potentially lower seizure threshold in susceptible individuals
contraindication_associations.notes: Use caution in epilepsy; limited data but altered consciousness states may mask or provoke seizure activity

contraindication_associations.reason: Kappa-opioid activation produces transient cardiovascular effects including blood pressure fluctuations and dysregulated autonomic responses
contraindication_associations.notes: May be risky in severe cardiovascular disease due to unpredictable hemodynamic changes during acute effects

description: The luminous, spiraling vine whose seeds carry ergine, a natural relative of LSD, used by the Zapotec and Aztec as tlitlitzin—the sacred black seeds of divinatory vision. Morning glory embodies the paradox of the ordinary garden plant concealing extraordinary depths.
habitat: Tropical and subtropical regions, readily climbing fences, trellises, and other structures in full sun with moderate moisture.
energetic_quality: Cool and luminous, with a gently spiraling expansiveness that lifts perception upward and outward like the vine itself reaching toward light.
doctrine_of_signatures: The vine spirals upward in a helix, mirroring the ascending spiral of consciousness its seeds facilitate. The flowers open at dawn and close by noon, suggesting revelation that arrives with the morning light of new awareness.
safety_notes: Commercially available seeds are often treated with fungicides or emetics to discourage ingestion. Causes significant nausea and vasoconstriction. Contraindicated in pregnancy and cardiovascular conditions. Ergine content varies widely between seed varieties.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Entheogenic, sedative, uterotonic, anxiolytic
parts.notes: Seeds are the sole psychoactive part, traditionally ground and consumed as an aqueous infusion. The Heavenly Blue variety is most commonly associated with entheogenic use.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Mildly sedative, traditional poultice material
parts.notes: Leaves contain trace alkaloids and are sometimes used in traditional medicine as a poultice but are not the primary entheogenic material.

planet_associations.notes: Neptune governs the dreamy, visionary, and boundary-softening nature of the ergine experience.
The Moon reflects morning glory's connection to dreams, intuition, and the cyclical opening and closing of the flowers with the rhythms of light.

zodiac_associations.notes: Pisces resonates with the dreamy, fluid, and gently transcendent quality of the morning glory experience.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Aztec and Zapotec priests used ololiuqui seeds as a divinatory sacrament for accessing visionary states and communicating with the divine.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Traditional preparation involves grinding seeds and soaking in cold water; 150-300 seeds of Heavenly Blue variety.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Low doses can produce vivid, lucid dreaming and enhanced dream recall when taken before sleep.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Sub-entheogenic doses of 25-50 seeds taken as a cold-water infusion before sleep.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: The sedative and anxiolytic properties of ergine may provide temporary relief from acute anxiety states.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Low doses only; higher doses may paradoxically increase anxiety due to vasoconstriction and nausea.

contraindication_associations.reason: Seeds contain ergine (LSA) and other ergot alkaloids which are potent uterotonic agents that stimulate uterine contractions and can cause miscarriage
contraindication_associations.notes: Ergot alkaloids are classic abortifacients; absolutely contraindicated throughout pregnancy

contraindication_associations.reason: LSA produces psychedelic effects through serotonin 5-HT2A receptor agonism, capable of triggering psychotic episodes and hallucinogen persisting perception disorder
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid with history of psychosis, schizophrenia, or bipolar disorder; risk of prolonged psychological disturbance

contraindication_associations.reason: Ergot alkaloids cause peripheral vasoconstriction and coronary vasospasm; can induce angina and dangerous changes in blood pressure
contraindication_associations.notes: Ergot-related vasospasm can be life-threatening with existing coronary artery disease

contraindication_associations.reason: Ergot alkaloids suppress prolactin secretion and are excreted in breast milk, posing risk to the nursing infant
contraindication_associations.notes: Ergot alkaloids may inhibit lactation and expose infant to psychoactive compounds

contraindication_associations.reason: Ergot alkaloids undergo hepatic metabolism; impaired liver function increases risk of ergotism and prolonged vasoconstriction
contraindication_associations.notes: Reduced hepatic clearance can intensify and prolong toxic effects

description: A vigorous tropical climber bearing large, silvery-haired seeds that contain potent concentrations of ergine and related lysergamides. Hawaiian Baby Woodrose offers a longer and deeper ergine journey than its morning glory cousin, with a more embodied and emotionally penetrating character.
habitat: Tropical forests, coastal areas, and cultivated gardens, climbing vigorously over trees and structures in warm, humid climates.
native_region: Indian subcontinent (India, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka); naturalized in Hawaii and the Caribbean
energetic_quality: Warming and deeply grounding despite its visionary action, with a heavy, sedating quality that anchors the experience in the body.
doctrine_of_signatures: The large, silvery-furred seeds resemble small moons cradled in their pods, reflecting the lunar and dreamlike quality of the experience they produce. The vine's tenacious climbing habit mirrors the ascending reach of consciousness.
safety_notes: Causes pronounced nausea, vasoconstriction, and lethargy. Seed coatings may contain toxic compounds and are often removed before ingestion. Contraindicated in pregnancy, cardiovascular disease, and liver conditions. Effects last 6-10 hours.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Entheogenic, sedative, anxiolytic, vasoconstrictive
parts.notes: Seeds are the primary psychoactive material, containing significantly higher ergine concentrations than morning glory. The outer seed coat is typically removed to reduce nausea.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Anti-inflammatory, wound healing, mild analgesic
parts.notes: Leaves are used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine as a topical application for skin conditions and joint inflammation, separate from entheogenic use.

planet_associations.notes: Neptune rules the visionary, dreamy, and boundary-dissolving states produced by the ergine-rich seeds.
The Moon reflects the deeply emotional, intuitive, and dream-enhancing qualities of the Hawaiian Baby Woodrose experience.

zodiac_associations.notes: Pisces governs the oceanic, dreamy dissolution and emotional depth characteristic of the Hawaiian Baby Woodrose journey.
Cancer resonates with the deeply emotional, nurturing, and memory-surfacing qualities of the experience, which often brings childhood memories to the surface.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Produces sustained visionary states lasting 6-10 hours with pronounced introspective and emotional depth.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 4-8 seeds with outer coating removed, chewed thoroughly or ground and consumed with water.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Sub-entheogenic doses reliably enhance dream vividness, emotional depth, and recall.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 seeds taken before sleep for dream enhancement without full entheogenic effects.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: The emotionally opening and perspective-shifting nature of the experience may provide temporary but meaningful relief from depressive rumination.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Entheogenic doses in supported settings; not a replacement for ongoing mental health care.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: The sustained visionary experience can facilitate meaningful encounters with questions of purpose, mortality, and belonging.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Full dose in safe, comfortable setting with experienced support.

contraindication_associations.reason: Seeds contain high concentrations of ergine (LSA) and other ergot alkaloids with potent oxytocic and uterotonic properties
contraindication_associations.notes: Strongly abortifacient due to ergot alkaloid content; contraindicated throughout pregnancy

contraindication_associations.reason: LSA is a 5-HT2A agonist producing hallucinogenic effects; higher ergot alkaloid content than morning glory increases risk of adverse psychological reactions
contraindication_associations.notes: Higher potency than morning glory seeds; significant risk of psychotic episodes and lasting perceptual disturbances

contraindication_associations.reason: Ergot alkaloids cause significant peripheral and coronary vasoconstriction; may induce vasospastic angina and arrhythmias
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid with any cardiovascular conditions; ergot-induced vasospasm can be fatal

contraindication_associations.reason: Serotonergic stimulation by LSA may lower seizure threshold; ergot alkaloids can also cause convulsions at higher doses
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid in epilepsy; convulsions reported as a symptom of ergot alkaloid toxicity

contraindication_associations.reason: Hepatic metabolism of ergot alkaloids is impaired in liver disease, leading to accumulation and increased risk of ergotism
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor for signs of ergotism including peripheral vasoconstriction and nausea

description: The iconic red-capped mushroom of fairy tales and shamanic antiquity, fly agaric is the oldest known entheogen in human history, woven into the spiritual traditions of Siberian shamanism and possibly Vedic soma. Its medicine is dreamy, delirious, and deeply connected to the ancestral realm.
habitat: Temperate and boreal forests forming mycorrhizal relationships with birch, pine, spruce, and fir trees in acidic, well-drained soils.
native_region: Circumboreal across Northern Europe, Asia, and North America
energetic_quality: Warming and intoxicating, with a heavy, dreamy quality that draws consciousness downward into trance states and ancestral memory.
doctrine_of_signatures: The vivid red cap with white spots is nature's most unmistakable signal—simultaneously a warning and an invitation. The mushroom emerges from a white universal veil like consciousness emerging from the undifferentiated, mirroring the birth of awareness from primordial unity.
safety_notes: Contains ibotenic acid which converts to muscimol upon drying; fresh specimens can cause significant toxicity including seizures. Dosing is highly unpredictable due to variable alkaloid content. Can cause dangerous delirium, ataxia, and unconsciousness. Not recommended without extensive preparation knowledge.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Entheogenic, sedative, deliriant, analgesic, GABAergic
