ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-6 grams dried root simmered for 20-30 minutes, taken 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: DGL form increases mucin production in the gastric lining and inhibits H. pylori adhesion, promoting ulcer healing without mineralocorticoid side effects.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 380-760 mg DGL chewed 20 minutes before meals, 3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Supports adrenal function by inhibiting cortisol breakdown via 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase inhibition, extending cortisol half-life during adrenal insufficiency.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-4 grams root in decoction with other Qi-tonifying herbs, used in short therapeutic courses.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Glycyrrhizin demonstrates broad-spectrum antiviral activity and the root's demulcent properties soothe sore throat and reduce mucosal inflammation.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 grams root steeped in hot water for 10-15 minutes, taken 3-4 times daily during acute illness.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Glycyrrhizin reduces serum testosterone through inhibition of 17-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase. Traditionally paired with white peony for PCOS.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-4 g dried root simmered for 15 minutes, taken daily. Monitor blood pressure.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Deglycyrrhizinated licorice (DGL) supports mucosal repair and protects the gut lining from further inflammatory damage.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: DGL: 380-760 mg chewable tablets before meals. Regular: 1-2 g root simmered 10 minutes.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Supports adrenal function and prevents cortisol breakdown, addressing the adrenal insufficiency component of CFS.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 g dried root simmered 10 minutes, 1-2 cups daily. Monitor blood pressure.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Inhibits 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, preventing cortisol breakdown and supporting adrenal output during depletion.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 g dried root simmered 10 minutes, taken in the morning. Avoid in hypertension.

contraindication_associations.reason: Glycyrrhizin inhibits 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 2, causing cortisol to activate mineralocorticoid receptors, resulting in sodium retention, potassium excretion, and significant blood pressure elevation.
contraindication_associations.notes: Strongly contraindicated in hypertension. Even moderate intake can cause pseudoaldosteronism. DGL (deglycyrrhizinated licorice) is a safer alternative.

contraindication_associations.reason: Licorice-induced hypokalemia from mineralocorticoid excess can prolong the QT interval, trigger cardiac arrhythmias, and exacerbate heart failure through fluid retention.
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid in congestive heart failure, arrhythmias, and coronary artery disease. Monitor potassium levels if use is unavoidable.

contraindication_associations.reason: Glycyrrhizin-mediated mineralocorticoid excess causes sodium and fluid retention, increasing glomerular pressure and accelerating renal function decline in compromised kidneys.
contraindication_associations.notes: Contraindicated in chronic kidney disease. The impaired renal excretion of glycyrrhizin further amplifies toxicity.

contraindication_associations.reason: Glycyrrhizin crosses the placenta and inhibits placental 11-beta-HSD2, exposing the fetus to excess maternal cortisol. Associated with preterm birth and impaired cognitive development in offspring.
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during pregnancy. Studies link high consumption to shorter gestational length and lower birth weight.

contraindication_associations.reason: Licorice root contains isoflavones including glabridin and liquiritigenin that exhibit estrogenic activity by binding estrogen receptors, potentially promoting estrogen-dependent tumor growth.
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid in hormone-receptor-positive cancers. Consult oncologist before any use.

description: Dong Quai, known as the Female Ginseng, is the premier blood tonic in traditional Chinese medicine and one of the most important herbs for gynecological health across Asian healing traditions. Its aromatic, fleshy root nourishes and invigorates the blood, regulates menstruation, and harmonizes the uterine environment with a gentle yet profound efficacy.
habitat: Cool, high-altitude mountain regions at 2500-3000 meters, in moist, shaded ravines with rich, loamy soil.
native_region: High-altitude regions of China (Gansu, Sichuan, Yunnan provinces)
energetic_quality: Warm, sweet, pungent, bitter; nourishes blood, invigorates blood circulation, moistens the intestines, and regulates menstruation.
doctrine_of_signatures: The root's rich, blood-red interior and its aromatic, warming nature signal its deep affinity for nourishing and moving the blood, particularly in the pelvic region and reproductive organs.
safety_notes: Avoid during pregnancy and with heavy menstrual bleeding. Contains furanocoumarins that increase photosensitivity. May potentiate anticoagulant medications. Not recommended with estrogen-sensitive conditions without professional guidance.

parts.typical_compounds: Z-ligustilide, ferulic acid, polysaccharides, butylidenephthalide, furanocoumarins (bergapten, psoralen), vitamins B12 and E
parts.therapeutic_properties: Blood tonic, emmenagogue, antispasmodic, analgesic, mild sedative, anti-inflammatory, immunomodulatory
parts.notes: Different parts of the root are used for different purposes in TCM: the head tonifies blood, the body nourishes blood, and the tail invigorates blood and breaks stasis.

parts.typical_compounds: Essential oils, coumarins, flavonoids (lower concentration than root)
parts.therapeutic_properties: Mild antispasmodic, aromatic, carminative
parts.notes: Leaves are occasionally used in folk preparations and teas but are considered far less potent than the root for blood-nourishing purposes.

planet_associations.notes: Dong Quai's deep association with feminine reproductive health, blood nourishment, and its role as the supreme women's tonic connect it directly to Venus's governance of the feminine body, fertility, and hormonal harmony.
Its regulation of menstrual cycles and its influence on the fluids and blood of the body resonate with the Moon's governance of cycles, tides, and feminine rhythms.

zodiac_associations.notes: Dong Quai's lunar sympathies, its blood-nourishing and uterine-regulating properties, and its deep connection to maternal and reproductive health align with Cancer's domain of nurturing, fertility, and the womb.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Z-ligustilide and ferulic acid regulate uterine smooth muscle, promoting healthy contraction patterns and normalizing menstrual flow and timing.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 6-12 grams dried root simmered for 30-45 minutes, taken daily in the follicular phase of the menstrual cycle.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Butylidenephthalide provides antispasmodic and analgesic effects, particularly for cramping pain related to blood stasis.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 mL of 1:3 tincture taken 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Invigorates blood circulation, reduces blood viscosity, and inhibits platelet aggregation, improving flow to the extremities and pelvic region.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 6-9 grams root decocted with other blood-moving herbs such as red peony and ligusticum.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: As a blood tonic, dong quai addresses fatigue stemming from blood deficiency patterns with symptoms of pallor, dizziness, and blurred vision.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 6-12 grams decocted with astragalus and codonopsis for Qi and blood dual tonification.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Supports pelvic blood circulation and reduces stagnation, while modulating estrogen metabolism implicated in endometrial growth.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-4 ml tincture twice daily. Avoid during heavy menstrual bleeding.

contraindication_associations.reason: Dong quai contains coumarins (including osthole and psoralen) that inhibit platelet aggregation and potentiate anticoagulant effects of warfarin by competing for CYP2C9-mediated metabolism.
contraindication_associations.notes: Contraindicated with warfarin and other anticoagulants. Discontinue at least 14 days before surgery. Monitor INR closely.

contraindication_associations.reason: Ferulic acid and ligustilide in dong quai exhibit phytoestrogenic activity, binding estrogen receptors and potentially stimulating proliferation of hormone-receptor-positive cancer cells.
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid in estrogen-receptor-positive breast, ovarian, and endometrial cancers. Not recommended during tamoxifen or aromatase inhibitor therapy.

contraindication_associations.reason: Dong quai stimulates uterine contractions through direct smooth muscle stimulation and prostaglandin modulation, significantly increasing risk of miscarriage and preterm labor.
contraindication_associations.notes: Strictly contraindicated during pregnancy. Traditional Chinese medicine classifies it as an emmenagogue.

contraindication_associations.reason: Dong quai contains furanocoumarins that cause photosensitization and may trigger phototoxic dermatitis. Cross-reactivity exists with other Apiaceae family members (celery, carrot, parsley).
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid excessive sun exposure during use. Individuals allergic to Apiaceae family plants should exercise caution.

description: Gotu Kola is revered across Ayurvedic, Chinese, and Southeast Asian medicine systems as the premier herb for the mind and nervous system, earning the epithet Brahmi in some traditions for its capacity to expand consciousness and sharpen intellect. This humble creeping plant, which thrives near water, has been used for millennia to enhance meditation, heal wounds, and restore clarity to the mind.
habitat: Tropical and subtropical wetlands, marshy areas, riverbanks, and shaded moist ground; thrives in humid conditions near water sources.
native_region: Indian subcontinent, Southeast Asia, southern China
energetic_quality: Cool, bitter, sweet; calms the mind, clears heat, nourishes the nervous tissue (majja dhatu), and promotes sattvic (pure) mental quality.
doctrine_of_signatures: The round, kidney-shaped leaves resembling miniature brains or the cerebral cortex signal gotu kola's powerful affinity for the brain, nervous system, and cognitive enhancement.
safety_notes: Rare cases of hepatotoxicity reported with prolonged high-dose use. Avoid with liver disease. May cause photosensitivity in sensitive individuals. Cycling use (6 weeks on, 2 weeks off) is often recommended.

parts.typical_compounds: Asiaticoside, madecassoside, asiatic acid, madecassic acid, brahmoside, brahminoside, centellose
parts.therapeutic_properties: Nootropic, anxiolytic, wound healing, venotropic, neuroprotective, collagen stimulant, adaptogenic
parts.notes: The entire aerial herb is used, but the leaves carry the highest concentration of active triterpenoid saponins.

parts.typical_compounds: Triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids (quercetin, kaempferol), phenolic acids, polyacetylenes, volatile oils
parts.therapeutic_properties: Rejuvenative (rasayana), nervine tonic, circulatory stimulant, anti-inflammatory
parts.notes: Fresh whole plant juice is traditionally considered most potent in Ayurveda and is consumed with milk or ghee to enhance absorption.

planet_associations.notes: Gotu kola's profound effects on the brain, cognition, communication, and the nervous system directly correspond to Mercury's governance of the intellect, neural pathways, and mental agility.

Its affinity for watery habitats, its cooling nature, its effect on the mind's clarity and dream states, and its traditional use in meditation align with the Moon's domain of intuition and inner reflection.

zodiac_associations.notes: Under Mercury's rulership, gotu kola's cognitive-enhancing and nervine properties connect it to Gemini's intellectual curiosity, communication, and neural flexibility.
Its Mercurial nature and association with detailed cognitive function, analytical clarity, and systematic healing resonate with Virgo's discerning, health-focused archetype.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Asiaticoside and madecassoside modulate GABA-A receptor activity and reduce acoustic startle response, providing anxiolytic effects without cognitive impairment.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-4 mL of 1:5 tincture taken 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Asiatic acid stimulates collagen synthesis (types I and III), enhances angiogenesis, and accelerates re-epithelialization in both acute and chronic wounds.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Strong infusion applied topically as a compress, or 500-1000 mg standardized extract taken internally daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Enhances dendritic branching and hippocampal neurogenesis, improving memory consolidation, attention span, and cognitive processing speed.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-3 fresh leaves eaten daily, or 1-2 teaspoons dried herb steeped for 10-15 minutes, taken twice daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Triterpenoids improve skin elasticity, reduce scarring, and modulate fibroblast activity in conditions including eczema, psoriasis, and keloid scarring.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300-600 mg standardized extract taken twice daily for 4-8 weeks.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Triterpenoid saponins support nerve regeneration and improve microcirculation, aiding in myelin repair processes.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried herb per cup, steeped 10 minutes. Drink 2-3 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Supports connective tissue health and wound healing, particularly beneficial for lupus-related skin manifestations.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried herb per cup, steeped 10 minutes. Drink 2-3 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Supports thyroid function and reduces inflammation, traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for thyroid conditions.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried herb per cup, steeped 10 minutes. Drink 2-3 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Anti-inflammatory and connective tissue-supporting herb that helps manage autoimmune tissue damage.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried herb per cup, steeped 10 minutes. Drink 2-3 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Supports kidney tissue repair and reduces fibrosis through triterpenoid saponin action.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried herb per cup, steeped 10 minutes. Drink 2-3 cups daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Asiaticoside and madecassoside have demonstrated embryotoxic effects in animal studies, and gotu kola has traditional use as an emmenagogue that may stimulate uterine activity.
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during pregnancy. Animal data suggests potential risk to fetal development.

contraindication_associations.reason: Rare but documented cases of hepatotoxicity linked to gotu kola triterpenoids, possibly through reactive metabolite formation during hepatic biotransformation via CYP enzymes.
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid in pre-existing liver disease. Monitor liver enzymes periodically during long-term use. Discontinue if jaundice or elevated transaminases occur.

contraindication_associations.reason: Asiatic acid in gotu kola may lower blood glucose by enhancing insulin signaling pathways, potentially causing additive hypoglycemia with antidiabetic medications.
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor blood glucose when combining with diabetes medications. Adjust doses as needed.

description: Brahmi is one of the most important nootropic herbs in Ayurvedic medicine, named after Brahman (the supreme consciousness in Hindu philosophy) for its legendary capacity to expand awareness and enhance the intellect. This small, succulent creeping herb has been used for over three thousand years by Vedic scholars to improve memory, learning, and the capacity for deep meditative absorption.
habitat: Wet, marshy, shallow waterways, and tropical wetlands; often grows semi-aquatically in muddy soils near ponds, rivers, and rice paddies.
native_region: Indian subcontinent, tropical and subtropical Asia
energetic_quality: Cool, bitter, sweet; calms Vata and Pitta, nourishes Majja Dhatu (nerve tissue), opens and clarifies the channels of the mind.
doctrine_of_signatures: The small, rounded leaves growing in interconnected mats along waterways mirror the branching neural networks of the brain, signifying its role in strengthening synaptic connections and neural communication.
safety_notes: May cause mild gastrointestinal upset, nausea, or diarrhea if taken on an empty stomach. Best taken with food or ghee. Caution with thyroid medications as it may increase T4 levels. Generally safe for long-term use.

parts.typical_compounds: Bacosides (A and B), bacopasaponins, brahmine, herpestine, apigenin, luteolin, flavonoids
parts.therapeutic_properties: Nootropic, anxiolytic, neuroprotective, antioxidant, anti-epileptic, memory enhancer
parts.notes: The leaves are the primary medicinal part; bacosides A and B are the principal active compounds responsible for cognitive enhancement.

parts.typical_compounds: Bacosides, triterpenoid saponins, alkaloids (brahmine, nicotine in trace amounts), flavonoids, phytosterols
parts.therapeutic_properties: Rejuvenative (medhya rasayana), adaptogenic, antidepressant, cognitive tonic
parts.notes: The whole aerial plant is used in traditional Ayurvedic preparations, often processed with ghee (brahmi ghrita) for enhanced bioavailability and nervous system penetration.

planet_associations.notes: Brahmi's cooling nature, its aquatic habitat, its capacity to calm the mind and enhance intuitive consciousness, and its association with the subtle mental body connect it to the Moon's governance of the mind (manas), memory, and receptive awareness.
Its direct effects on learning, verbal memory, information processing, and synaptic communication align with Mercury's domain of intellect and neural transmission.

zodiac_associations.notes: Brahmi's lunar rulership, aquatic nature, and deep association with memory, emotional processing, and the nurturing of mental faculties connect it to Cancer's watery, receptive, memory-keeping archetype.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Bacosides enhance synaptic communication, increase dendritic branching in the hippocampus, and upregulate serotonin and acetylcholine activity, improving memory acquisition and retention.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300-450 mg standardized extract (containing 55% bacosides) daily for a minimum of 8-12 weeks for measurable cognitive effects.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Modulates GABA and serotonin receptor systems, reducing both trait and state anxiety without impairing cognitive function.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-3 grams whole dried herb steeped for 15 minutes, taken twice daily with ghee or warm milk.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Reduces cortisol and adapts the stress response, demonstrating both anxiolytic and adaptogenic properties in clinical settings.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-4 mL of 1:3 tincture taken twice daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Calming effects on the nervous system combined with mild sedative properties help establish healthy sleep patterns without morning drowsiness.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 grams whole herb decocted with ashwagandha and warm milk, taken 30-60 minutes before sleep.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Bacosides modulate serotonin and GABA pathways, with traditional use for seizure disorders and neuroprotection.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-4 ml tincture or 300 mg extract twice daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Bacosides in Bacopa monnieri have not been adequately studied for reproductive safety, and the herb's cholinergic activity may affect fetal neurodevelopment unpredictably.
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during pregnancy due to insufficient safety data and theoretical cholinergic risks to the developing fetus.

contraindication_associations.reason: Brahmi stimulates gastric acid secretion and increases acetylcholine activity in the gastrointestinal tract, which can exacerbate peptic ulcer disease and gastric erosions.
contraindication_associations.notes: Take with food to minimize gastric irritation. Avoid in active peptic ulcer disease.

contraindication_associations.reason: Bacosides and bacopasaponins may be excreted in breast milk, and their effects on infant neurodevelopment through cholinergic modulation are unknown.
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during breastfeeding until adequate safety data is available.

contraindication_associations.reason: Brahmi's cholinergic activity can cause bradycardia by increasing vagal tone, which may be clinically significant in patients with pre-existing cardiac conduction disorders.
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor heart rate in patients with bradycardia or heart block. Use with caution alongside beta-blockers or calcium channel blockers.

description: Shatavari, whose Sanskrit name translates to 'she who possesses a hundred husbands,' is the foremost rejuvenative tonic for the female reproductive system in Ayurvedic medicine. Its bundle of tuberous roots nourishes the deepest tissue layers of the body, supports fertility and lactation, and provides cooling, moistening relief to all conditions of dryness and depletion.
habitat: Tropical and subtropical forests, rocky and gravelly soils at elevations from sea level to 1500 meters; often found growing as a thorny climbing vine in deciduous and evergreen forests.
native_region: Indian subcontinent, Sri Lanka, Southeast Asia, tropical Africa
energetic_quality: Cool, sweet, bitter; nourishes Rasa and Shukra Dhatus, pacifies Vata and Pitta, increases Ojas (vital essence), and moistens all dry conditions.
doctrine_of_signatures: The multitude of tuberous roots bundled together (racemosus means 'having many clusters') suggests abundance and fertility, signaling the plant's capacity to nourish reproductive vitality and support the bearing of new life.
safety_notes: Avoid with estrogen-sensitive conditions without professional guidance. May aggravate Kapha-type congestion in individuals with excess dampness. Caution with kidney disorders. Generally very safe for long-term use.

parts.typical_compounds: Steroidal saponins (shatavarin I-IV), isoflavones, asparagamine, racemofuran, mucilage, oligosaccharides
parts.therapeutic_properties: Galactagogue, adaptogenic, demulcent, anti-inflammatory, immunomodulatory, phytoestrogenic, rejuvenative
parts.notes: The tuberous roots are dried, powdered, and traditionally prepared with milk and ghee (shatavari kalpa) to enhance absorption and nourishing properties.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Mild antioxidant, diuretic
parts.notes: The thorny climbing stems are occasionally used in folk preparations but are secondary to the roots in therapeutic importance.

planet_associations.notes: Shatavari's cooling, moistening, nurturing essence and its profound connection to fertility, lactation, menstrual cycles, and the nourishment of bodily fluids align deeply with the Moon's governance of the feminine, maternal principle and fluid regulation.

Its role as the supreme female reproductive tonic, its association with beauty, sensuality, and fertility, and its sweet, nourishing nature connect to Venus's domain of love, attraction, and feminine vitality.

zodiac_associations.notes: Shatavari's lunar rulership, maternal associations, lactation support, and deeply nurturing quality resonate with Cancer's archetype of the Great Mother, fertility, and emotional nourishment.
Its Venusian sweetness, sensual nourishing quality, and connection to physical fertility and abundance align with Taurus's earthy, pleasure-seeking, growth-oriented nature.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Steroidal saponins provide gentle phytoestrogenic support, helping normalize estrogen levels and smooth hormonal transitions across the reproductive lifespan.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 500-1000 mg powdered root twice daily, or 1-2 teaspoons powder in warm milk.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Phytoestrogens and cooling demulcent action reduce hot flashes, night sweats, vaginal dryness, and mood fluctuations associated with menopause.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-6 grams root powder simmered in milk for 15-20 minutes, taken 1-2 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Mucilaginous compounds coat and protect gastric mucosa, while anti-inflammatory saponins reduce mucosal inflammation and promote tissue repair.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 500 mg powdered root taken 30 minutes before meals, 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Nourishes Shukra Dhatu (reproductive tissue), improves vaginal lubrication, and enhances sexual receptivity through hormonal and tissue-level support.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-6 grams root powder prepared with warm milk, honey, and ghee, taken daily for several months.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Premier Ayurvedic galactagogue that enhances breast milk production and quality through hormonal support.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 500 mg-1 g root powder twice daily, or 2-4 ml tincture twice daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Shatavari contains steroidal saponins (shatavarinds) with estrogenic activity that bind estrogen receptors and may promote growth of hormone-receptor-positive tumors.
contraindication_associations.notes: Contraindicated in estrogen-receptor-positive breast, ovarian, and uterine cancers. Avoid alongside hormonal cancer therapies.

contraindication_associations.reason: While traditionally used in postpartum care, shatavari's potent phytoestrogenic saponins may disrupt the delicate hormonal balance required for early pregnancy maintenance.
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during the first trimester. Consult an Ayurvedic practitioner or obstetrician before use in later pregnancy.

contraindication_associations.reason: Shatavari belongs to the Asparagaceae family and can cause allergic reactions in individuals sensitive to asparagus, including urticaria and bronchospasm.
contraindication_associations.notes: Contraindicated in individuals with known asparagus allergy. Start with small test doses in atopic individuals.

contraindication_associations.reason: Shatavari has diuretic properties and contains oxalates that may contribute to kidney stone formation and electrolyte imbalances in patients with impaired renal function.
contraindication_associations.notes: Use cautiously in chronic kidney disease. Ensure adequate hydration and monitor electrolytes.

description: Neem, called the Village Pharmacy in India, is one of the most versatile medicinal trees in the Ayurvedic pharmacopoeia, with virtually every part of the tree possessing therapeutic value. Its intensely bitter taste and powerful antimicrobial properties have made it a cornerstone of tropical medicine for treating infections, skin diseases, parasites, and fever for over four thousand years.
habitat: Tropical and semi-tropical lowlands, arid and semi-arid regions; remarkably drought-tolerant and capable of thriving in poor, sandy, or laterite soils.
energetic_quality: Cold, bitter, astringent; strongly clears heat and toxins, purifies the blood, reduces Pitta and Kapha, dries excess dampness.
doctrine_of_signatures: The tree's extreme bitterness and its natural resistance to nearly all insect pests signal its powerful capacity to purify, cleanse, and protect the body from parasites, infections, and toxic accumulation.
safety_notes: Strong bitter and cold nature can aggravate Vata and weaken digestion if overused. Neem oil should not be ingested in large quantities. Avoid during pregnancy. Neem seed oil can be toxic in children. External use is generally very safe.

parts.typical_compounds: Nimbin, nimbidin, nimbinin, azadirachtin, quercetin, catechins, carotenoids, ascorbic acid
parts.therapeutic_properties: Antibacterial, antifungal, antiviral, antiparasitic, blood purifying, hypoglycemic, anti-inflammatory
parts.notes: Leaves are the most commonly used part in daily Ayurvedic practice, consumed as decoctions, pastes, or dried powder.

parts.typical_compounds: Nimbidin, tannins, nimbin, nimbinin, gallic acid, catechins
parts.therapeutic_properties: Astringent, antipyretic, antiulcer, antimicrobial, dental hygiene
parts.notes: Neem bark is traditionally chewed as a dental hygiene tool (datun) and decocted for fever and skin conditions.

parts.typical_compounds: Azadirachtin (highest concentration), nimbin, salanin, meliantriol, fatty acids (oleic, stearic)
parts.therapeutic_properties: Insecticidal, antiparasitic, spermicidal, anti-inflammatory
parts.notes: Neem seed oil is primarily used externally and in agriculture; azadirachtin concentration is highest in the seeds.

planet_associations.notes: Neem's extreme bitterness, its austere purifying nature, its association with discipline and elimination of what is unwanted, and its capacity to endure harsh environments connect it to Saturn's themes of purification through restriction, cleansing, and karmic correction.

zodiac_associations.notes: Neem's Saturnian austerity, bitter purification, endurance in harsh conditions, and no-nonsense approach to eliminating disease resonate with Capricorn's disciplined, practical, and enduring nature.
Its powerful detoxifying, antiparasitic, and blood-purifying properties align with Scorpio's transformative, eliminative archetype and its governance of toxins and regeneration.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Nimbidin and quercetin provide broad-spectrum antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory action effective against acne, eczema, psoriasis, and fungal skin infections.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Strong infusion of 15-20 leaves applied topically as a wash, or 2-4 grams dried leaf powder taken internally daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Azadirachtin and nimbidin disrupt parasite reproductive cycles and create an inhospitable intestinal environment for helminths and protozoa.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 grams dried leaf powder in decoction, taken on an empty stomach for 7-14 days.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Neem oil and its constituent compounds demonstrate potent antifungal activity against Candida, dermatophytes, and Aspergillus species.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Diluted neem oil (2-5%) applied topically 2-3 times daily; internal use of neem leaf tincture 1-2 mL twice daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Antimicrobial tannins and nimbidin reduce oral bacterial load, inhibit plaque formation, and reduce gingival inflammation.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Bark decoction used as a mouthwash 2-3 times daily, or fresh neem twig chewed as a natural toothbrush.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties address both the skin and joint manifestations of PsA.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried leaf per cup, steeped 10 minutes. Drink 1-2 cups daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Neem oil contains azadirachtin and other limonoids that exhibit potent anti-fertility effects, including abortifacient activity through post-implantation embryo resorption demonstrated in animal studies.
contraindication_associations.notes: Strictly contraindicated during pregnancy and when trying to conceive. Known abortifacient in traditional medicine.

contraindication_associations.reason: Neem leaf extracts contain hepatotoxic compounds including nimbolide that can cause dose-dependent hepatocellular necrosis, particularly dangerous in patients with pre-existing liver impairment.
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid oral neem preparations in liver disease. Monitor liver function tests if topical use is extensive.

contraindication_associations.reason: Neem polysaccharides and nimbidin stimulate macrophage and lymphocyte activity, potentially triggering autoimmune flares by amplifying the aberrant immune response.
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid in autoimmune conditions. The immunostimulatory effects may counteract immunosuppressive therapy.

contraindication_associations.reason: Neem leaf flavonoids and terpenoids significantly reduce blood glucose by enhancing peripheral glucose utilization and hepatic glycogen synthesis, risking hypoglycemia with concurrent antidiabetic drugs.
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor blood glucose carefully. May require adjustment of antidiabetic medication doses.

description: Andrographis, known as the King of Bitters or Kalmegh in Ayurveda, is a powerful bitter herb used extensively across Indian, Chinese, and Southeast Asian medicine for its remarkable immune-stimulating and fever-reducing properties. Its intensely bitter aerial parts have gained significant modern research attention for their effectiveness against upper respiratory infections and their broad-spectrum antimicrobial action.
habitat: Tropical and subtropical plains, forest edges, and wastelands; thrives in moist, shaded to semi-shaded conditions with moderate rainfall.
native_region: Indian subcontinent, Sri Lanka, Southeast Asia
energetic_quality: Cold, intensely bitter; clears heat and toxins, dries dampness, purifies blood, and resolves infection. Reduces Pitta and Kapha but may aggravate Vata.
doctrine_of_signatures: The plant's extreme bitterness, the most bitter of any commonly used herb, signals its potent capacity to clear heat-toxins from the body, acting as a purifying fire that burns away infection and inflammation.
safety_notes: Very cold and bitter nature can impair digestion and aggravate Vata with prolonged use. Avoid during pregnancy (potential anti-fertility effects). May interact with anticoagulant, antihypertensive, and immunosuppressive medications. Best used in acute, short-term courses.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Immunostimulant, antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, antiviral, hepatoprotective, cholagogue, bitter tonic
parts.notes: Leaves contain the highest concentration of andrographolide and are the primary part used in standardized extracts.

parts.typical_compounds: Andrographolide (moderate concentration), diterpene lactones, flavonoids, polyphenols
parts.therapeutic_properties: Bitter tonic, antipyretic, immune stimulant
parts.notes: The entire aerial portion (leaves and stems) is used in traditional preparations; stems contribute to overall diterpene content.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Antipyretic, anti-diarrheal, bitter tonic
parts.notes: Roots are used in some traditional preparations, particularly for gastrointestinal complaints and intermittent fevers.

planet_associations.notes: Andrographis's fierce, combative approach to infection, its immune-activating properties, and its capacity to aggressively clear heat-toxins from the body align with Mars's warrior energy and its governance of acute inflammatory processes and fevers.
Its extreme bitterness, purifying austerity, and capacity to discipline the body through elimination and restriction resonate with Saturn's cleansing severity.

zodiac_associations.notes: Andrographis's Martian rulership, acute fever-resolving action, and aggressive immune stimulation connect to Aries's fast-acting, combative, and pioneering energy.
Its capacity for deep purification, toxin elimination, and transformation of disease states resonates with Scorpio's regenerative, eliminative archetype.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Andrographolide reduces severity and duration of upper respiratory tract infections by enhancing immune cell activity and demonstrating direct antiviral effects.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 400-1200 mg standardized extract (containing 30-60 mg andrographolide) daily at onset of symptoms, for up to 5 days.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Andrographolide inhibits NF-kB activation, reduces pro-inflammatory cytokine expression, and modulates COX-2 activity.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-4 mL of 1:3 tincture taken 3 times daily during acute inflammatory episodes.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Cholagogue and hepatoprotective actions stimulate bile flow and protect hepatocytes from chemical and oxidative damage.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-6 grams dried aerial parts decocted for 15-20 minutes, taken twice daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Intense bitter taste reflexively stimulates digestive secretions, gastric acid, bile, and pancreatic enzymes, improving overall digestive function.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 mL of root tincture taken 15-20 minutes before meals.

contraindication_associations.reason: Andrographolide has demonstrated anti-implantation and abortifacient effects in animal studies by disrupting progesterone-mediated endometrial receptivity.
contraindication_associations.notes: Strictly contraindicated during pregnancy. Known anti-fertility properties in traditional medicine systems.

contraindication_associations.reason: Andrographolide modulates immune function by stimulating both innate and adaptive immunity, including enhanced phagocytosis and T-cell activation, which may exacerbate autoimmune pathology.
contraindication_associations.notes: Use with caution in autoimmune conditions. Monitor for disease flare-ups during use.

contraindication_associations.reason: Andrographolide inhibits platelet-activating factor and thrombin-induced platelet aggregation, increasing hemorrhagic risk especially when combined with anticoagulants.
contraindication_associations.notes: Discontinue at least one week before surgery. Avoid concomitant use with warfarin or aspirin.

contraindication_associations.reason: Andrographolide enhances glucose uptake by activating AMPK signaling in skeletal muscle and adipose tissue, potentially causing hypoglycemia with concurrent antidiabetic therapy.
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor blood glucose levels. Dose adjustments of diabetes medications may be needed.

description: Chinese Skullcap, known as Huang Qin in traditional Chinese medicine, is one of the most important heat-clearing and damp-drying herbs in the Chinese materia medica. Its golden-yellow root has been a mainstay of classical formulas for over two thousand years, valued for its ability to clear inflammatory heat from the lungs, liver, and gastrointestinal tract while demonstrating remarkable broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity.
habitat: Dry, sunny grasslands, stony hillsides, and sandy soils in temperate continental climates at elevations of 200-2000 meters.
native_region: Northern and eastern China, Mongolia, Siberia
energetic_quality: Cold, bitter; clears heat, dries dampness, drains fire (particularly from the upper and middle burners), stops bleeding, and calms the fetus.
doctrine_of_signatures: The bright yellow color of the root's interior signals its affinity for clearing hot, inflammatory conditions, particularly jaundice and hepatic heat, while the hollow, aged roots suggest the clearing of toxic accumulation from within.
safety_notes: Cold and bitter nature contraindicates use in spleen-stomach cold deficiency with diarrhea. Rare cases of hepatotoxicity have been reported, though causality is debated. May interact with cyclosporine and other transplant medications. Avoid during pregnancy unless under professional guidance.

parts.typical_compounds: Baicalin, baicalein, wogonin, wogonoside, oroxylin A, chrysin, skullcapflavone II
parts.therapeutic_properties: Anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, antiviral, hepatoprotective, neuroprotective, anxiolytic, antiallergic, antitumor
parts.notes: The root is the primary medicinal part; older roots with a hollow center (ku qin) are considered best for clearing lung heat, while younger solid roots (zi qin) are preferred for clearing damp-heat and stopping bleeding.

parts.typical_compounds: Flavonoids (lower concentrations of baicalin and wogonin), phenolic acids, tannins
parts.therapeutic_properties: Mild anti-inflammatory, antioxidant
parts.notes: Aerial parts are less commonly used than the root but appear in some folk preparations for upper respiratory complaints.

planet_associations.notes: Chinese Skullcap's cold, bitter, restrictive nature and its capacity to clear heat through elimination and drying align with Saturn's governance of contraction, purification through austerity, and the cooling of excess.
Its cooling, anti-inflammatory nature and its traditional use in calming the fetus and reducing agitation connect to the Moon's cooling, calming, and protective maternal influence.

zodiac_associations.notes: Chinese Skullcap's Saturnian cold austerity, disciplined approach to clearing excess, and its enduring, time-tested medicinal role resonate with Capricorn's practical, rigorous, and reliable nature.

Its Saturnian co-rulership and its broad-spectrum, systematic approach to clearing heat and dampness from multiple organ systems align with Aquarius's themes of systemic reform and detached, intellectual healing.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Baicalin and wogonin inhibit NF-kB, COX-2, 5-LOX, and multiple inflammatory cytokines with potency comparable to some pharmaceutical anti-inflammatories.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 6-15 grams dried root decocted for 20-30 minutes, taken 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Baicalein stabilizes mast cells and inhibits histamine release, while wogonin modulates IgE-mediated allergic responses.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 mL of 1:3 tincture taken 2-3 times daily during allergy season.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Clears lung heat and damp-phlegm, reducing cough, thick yellow sputum, and upper respiratory bacterial and viral infections.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 9-15 grams root decocted in classical formulas such as Xiao Chai Hu Tang.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Baicalin and wogonin bind to GABA-A receptors at the benzodiazepine site, producing anxiolytic effects without significant sedation or dependence.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 250-500 mg standardized extract taken 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Baicalin inhibits multiple inflammatory pathways and provides antioxidant protection to joint tissues.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-4 ml tincture or 250-500 mg extract, 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Baicalin inhibits multiple inflammatory pathways involved in autoimmune activation.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-4 ml tincture or 250-500 mg extract, 2-3 times daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Baicalin and baicalein exhibit antiproliferative effects and may interfere with trophoblast development. Skullcap has traditional use as a uterine stimulant in Chinese medicine.
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during pregnancy. Some traditional formulas use it for threatened miscarriage only under expert supervision.

contraindication_associations.reason: Adulteration with germander (Teucrium) species has caused documented cases of severe hepatotoxicity, and some skullcap flavonoids undergo hepatic bioactivation to reactive quinone metabolites.
contraindication_associations.notes: Source only from reputable suppliers with verified identity testing. Monitor liver function during long-term use.

contraindication_associations.reason: Baicalin enhances insulin sensitivity by activating PPAR-gamma receptors and improves glucose tolerance, which may potentiate hypoglycemia with antidiabetic medications.
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor blood glucose when using alongside diabetes medications. Adjust doses as needed.

contraindication_associations.reason: The bitter flavonoids in Chinese skullcap stimulate gastric acid secretion via bitter taste receptor (TAS2R) activation, potentially aggravating active peptic ulcers.
contraindication_associations.notes: Take with food to minimize gastric irritation. Avoid during active ulcer flares.

description: A stately deciduous tree whose thick, aromatic bark has been one of the most valued substances in Traditional Chinese Medicine for over two thousand years. Magnolia bark calms the spirit and descends rebellious qi, addressing the twin plagues of anxiety and digestive bloating with equal authority.
habitat: Mountain forests and valleys at moderate elevations in humid subtropical regions. Prefers deep, fertile, well-drained soils with consistent moisture.
energetic_quality: Warming, drying, descending. Moves stagnant qi downward while calming the shen and settling the middle burner.
doctrine_of_signatures: The thick, layered bark peels away to reveal aromatic inner layers, suggesting the plant's capacity to strip away layers of tension and agitation to reach a calm center beneath.
safety_notes: Avoid during pregnancy due to uterine-stimulating potential. May potentiate sedative medications and benzodiazepines. Not recommended for long-term continuous use without practitioner guidance. May lower blood pressure in sensitive individuals.

parts.typical_compounds: Magnolol, honokiol, volatile oils, alkaloids, tannins
parts.therapeutic_properties: Anxiolytic, antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, digestive carminative, mild sedative
parts.notes: The primary medicinal part, traditionally harvested from trees at least fifteen years old. The bark is steamed, rolled, and dried. Used in decoctions, tinctures, and standardized extracts for anxiety and digestive complaints.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Nasal decongestant, anti-inflammatory, mild anxiolytic
parts.notes: The flower buds (xin yi hua) are used specifically in TCM for sinus congestion and nasal obstruction. They carry a lighter, more ascending energy than the bark.

planet_associations.notes: Saturn governs structure, boundaries, and the containment of excess. Magnolia bark constrains agitation and returns scattered energy to its proper vessel, embodying Saturnian discipline.
The sedative and shen-calming properties align with the Moon's governance of sleep, emotional tides, and the subconscious.

zodiac_associations.notes: Capricorn's Saturnian nature resonates with magnolia bark's structured, disciplining effect on restless energy and scattered mind.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Honokiol and magnolol modulate GABA receptors, producing anxiolytic effects comparable to benzodiazepines without the addictive potential.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 30-60 drops of tincture, 2-3 times daily. Standardized extracts typically dosed at 200-400 mg daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Moves stagnant qi in the middle burner, relieving bloating, fullness, and distension. A cornerstone herb in TCM formulas for food stagnation.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-9 grams of dried bark simmered in 2 cups water for 20-30 minutes. Drink between meals.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Calms the shen and descends rising yang energy that disturbs sleep. Particularly effective for insomnia driven by racing thoughts or worry.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 40-60 drops of tincture taken 30-60 minutes before bed. May combine with passionflower or valerian.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Reduces cortisol levels and moderates the stress response, supporting resilience without sedation at lower doses.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 200-400 mg standardized bark extract daily, divided into two doses.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Honokiol and magnolol reduce anxiety, improve sleep quality, and modulate pain pathways.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 200-400 mg extract twice daily, or 2-3 ml tincture.

contraindication_associations.reason: Honokiol and magnolol cross the placental barrier in animal models and exhibit neurosedative effects via GABA-A receptor modulation that could adversely affect fetal neurological development.
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during pregnancy. The GABA-ergic effects may pose risks to fetal brain development.

contraindication_associations.reason: Honokiol potentiates GABA-A receptor activity and enhances sedation, which may cause excessive CNS depression when combined with benzodiazepines, barbiturates, or other psychotropic medications.
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid combining with sedatives, anxiolytics, or antipsychotics. May potentiate CNS depressant effects and impair alertness.

contraindication_associations.reason: Magnolol inhibits platelet aggregation by suppressing thromboxane B2 production and collagen-induced platelet activation, extending bleeding time.
contraindication_associations.notes: Discontinue before surgery. Use cautiously with anticoagulant or antiplatelet medications.

contraindication_associations.reason: Honokiol and magnolol are lipophilic compounds likely to concentrate in breast milk, and their GABA-A receptor-modulating effects may cause sedation in nursing infants.
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during breastfeeding due to risk of infant sedation and unknown long-term neurodevelopmental effects.

description: A vigorous twining vine whose tuberous root is one of the most legendary longevity tonics in Chinese medicine, named after a man said to have restored his gray hair and youthful vigor through its use. He Shou Wu nourishes the blood, replenishes jing essence, and darkens the hair, serving as a premier anti-aging tonic for the kidneys and liver.
habitat: Hillsides, forest margins, valley edges, and rocky slopes in subtropical to temperate regions. Thrives in well-drained soil with partial shade.
energetic_quality: Slightly warming, moistening, tonifying. Deeply nourishes yin and blood while gently astringeing jing essence to prevent its loss.
doctrine_of_signatures: The root grows in intertwined, human-like shapes suggesting the entanglement of vitality and longevity. Its dark reddish-brown color when properly prepared signals its blood-nourishing and kidney-tonifying nature.
safety_notes: Must be properly prepared (processed with black beans) to reduce potential hepatotoxicity; raw He Shou Wu has laxative effects and should not be used as a tonic. Cases of liver injury have been reported with prolonged use of improperly processed root. Avoid with pre-existing liver conditions. Not for use during acute illness with dampness or phlegm.

parts.typical_compounds: Emodin, chrysophanol, stilbene glycosides (2,3,5,4'-tetrahydroxystilbene-2-O-beta-D-glucoside), tannins, lecithin, anthraquinones
parts.therapeutic_properties: Blood tonic, jing restorative, anti-aging, hepatoprotective (when prepared), cholesterol-lowering, hair-darkening
parts.notes: The primary medicinal part. Must be distinguished between raw (sheng) and prepared (zhi) forms. Prepared He Shou Wu is steamed with black bean juice multiple times to transform its chemistry from laxative to deeply tonifying.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Calming, sedative, channel-unblocking, sleep-promoting
parts.notes: The vine stem (ye jiao teng or 'night-交-vine') is used as a separate medicine in TCM for insomnia and dream-disturbed sleep. It calms the spirit and nourishes the heart blood.

planet_associations.notes: Saturn rules longevity, time, and the slow accumulation of wisdom. He Shou Wu's legendary association with reversing aging and restoring vitality across decades aligns with Saturn's governance of the long arc of life.
Venus governs beauty, hair, and the nourishment of blood and yin. He Shou Wu's capacity to darken hair and restore youthful radiance reflects Venusian regeneration.

zodiac_associations.notes: Capricorn's association with endurance, discipline, and the mastery of time mirrors He Shou Wu's role as a premier longevity tonic that rewards patient, long-term use.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Deeply replenishes jing and nourishes kidney essence, addressing the root cause of chronic fatigue arising from constitutional depletion or aging.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 9-15 grams prepared (zhi) root simmered for 30-45 minutes. Take daily for extended periods under practitioner guidance.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Nourishes kidney yin and essence, supporting hormonal equilibrium particularly in conditions of premature aging, early graying, and declining reproductive vitality.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 9-15 grams prepared root in decoction, taken daily. Often combined with other kidney-tonifying herbs.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Nourishes liver blood and supports healthy cholesterol metabolism, gently moving stagnation through tonification rather than dispersal.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 500-1000 mg prepared root powder in capsules, twice daily with meals.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: The vine stem (ye jiao teng) calms the spirit and nourishes heart blood, addressing insomnia with vivid dreaming or restless sleep.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 9-15 grams of dried vine stem simmered for 20 minutes. Drink in the evening before bed.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Traditional liver and kidney tonic with hepatoprotective effects, supporting liver function and blood nourishment.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 6-12 g prepared root simmered 20-30 minutes, taken daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Anthraquinones (emodin, chrysophanol) and stilbene glycosides in raw He Shou Wu cause dose-dependent hepatocellular injury through mitochondrial dysfunction, oxidative stress, and bile acid transporter inhibition, with numerous documented cases of severe drug-induced liver injury.
contraindication_associations.notes: Strongly contraindicated in any liver condition. Even processed forms carry hepatotoxicity risk. Regular liver function monitoring is essential. Discontinue immediately if transaminases elevate.

contraindication_associations.reason: Emodin and other anthraquinones are mutagenic and genotoxic in vitro, and the significant hepatotoxicity risk poses unacceptable danger during pregnancy.
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during pregnancy. The teratogenic potential of anthraquinones and hepatotoxicity risk make this unsafe.

contraindication_associations.reason: Anthraquinone compounds in He Shou Wu cause direct irritation to the gastrointestinal mucosa and stimulate intestinal secretion, exacerbating ulcerative conditions.
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid in active peptic ulcer disease or inflammatory bowel conditions. The laxative effect may worsen GI symptoms.

contraindication_associations.reason: Emodin and its metabolites are nephrotoxic at high doses, causing tubular necrosis. Impaired renal clearance increases anthraquinone accumulation and toxicity risk.
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid in chronic kidney disease. Monitor renal function if use is deemed necessary.

description: A parasitic fungus that in nature colonizes insect larvae, transforming their bodies into fruiting stalks of extraordinary medicinal potency. Cordyceps has been revered in Tibetan and Chinese medicine for centuries as a supreme tonic for kidney yang, lung qi, and athletic endurance, earning its reputation as the bridge between the mineral and vital kingdoms.
habitat: High-altitude alpine meadows and grasslands, traditionally found at elevations above 3,500 meters in the Tibetan Plateau. Cultivated strains now grown on grain substrates in controlled environments.
energetic_quality: Warming, moistening, tonifying. Simultaneously nourishes yin and yang without being excessively hot, a rare and valued quality among tonic herbs.
doctrine_of_signatures: The transformation of dead matter into vital, upward-reaching fruiting bodies mirrors cordyceps' ability to convert depleted reserves into vigorous life force, turning exhaustion into endurance.
safety_notes: Generally well-tolerated. May interact with immunosuppressant drugs due to immune-modulating activity. Caution with anticoagulant medications as it may have mild blood-thinning effects. Individuals with autoimmune conditions should consult a practitioner before use.

parts.typical_compounds: Cordycepin, adenosine, polysaccharides (beta-glucans), mannitol, ergosterol, cordycepic acid
parts.therapeutic_properties: Adaptogenic, anti-fatigue, immunomodulating, kidney yang tonic, lung tonic, endurance-enhancing, anti-inflammatory
parts.notes: The entire fruiting body and mycelium are used medicinally. Traditionally simmered with duck or chicken in tonic soups. Modern preparations include hot water extracts, dual extracts, and powdered capsules.

parts.typical_compounds: Cordycepin, polysaccharides, proteins, amino acids, trace minerals
parts.therapeutic_properties: Nutritive tonic, qi-building, stamina-enhancing
parts.notes: Whole dried cordyceps can be eaten directly or added to soups and stews. The whole form provides the full spectrum of nutrients and medicinal compounds in their natural matrix.

planet_associations.notes: Mars governs vitality, physical endurance, and the yang fire of the body. Cordyceps' legendary ability to enhance athletic performance and sexual vitality resonates with Martian energy.
The Sun rules the vital force and core energy reserves. Cordyceps' capacity to replenish deep reserves of jing and qi mirrors the Sun's life-sustaining radiance.

zodiac_associations.notes: Aries embodies the pioneering, energetic drive that cordyceps ignites in the body, fueling endurance and physical courage.
Scorpio governs transformation and regeneration, mirroring the fungus's extraordinary life cycle of converting death into vital medicine.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Cordycepin and adenosine enhance cellular ATP production, directly combating fatigue at the mitochondrial level while tonifying kidney yang.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-6 grams dried fruiting body simmered for 30-40 minutes, or 1-3 grams powdered extract daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Tonifies lung qi and strengthens respiratory capacity. Traditionally used for chronic cough, asthma, and shortness of breath arising from kidney-lung deficiency.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-9 grams dried cordyceps simmered in water or broth. Take daily during convalescence or chronic respiratory weakness.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Warms kidney yang and replenishes jing essence, the foundational substance governing reproductive vitality and sexual energy.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-3 grams powdered extract daily in capsules, taken consistently for 4-8 weeks for full effect.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Beta-glucan polysaccharides modulate immune function, upregulating weakened immunity while calming overactive immune responses.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-6 grams daily as hot water extract or dual extract. Consistent use over weeks yields the best immune-modulating results.

contraindication_associations.reason: Cordycepin (3'-deoxyadenosine) is a nucleoside analogue that can interfere with DNA/RNA synthesis, posing theoretical mutagenic and teratogenic risks to the developing fetus.
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during pregnancy. The nucleoside analogue activity of cordycepin makes safety during fetal development uncertain.

contraindication_associations.reason: Cordyceps polysaccharides and cordycepin stimulate macrophage activation, enhance natural killer cell activity, and promote Th1 cytokine production, potentially worsening autoimmune tissue damage.
contraindication_associations.notes: Use with caution in autoimmune conditions. Monitor for disease flare-ups and consider immunological assessment.

contraindication_associations.reason: Cordycepin inhibits ADP-induced platelet aggregation and suppresses platelet-derived growth factor signaling, potentially prolonging bleeding time.
contraindication_associations.notes: Discontinue before surgical procedures. Monitor coagulation parameters with concurrent anticoagulant use.

contraindication_associations.reason: Cordyceps polysaccharides mimic insulin activity and enhance glucose transporter (GLUT4) translocation to cell membranes, potentially causing hypoglycemia with antidiabetic medications.
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor blood glucose when using alongside diabetes medications. Adjust doses as needed.

description: A spectacular white fungus that cascades in shaggy, icicle-like spines from hardwood trees, resembling a lion's mane or a frozen waterfall of mycelium. Lion's Mane has gained extraordinary scientific attention for its unique ability to stimulate nerve growth factor (NGF) production, making it a premier brain and nervous system tonic.
habitat: Dead and dying hardwood trees, particularly oak, walnut, and beech, in temperate forests. Found on fallen logs and wounds of living trees in late summer through autumn.
energetic_quality: Neutral to slightly warming, moistening, clarifying. Opens and nourishes the channels of cognition while supporting the stomach and spleen.
doctrine_of_signatures: The cascading, branching tendrils mirror the dendritic branching of neurons, directly signaling the plant's affinity for stimulating nerve growth and neural connectivity.
safety_notes: Generally very well-tolerated. Rare cases of allergic dermatitis reported. May slow blood clotting; use caution with anticoagulant medications. Individuals with mushroom allergies should avoid use.

parts.typical_compounds: Hericenones, erinacines, beta-glucan polysaccharides, ergosterol, polypeptides
parts.therapeutic_properties: Neurotrophic, neuroprotective, cognitive-enhancing, anti-inflammatory, gastroprotective, immunomodulating
parts.notes: Both the fruiting body and the mycelium contain active compounds, though their profiles differ. Hericenones are concentrated in the fruiting body while erinacines are found primarily in the mycelium. Dual extraction captures both water-soluble polysaccharides and alcohol-soluble terpenoids.

parts.typical_compounds: Polysaccharides, proteins, amino acids, dietary fiber, minerals
parts.therapeutic_properties: Nutritive, digestive tonic, prebiotic, mild cognitive support
parts.notes: Edible as a culinary mushroom with a lobster-like flavor and texture. Fresh or dried whole mushrooms provide nutritive value alongside medicinal benefits, making it an ideal functional food.

planet_associations.notes: Mercury governs the mind, communication, neural pathways, and cognitive function. Lion's Mane's unique ability to stimulate NGF and enhance mental clarity places it squarely under Mercury's rulership.

zodiac_associations.notes: Gemini, ruled by Mercury, governs the nervous system and mental agility. Lion's Mane's neurotrophic properties directly support the Geminian domain of thought and communication.
Virgo's association with digestion and analytical precision mirrors Lion's Mane's dual action on the gut and the brain, supporting the gut-brain axis.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Hericenones and erinacines cross the blood-brain barrier to stimulate NGF synthesis, supporting neuronal growth, myelination, and cognitive clarity.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-3 grams powdered extract or 3-5 grams dried mushroom in decoction daily. Best taken in the morning for cognitive support.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Promotes nerve regeneration and remyelination, addressing neuropathic pain at the structural level rather than merely masking symptoms.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 500-1000 mg extract standardized to hericenones, taken 2-3 times daily. Consistent use over 4-12 weeks for nerve regeneration.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Gastroprotective polysaccharides strengthen the mucosal lining and support healthy stomach tissue, with traditional use for gastric and duodenal ulcers.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Consume fresh or dried mushroom as food, or take 500 mg extract 2-3 times daily before meals.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Supports neuroplasticity and NGF production, with clinical studies demonstrating reduction in depression and anxiety scores after four weeks of supplementation.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-3 grams extract daily in divided doses. Allow 4-8 weeks for mood-related benefits to manifest.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Contains hericenones and erinacines that stimulate nerve growth factor (NGF) production, supporting myelin repair and neuroprotection.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 ml tincture or 500-1000 mg extract, 2-3 times daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Lion's mane contains hericenones and erinacines that stimulate nerve growth factor (NGF) synthesis, and the effects of exogenous NGF stimulation on fetal neurological development are unknown.
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during pregnancy due to insufficient safety data. The neurotrophic activity warrants caution during fetal development.

contraindication_associations.reason: Hericenones in lion's mane inhibit platelet aggregation by interfering with arachidonic acid-mediated platelet activation pathways.
contraindication_associations.notes: Use caution with anticoagulant or antiplatelet therapy. Discontinue before surgical procedures.

contraindication_associations.reason: Lion's mane can trigger allergic reactions in individuals sensitive to fungi, including contact dermatitis and respiratory hypersensitivity due to fungal protein allergens.
contraindication_associations.notes: Contraindicated in individuals with known mushroom allergies. Start with low doses and monitor for allergic reactions.

contraindication_associations.reason: Lion's mane polysaccharides (beta-glucans) stimulate dendritic cell maturation and enhance Th1 immune responses, which may exacerbate autoimmune disease activity.
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor for autoimmune flare-ups during use. Discuss with immunologist before starting supplementation.

description: A dense, charcoal-black conk that erupts from the trunks of birch trees like a piece of burnt earth, belying an interior of rich amber-orange tissue dense with melanin and antioxidants. Chaga is among the most potent antioxidant substances in the natural world, traditionally used across Siberia and Northern Asia as a deep immune tonic and cancer-preventive folk medicine.
habitat: Cold-climate birch forests in boreal and subarctic regions. Grows exclusively on living birch trees, forming a symbiotic relationship over ten to twenty years before harvest.
native_region: Siberia, Russia, Northern China, Korea, Japan
energetic_quality: Cooling, drying, consolidating. Draws scattered defensive energy inward and concentrates it, building deep immune reserves while quenching inflammatory heat.
doctrine_of_signatures: The black exterior and golden interior mirror the alchemical process of calcination, suggesting the transformation of gross matter into purified medicine. Its growth on birch, a tree of purification, reinforces its cleansing nature.
safety_notes: May lower blood sugar; monitor carefully with diabetes medications. High oxalate content may be contraindicated in kidney stone conditions. May interact with anticoagulant medications. Avoid with autoimmune conditions unless under practitioner guidance.

parts.typical_compounds: Melanin, betulinic acid (from birch), polysaccharides (beta-glucans), triterpenes (inotodiol, lanosterol), superoxide dismutase (SOD), polyphenols
parts.therapeutic_properties: Antioxidant, immunomodulating, anti-inflammatory, hepatoprotective, adaptogenic, potential anti-tumor
parts.notes: The sclerotium (hardened mass) is the medicinal part, not a true fruiting body. Requires hot water extraction to release beta-glucans and alcohol extraction for triterpenes. Dual extraction yields the fullest medicinal profile.

parts.typical_compounds: Melanin, polysaccharides, minerals (zinc, iron, manganese), B vitamins, dietary fiber
parts.therapeutic_properties: Nutritive tonic, antioxidant, mineralizing, gentle immune support
parts.notes: Ground whole chaga can be simmered as a tea with a mild, vanilla-coffee flavor. Provides broad-spectrum nutritive support alongside immune-modulating compounds.

planet_associations.notes: Saturn governs endurance, structure, and protection against decay. Chaga's extraordinarily slow growth over decades, its dense protective nature, and its deep consolidating immune effects embody Saturn's patient, fortifying medicine.

zodiac_associations.notes: Capricorn's Saturnian discipline and focus on long-term structural integrity mirrors chaga's slow-building, deeply consolidating approach to immune defense.
Aquarius, co-ruled by Saturn, governs the circulation and the broader collective immune intelligence that chaga's polysaccharides help orchestrate.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Beta-glucan polysaccharides powerfully modulate immune function, upregulating natural killer cells and macrophage activity while balancing overactive immune responses.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 grams dried chaga chunks simmered for 1-4 hours in low heat. Drink 1-3 cups daily. May reuse chunks for multiple decoctions.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Triterpenes and polyphenols suppress NF-kB inflammatory pathways while superoxide dismutase quenches oxidative stress at the cellular level.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-3 grams dual extract daily, or 2-3 cups of long-simmered chaga tea.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Polysaccharides improve insulin sensitivity and help regulate blood glucose levels, with several animal studies showing significant hypoglycemic effects.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-3 cups chaga decoction daily between meals. Monitor blood sugar closely if on diabetic medications.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Anti-inflammatory and mucosal-protective properties support healing of gastric tissue, with traditional use across Siberia for stomach ailments and ulcers.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 cups warm chaga decoction daily, taken between meals on an empty stomach.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Betulinic acid and polysaccharides demonstrate anti-proliferative effects on cancer cells and immune-enhancing properties.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 g dried chunks simmered 30 minutes, or 1-2 g extract daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Chaga's bioactive triterpenoids (betulinic acid, inotodiol) and oxalate content have not been evaluated for reproductive safety, and the potent immunomodulatory effects pose unpredictable risks during pregnancy.
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during pregnancy due to lack of safety data and unknown effects on fetal immune development.

contraindication_associations.reason: Chaga contains extremely high concentrations of oxalates (up to 14% dry weight), which can cause oxalate nephropathy, acute kidney injury, and accelerate renal decline in compromised kidneys.
contraindication_associations.notes: Contraindicated in kidney disease and history of oxalate kidney stones. A documented case of oxalate nephropathy exists from chronic chaga consumption.

contraindication_associations.reason: Chaga polysaccharides and triterpenoids inhibit platelet aggregation and may potentiate the effects of anticoagulant medications by modulating coagulation cascade factors.
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid combining with anticoagulant therapy. Discontinue before surgical procedures.

contraindication_associations.reason: Chaga beta-glucans are potent immunostimulants that activate macrophages, dendritic cells, and natural killer cells through Dectin-1 and TLR-2 receptor signaling, potentially intensifying autoimmune responses.
contraindication_associations.notes: Use with caution in autoimmune conditions. May counteract immunosuppressive therapy.

contraindication_associations.reason: Chaga polysaccharides lower blood glucose by enhancing hepatic glycogen synthesis and improving insulin receptor sensitivity, risking hypoglycemia with concurrent antidiabetic drugs.
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor blood glucose closely when using alongside diabetes medications. Dose adjustments may be required.

description: A strikingly beautiful bracket fungus that fans across fallen logs in concentric bands of brown, tan, blue, green, and white, resembling the spread tail feathers of a wild turkey. Turkey Tail is the most clinically studied medicinal mushroom on Earth, with its polysaccharide compounds PSK and PSP approved as adjunctive cancer therapies in Japan and China.
habitat: Dead and decaying hardwood logs and stumps in temperate and boreal forests worldwide. One of the most common bracket fungi, found year-round in diverse forest ecosystems.
energetic_quality: Slightly cooling, neutral moisture, consolidating. Strengthens the wei qi (defensive energy) and supports the body's capacity to distinguish self from non-self.
doctrine_of_signatures: The concentric, layered bands of color suggest the multiple layers of immune defense that turkey tail orchestrates. Its ubiquitous presence across all forest ecosystems reflects the universality and adaptability of healthy immune function.
safety_notes: Generally very safe and well-tolerated. Mild digestive upset possible when starting. May interact with immunosuppressive medications. Individuals with mushroom allergies should avoid use.

parts.typical_compounds: Polysaccharide-K (PSK/krestin), polysaccharopeptide (PSP), beta-glucans, ergosterol, triterpenes, phenolic compounds
parts.therapeutic_properties: Immunomodulating, prebiotic, anti-tumor adjunctive, antioxidant, hepatoprotective, antimicrobial
parts.notes: The tough fruiting body is not palatable eaten directly and requires hot water extraction to release its beta-glucans. PSK and PSP are the most studied compounds, used clinically as adjunctive immunotherapy in Asia.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Prebiotic, gentle immune support, digestive aid
parts.notes: Ground whole turkey tail provides prebiotic fiber that nourishes beneficial gut microbiota. The chitin content supports structural integrity of the digestive lining.

planet_associations.notes: Jupiter governs expansion, protection, and the abundance of life force. Turkey Tail's extraordinary capacity to amplify immune intelligence and its generous, widespread presence in nature align with Jupiter's beneficent rulership.

zodiac_associations.notes: Sagittarius, ruled by Jupiter, governs the liver and the body's innate wisdom. Turkey Tail's hepatoprotective and immune-educating properties support the Sagittarian expansion of the body's inner knowledge.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: PSK and PSP polysaccharides dramatically enhance natural killer cell activity, T-cell proliferation, and macrophage function, with extensive clinical evidence from oncology settings.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-6 grams dried mushroom simmered for 1-2 hours, or 1-3 grams concentrated extract daily. Clinically studied at 3 grams PSK daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Prebiotic polysaccharides nourish beneficial gut microbiota, improving digestive function and microbial diversity in the lower gastrointestinal tract.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 cups turkey tail decoction daily, or 1-2 grams extract with meals.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Rapidly upregulates innate immune defenses, helping the body mount a more effective response to viral and bacterial infections.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-6 grams simmered for 45-60 minutes at first sign of illness. Take 2-3 cups daily through the acute phase.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Modulates inflammatory cytokine production, helping resolve chronic low-grade inflammation that underlies many degenerative conditions.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-3 grams extract daily in capsules, taken consistently for 8-12 weeks for anti-inflammatory benefits.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: PSK and PSP polysaccharides enhance anti-cancer immune function. Used as adjunct therapy in Japan for cancer treatment.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 g dried mushroom simmered 30 minutes, or 1-3 g extract daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Insufficient safety data on Trametes versicolor polysaccharide-K (PSK) and polysaccharopeptide (PSP) during pregnancy; potential immunomodulatory effects may affect fetal development
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid supplemental doses during pregnancy; culinary use in small amounts has limited historical concern but caution is warranted

contraindication_associations.reason: Beta-glucans and polysaccharopeptides stimulate innate and adaptive immune pathways including NK cell and T-cell activation, which may exacerbate autoimmune flare-ups
contraindication_associations.notes: Individuals with lupus, rheumatoid arthritis, or multiple sclerosis should consult their immunologist before use

contraindication_associations.reason: Hepatic metabolism of bioactive polysaccharides may place additional burden on compromised liver function; isolated case reports of elevated liver enzymes with high-dose mushroom extracts
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor liver function tests if using concentrated extracts; standard culinary amounts are generally considered safe

description: A magnificent cluster mushroom that erupts in dense, overlapping fans from the base of oak trees, sometimes reaching enormous sizes that earned it the Japanese name 'dancing mushroom,' said to describe the joy of those who discovered it in the wild. Maitake uniquely combines culinary excellence with powerful immune-modulating and metabolic-balancing properties.
habitat: Base of hardwood trees, especially oaks, in temperate deciduous forests. Fruits in autumn and can return to the same tree for many years.
energetic_quality: Neutral to slightly cooling, moistening, harmonizing. Balances the middle burner while strengthening deep immune reserves without creating excess heat.
doctrine_of_signatures: The overlapping, petal-like fronds suggest layered protection and abundant generosity. The mushroom's massive size at the base of the oak, the tree of strength, signals its capacity to build foundational resilience.
safety_notes: Generally well-tolerated. May lower blood sugar; use caution with diabetes medications. May potentiate antihypertensive medications. Avoid with immunosuppressive therapy unless under medical supervision.

parts.typical_compounds: D-fraction (beta-glucan complex), MD-fraction, grifolan, ergosterol, lectins, polysaccharides
parts.therapeutic_properties: Immunomodulating, anti-tumor adjunctive, hypoglycemic, hypotensive, hepatoprotective, adaptogenic
parts.notes: The D-fraction and MD-fraction are proprietary beta-glucan extracts that are among the most studied compounds in medicinal mycology. Hot water extraction is essential for releasing beta-glucans from chitin cell walls.

parts.typical_compounds: Polysaccharides, proteins, B vitamins, vitamin D (when sun-exposed), minerals (potassium, phosphorus), dietary fiber
parts.therapeutic_properties: Nutritive, prebiotic, gentle immune support, metabolic balancing
