parts.notes: An excellent culinary mushroom with a rich, earthy flavor. Can be sauteed, added to soups, or dried and powdered. Sun-dried maitake produces significant amounts of vitamin D2.

planet_associations.notes: Jupiter governs abundance, expansion, and the generous proliferation of life force. Maitake's massive fruiting bodies, joyful name, and broadly protective immune-enhancing properties align with Jupiter's beneficence.
Venus governs nourishment and metabolic harmony. Maitake's capacity to balance blood sugar and blood pressure reflects Venusian equilibrium in the body's vital processes.

zodiac_associations.notes: Sagittarius, ruled by Jupiter, resonates with maitake's expansive, generous nature and its support of the liver's metabolic wisdom.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: D-fraction beta-glucans enhance dendritic cell maturation and natural killer cell activity, with clinical studies supporting adjunctive use in immune-compromised patients.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-7 grams dried mushroom simmered for 30-60 minutes, or standardized D-fraction extract as directed. Take daily for sustained immune support.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Alpha-glucosidase inhibition and enhanced insulin sensitivity help regulate postprandial blood glucose levels and improve glycemic control.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-3 grams extract daily in capsules, taken with meals. Monitor blood sugar levels closely if using diabetes medications.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Regular consumption supports healthy blood pressure through potassium content and vasodilatory compounds that relax arterial walls.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Consume 3-7 grams dried or equivalent fresh mushroom daily as food or in soups.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Polysaccharides modulate inflammatory signaling cascades, reducing chronic low-grade inflammation while preserving healthy immune vigilance.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-3 grams extract daily, or 2-3 cups maitake decoction, taken consistently for 8-12 weeks.

contraindication_associations.reason: Lack of controlled safety studies on Grifola frondosa D-fraction and other bioactive beta-glucans during pregnancy; immunomodulatory activity poses theoretical risk
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid concentrated extracts during pregnancy; small culinary amounts have traditional use but therapeutic doses are not recommended

contraindication_associations.reason: Maitake D-fraction and SX-fraction enhance insulin sensitivity and may potentiate hypoglycemic effects of diabetes medications including metformin, sulfonylureas, and exogenous insulin
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor blood glucose closely if combining with antidiabetic medications; dose adjustments may be necessary

contraindication_associations.reason: Maitake polysaccharides have demonstrated antiplatelet and mild anticoagulant activity in preclinical studies, increasing theoretical bleeding risk
contraindication_associations.notes: Discontinue at least two weeks before scheduled surgery; use caution with concurrent anticoagulant or antiplatelet therapy

description: An evergreen shrub whose tender young leaves, when minimally oxidized, produce the world's most widely consumed medicinal beverage, central to Asian culture and health practice for nearly five millennia. Green tea uniquely combines alert wakefulness with calm centeredness through the synergistic interplay of caffeine and L-theanine, a neurochemical balance that Zen monks have relied upon for centuries.
habitat: Subtropical and tropical highlands with acidic, well-drained soils, high humidity, and abundant rainfall. Thrives at elevations of 600-2000 meters where cool nights concentrate aromatic compounds.
energetic_quality: Cooling, slightly drying, clarifying. Clears heat and dampness while sharpening the mind and lifting the spirit without agitation.
doctrine_of_signatures: The fresh, vivid green color of the unoxidized leaves signals the preservation of vital, living energy. The careful, mindful preparation required for fine green tea mirrors the mental clarity it bestows.
safety_notes: Contains caffeine; sensitive individuals may experience insomnia, anxiety, or palpitations. May inhibit iron absorption if consumed with meals. High doses of concentrated extracts have been associated with rare liver toxicity. Avoid excessive consumption during pregnancy.

parts.typical_compounds: Catechins (EGCG, EGC, ECG, EC), L-theanine, caffeine, polyphenols, flavonoids, chlorophyll, theanine
parts.therapeutic_properties: Antioxidant, neuroprotective, thermogenic, cardioprotective, anti-inflammatory, cognitive-enhancing, chemopreventive
parts.notes: The youngest leaves and buds contain the highest concentrations of catechins and L-theanine. Shade-grown varieties (gyokuro, matcha) have elevated L-theanine levels. Water temperature and steeping time dramatically affect compound extraction.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Calming, mineralizing, mild digestive support
parts.notes: Tea stems (kukicha) contain higher L-theanine relative to caffeine, producing a calmer, more grounding beverage. Traditionally valued in Japanese macrobiotics for alkalizing and remineralizing properties.

planet_associations.notes: Mercury governs the mind, communication, and mental agility. Green tea's synergistic combination of caffeine and L-theanine produces the alert, focused, yet calm mental state that is the hallmark of Mercurial clarity.

zodiac_associations.notes: Gemini's mercurial nature of quick thinking, mental agility, and communicative ease is directly supported by green tea's cognitive-enhancing properties.
Virgo governs discernment, purification, and analytical precision. Green tea's cooling clarity and antioxidant purification align with Virgo's meticulous, health-conscious nature.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: The synergy of L-theanine and caffeine produces alpha brain wave activity associated with alert relaxation and enhanced focus, improving cognitive performance without jitteriness.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons high-quality loose leaf steeped at 70-80C for 1-3 minutes. Drink 2-4 cups daily for sustained cognitive benefits.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: EGCG potently inhibits NF-kB inflammatory pathways and reduces circulating inflammatory markers. Regular consumption is associated with reduced risk of chronic inflammatory diseases.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 cups daily, or 250-500 mg standardized EGCG extract.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Catechins improve endothelial function, promote nitric oxide production, and enhance arterial flexibility, supporting healthy blood flow and cardiovascular health.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 cups daily for cardiovascular benefits. Consistency of daily consumption is more important than high single doses.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Caffeine provides gentle stimulation while L-theanine prevents the crash and jitteriness of coffee, creating sustained energy without adrenal depletion.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-4 cups throughout the morning and early afternoon. Avoid after 2pm for sleep-sensitive individuals.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Catechins reduce LDL oxidation, improve endothelial function, and lower cardiovascular disease risk.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-3 cups daily, or 250-500 mg EGCG extract daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: EGCG inhibits aromatase, reduces estrogen receptor signaling, and provides chemopreventive effects in hormone-sensitive cancers.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 cups daily, or 250-500 mg EGCG extract daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Caffeine (30-50 mg per cup) crosses the placental barrier and may affect fetal heart rate; EGCG inhibits folate metabolism by blocking dihydrofolate reductase, potentially increasing risk of neural tube defects
contraindication_associations.notes: Limit to 1-2 cups per day during pregnancy; avoid concentrated green tea extract supplements entirely

contraindication_associations.reason: Caffeine content stimulates cardiac beta-adrenergic receptors, increasing heart rate and potentially triggering arrhythmias in susceptible individuals; catechins may interact with cardiac glycosides
contraindication_associations.notes: Limit caffeine intake from all sources; avoid concentrated extracts if taking antiarrhythmic medications or cardiac glycosides like digoxin

contraindication_associations.reason: Caffeine induces acute transient increases in blood pressure via sympathetic nervous system activation and adenosine receptor antagonism
contraindication_associations.notes: While long-term moderate consumption may have neutral or beneficial effects, acute intake can spike blood pressure; monitor response individually

contraindication_associations.reason: High-dose EGCG from concentrated green tea extracts has been linked to hepatotoxicity through mitochondrial membrane disruption and oxidative stress in hepatocytes
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid concentrated green tea extract supplements (especially those exceeding 800 mg EGCG per day); brewed tea in moderate amounts is generally considered safe

description: A robust, aromatic perennial mint-family member native to the subtropical hillsides of India, whose thick, tuberous roots contain forskolin, the only known natural compound that directly activates the enzyme adenylate cyclase. Coleus occupies a unique position in both Ayurvedic tradition and modern pharmacology as a plant that can directly modulate intracellular signaling cascades governing metabolism, circulation, and smooth muscle tone.
habitat: Dry, rocky hillsides and subtropical slopes at moderate elevations. Prefers warm, well-drained soils in seasonally dry tropical regions.
energetic_quality: Warming, drying, activating. Opens constricted channels and stimulates sluggish metabolic fires while moving stagnant blood and lymph.
doctrine_of_signatures: The thick, fleshy root system that stores energy deep underground signals the plant's capacity to activate deep metabolic reserves. Its membership in the aromatic mint family points to its ability to open and move stagnation.
safety_notes: Avoid during pregnancy. May potentiate blood pressure medications and anticoagulants. Use caution with heart medications as it increases cardiac contractility. May interact with drugs metabolized by cytochrome P450 enzymes. Low blood pressure patients should use cautiously.

parts.typical_compounds: Forskolin, diterpenoids, rosmarinic acid, volatile oils, flavonoids
parts.therapeutic_properties: Bronchodilator, vasodilator, positive inotrope, anti-inflammatory, thermogenic, anti-glaucoma, lipolytic
parts.notes: The tuberous root is the primary source of forskolin. Standardized extracts typically contain 10-20% forskolin. Used in Ayurveda for heart conditions, respiratory disorders, and urinary complaints.

parts.typical_compounds: Rosmarinic acid, volatile oils, flavonoids, diterpenoids (lower forskolin content)
parts.therapeutic_properties: Antioxidant, antimicrobial, mild bronchodilator, digestive carminative
parts.notes: Leaves contain less forskolin than the root but offer significant rosmarinic acid and aromatic volatile oils. Used as a culinary herb in some Indian regional cuisines and as a milder medicinal preparation.

planet_associations.notes: The Sun governs the heart, vital force, and metabolic fire. Coleus's direct action on cardiac contractility, cellular energy metabolism, and the activation of cAMP cascades aligns with solar governance of the body's central fire.
Mars governs muscular action, blood movement, and the breaking through of obstacles. Coleus's bronchodilatory and vasodilatory actions embody the Martian capacity to force open constricted pathways.

zodiac_associations.notes: Leo, ruled by the Sun, governs the heart and the central fire of vitality. Coleus's cardiotonic and metabolic-activating properties directly support Leo's domain.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Forskolin directly relaxes bronchial smooth muscle through cAMP activation, providing relief from asthmatic bronchoconstriction and improving airway function.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 250 mg standardized extract (10% forskolin) taken 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Vasodilatory action through cAMP-mediated smooth muscle relaxation reduces peripheral vascular resistance and lowers blood pressure.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 250 mg standardized extract (10% forskolin) twice daily. Monitor blood pressure and coordinate with prescribing physician.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Reduces intraocular pressure through increased aqueous humor outflow, with clinical studies supporting use as an adjunctive therapy for open-angle glaucoma.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 250 mg standardized extract twice daily for systemic support. Ophthalmic forskolin preparations also available under clinical supervision.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Positive inotropic action strengthens and regularizes cardiac contractions while the vasodilatory effect reduces cardiac workload, addressing palpitations of functional origin.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 20-40 drops tincture, 2-3 times daily. Use only under practitioner supervision for cardiac applications.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Forskolin activates adenylate cyclase, directly stimulating thyroid hormone production and release.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 50-100 mg extract standardized to 10% forskolin, 2-3 times daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Forskolin, the primary active diterpene in Coleus forskohlii, increases intracellular cyclic AMP which can stimulate uterine smooth muscle contractions and may induce miscarriage
contraindication_associations.notes: Strictly avoid during pregnancy due to uterotonic potential; this applies to all forskolin-containing supplements

contraindication_associations.reason: Forskolin activates adenylyl cyclase, increasing cardiac contractility (positive inotropic effect) and vasodilation, which may dangerously lower blood pressure or interact with cardiac medications
contraindication_associations.notes: Do not combine with antihypertensives, calcium channel blockers, or nitrates without cardiologist supervision; monitor blood pressure closely

contraindication_associations.reason: Forskolin inhibits platelet aggregation through cAMP-mediated suppression of thromboxane A2 synthesis and platelet activation factor
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid concurrent use with anticoagulants (warfarin, heparin) or antiplatelet drugs (aspirin, clopidogrel); discontinue before surgery

description: A fast-growing tropical tree whose leaves contain one of the most nutrient-dense profiles in the plant kingdom, earning it the name 'miracle tree' or 'tree of life' across the tropics. Moringa provides a complete array of essential amino acids, vitamins, and minerals from its leaves, while its seeds purify water and its roots carry a sharp, horseradish-like medicine.
habitat: Semi-arid tropical and subtropical lowlands. Thrives in sandy or loamy soils with good drainage, tolerating drought and poor soils with remarkable resilience.
energetic_quality: Slightly warming, drying, nourishing. Builds and replenishes depleted tissues while gently stimulating metabolism and clearing stagnation.
doctrine_of_signatures: The rapid, almost aggressive growth of the tree in depleted soils signals its capacity to build vitality from scarcity. The compound leaf structure, with many small leaflets, suggests the provision of many small but essential nutrients.
safety_notes: Leaves are generally very safe. Avoid bark and root preparations during pregnancy as they may stimulate uterine contractions. Root bark contains alkaloids with potential neurotoxicity at high doses. Seeds may lower blood pressure. May interact with thyroid medications and diabetes drugs.

parts.typical_compounds: Quercetin, chlorogenic acid, beta-carotene, vitamin C, iron, calcium, complete amino acids, isothiocyanates, flavonoids
parts.therapeutic_properties: Nutritive, galactagogue, anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, hypoglycemic, neuroprotective, antimicrobial
parts.notes: The most widely used part. Dried leaf powder retains most nutrients and can be added to foods, capsules, or teas. Contains all nine essential amino acids, making it a rare complete plant protein source.

parts.typical_compounds: Oleic acid, moringa oleifera coagulant protein, glucosinolates, isothiocyanates, phytosterols
parts.therapeutic_properties: Water-purifying, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, lipid-lowering
parts.notes: Seeds contain a natural coagulant protein that can purify turbid water, a remarkable survival property. The pressed oil (ben oil) is used in skincare and as a nutritive food oil.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Anti-inflammatory, analgesic, antimicrobial, circulatory stimulant
parts.notes: The root has a pungent, horseradish-like flavor and stronger medicinal action. Used in Ayurveda and folk medicine for circulatory and inflammatory conditions. Use cautiously due to alkaloid content.

planet_associations.notes: Jupiter governs abundance, nourishment, and the generous provision of life's essentials. Moringa's extraordinary nutrient density and its ability to thrive and give generously even in depleted conditions embody Jupiter's expansive beneficence.

zodiac_associations.notes: Sagittarius, ruled by Jupiter, embodies the optimistic, expansive life force that moringa represents, thriving across diverse tropical cultures as a universal provider of nourishment.
Cancer governs nourishment, mothering, and the stomach. Moringa's galactagogue properties and its role as a foundational nutritive food align with Cancer's nurturing domain.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Dense nutritional profile including iron, B vitamins, and complete amino acids addresses nutritional deficiency as a root cause of chronic fatigue, particularly in populations with limited dietary variety.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons (3-6 grams) dried leaf powder daily, added to food or taken in capsules.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Isothiocyanates and quercetin suppress inflammatory mediators including COX-2 and iNOS, reducing systemic inflammation at multiple signaling nodes.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-6 grams dried leaf powder daily, or 500 mg standardized extract twice daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Chlorogenic acid and isothiocyanates improve insulin sensitivity and reduce postprandial blood glucose spikes. Multiple clinical trials demonstrate significant glycemic improvements.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried leaf powder steeped as tea, or 3-6 grams powder with meals. Monitor blood sugar closely if on diabetes medications.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Rich phytonutrient profile supports hormonal equilibrium and thyroid function. Traditionally used as a galactagogue to support lactation in postpartum mothers.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-6 grams dried leaf powder daily. For lactation support, begin with lower doses and increase gradually.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Reduces blood glucose levels and provides dense nutrition to support overall metabolic health in T1D.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 g dried leaf powder daily with meals.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Clinical studies show moringa significantly increases breast milk volume. Rich in iron, calcium, and protein for maternal nutrition.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 500 mg-1 g dried leaf powder, 2-3 times daily with meals.

contraindication_associations.reason: Moringa oleifera root and bark contain alkaloids (moringine, moringinine) with documented uterotonic and abortifacient properties; even leaf extracts in high doses may stimulate uterine contractions
contraindication_associations.notes: Strictly avoid root and bark preparations during pregnancy; leaf consumption in food amounts may be acceptable in some traditional contexts but therapeutic doses should be avoided

contraindication_associations.reason: Isothiocyanates and chlorogenic acid in moringa leaves lower blood glucose by enhancing insulin secretion and improving cellular glucose uptake, risking hypoglycemia when combined with antidiabetic drugs
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor blood glucose levels carefully if taking alongside metformin, sulfonylureas, or insulin; adjust medication doses as needed

contraindication_associations.reason: High oxalate content in moringa leaves may contribute to calcium oxalate kidney stone formation; elevated mineral load may stress impaired renal filtration
contraindication_associations.notes: Those with existing kidney stones or reduced glomerular filtration rate should limit intake; ensure adequate hydration

description: The king of spices, a tropical climbing vine whose small, wrinkled, black drupes have driven global trade routes and shaped world history for millennia. Beyond its ubiquitous culinary role, black pepper is a profound bioenhancer that dramatically increases the bioavailability of other medicines, while its own piperine content stimulates digestive fire and clears cold, damp stagnation.
habitat: Tropical forests and plantations with warm, humid conditions, abundant rainfall, and partial shade. Climbs supporting trees or trellises to heights of four meters or more.
energetic_quality: Hot, drying, penetrating. Kindles agni (digestive fire), dispels cold and damp, and opens channels that have become blocked or sluggish.
doctrine_of_signatures: The small, hard, wrinkled black spheres concentrate intense heat within a compact form, signaling the plant's capacity to ignite metabolic fire from a tiny spark. The penetrating, sharp taste that reaches every corner of the palate mirrors piperine's ability to penetrate cellular barriers.
safety_notes: Generally safe in culinary quantities. Large supplemental doses may irritate the gastric mucosa. Significantly increases bioavailability of many pharmaceuticals; consult healthcare provider when using concentrated piperine supplements alongside medications. May increase absorption of drugs to potentially dangerous levels.

parts.typical_compounds: Piperine, piperyline, piperanine, volatile oils (beta-caryophyllene, limonene, alpha-pinene), chavicine, flavonoids
parts.therapeutic_properties: Bioenhancer, digestive stimulant, carminative, thermogenic, anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, expectorant
parts.notes: The unripe green drupes are harvested and dried until black and wrinkled. White pepper is the same fruit with the outer skin removed. Piperine content is typically 5-9% of the dried peppercorn by weight.

parts.typical_compounds: Volatile oils, flavonoids, alkaloids (lower piperine content), polyphenols
parts.therapeutic_properties: Mild analgesic, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial
parts.notes: Leaves are used in folk medicine as poultices for musculoskeletal pain and topically for skin conditions. Less commonly used than the fruit but carry a gentler version of the same warming medicine.

planet_associations.notes: Mars governs heat, sharpness, penetration, and the catalytic fire that initiates transformation. Black pepper's intensely hot, piercing quality and its capacity to break through barriers and enhance absorption are quintessentially Martian.

zodiac_associations.notes: Aries, ruled by Mars, embodies the initiating, fiery impulse that black pepper carries. Its capacity to kindle digestive fire and catalyze the action of other substances mirrors Aries's role as the spark of the zodiac.
Scorpio's transformative depth and penetrating insight align with piperine's ability to penetrate cellular membranes and transform the bioavailability of companion substances.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Piperine stimulates hydrochloric acid secretion, pancreatic enzymes, and intestinal motility, powerfully reigniting sluggish digestion and relieving bloating.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Freshly ground pepper with meals. For therapeutic use, 5-20 mg piperine daily in supplement form.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: The intense warming and expectorant action dispels cold and damp from the lungs and sinuses, while thermogenic properties help break fevers through diaphoresis.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 5-10 crushed peppercorns simmered with ginger and honey in hot water. Drink 2-3 times daily at onset of cold symptoms.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Piperine inhibits NF-kB and COX-2 inflammatory pathways. When combined with curcumin, it increases curcumin bioavailability by up to 2000%, dramatically enhancing anti-inflammatory outcomes.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 5-20 mg piperine daily, often taken alongside curcumin or other anti-inflammatory compounds to enhance their absorption.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Warming carminative action settles the stomach and dispels cold-type nausea associated with weak digestive fire and accumulated dampness.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 crushed peppercorns steeped in hot water with a squeeze of lemon. Sip slowly.

contraindication_associations.reason: Piperine in therapeutic doses may increase uterine contractions; additionally, piperine significantly enhances bioavailability of numerous drugs through CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein inhibition, complicating medication management during pregnancy
contraindication_associations.notes: Culinary amounts are generally safe; avoid concentrated piperine supplements during pregnancy due to drug interaction potential and uterotonic risk

contraindication_associations.reason: Piperine stimulates hydrochloric acid secretion by gastric parietal cells and may irritate compromised gastric mucosa, worsening existing ulceration
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid concentrated piperine supplements if active gastric or duodenal ulcers are present; moderate culinary use is generally tolerated

contraindication_associations.reason: Piperine potently inhibits hepatic cytochrome P450 enzymes (CYP3A4, CYP1A2, CYP2D6) and P-glycoprotein, drastically altering drug metabolism and increasing risk of drug toxicity in patients with impaired hepatic clearance
contraindication_associations.notes: Patients with liver disease taking medications metabolized by CYP enzymes should avoid supplemental piperine; inform prescribers of any black pepper extract use

description: An aromatic evergreen tree whose inner bark curls into the familiar quills that have perfumed kitchens and apothecaries since antiquity, once valued more highly than gold in the ancient spice trade. True cinnamon warms the body from the core outward, kindling digestive fire, improving insulin sensitivity, and circulating blood through cold, stagnant tissues with its sweet, penetrating heat.
habitat: Tropical lowland rainforests and coastal regions with warm, wet conditions and sandy soils. Cultivated extensively in plantations where trees are coppiced to produce thin, harvestable bark shoots.
energetic_quality: Hot, drying, stimulating. Warms the interior, disperses cold, moves blood, and kindles the yang fire of the kidneys and spleen.
doctrine_of_signatures: The bark's warm reddish-brown color and its tendency to curl inward upon drying suggest the warming, contracting, and circulating nature of its medicine. The sweet, fiery aroma emanating from the inner bark signals its capacity to warm the body's deepest interior.
safety_notes: True cinnamon (C. verum) is much safer than cassia cinnamon (C. cassia), which contains high levels of coumarin that can damage the liver with prolonged use. Avoid therapeutic doses during pregnancy due to uterine-stimulating potential. May lower blood sugar; monitor with diabetes medications. Large doses may irritate mucous membranes.

parts.typical_compounds: Cinnamaldehyde, eugenol, cinnamate, coumarin (trace in true cinnamon), polyphenols, proanthocyanidins, mucilage
parts.therapeutic_properties: Hypoglycemic, antimicrobial, carminative, circulatory stimulant, warming digestive, uterine stimulant, antioxidant
parts.notes: The inner bark is harvested from young coppiced shoots, peeled, and dried into quills. True cinnamon (verum) is distinguished from cassia by its paper-thin bark, lighter color, and vastly lower coumarin content.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Antimicrobial, analgesic, anti-inflammatory, dental antiseptic
parts.notes: Cinnamon leaf essential oil is dominated by eugenol rather than cinnamaldehyde, giving it a clove-like character. Used topically for pain and infection, and in dental preparations.

planet_associations.notes: The Sun governs warmth, vitality, and the central fire that sustains life. Cinnamon's warming, radiant quality that spreads from the center outward, its golden-brown color, and its association with prosperity and power align it with solar medicine.

zodiac_associations.notes: Leo, ruled by the Sun, governs the heart and the radiant warmth of the vital center. Cinnamon's warming, heart-opening, and circulatory properties resonate with Leo's generous, fiery nature.
Aries embodies the igniting spark and initiating warmth that cinnamon provides to cold, stagnant digestion and circulation.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Cinnamaldehyde and polyphenols enhance insulin receptor sensitivity and slow carbohydrate absorption, with multiple clinical trials demonstrating significant fasting glucose reductions.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 cinnamon sticks or 1 teaspoon ground bark steeped in hot water, 2-3 times daily with meals. Therapeutic dose: 1-6 grams daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Strongly warms the spleen and stomach, dispelling cold-type digestive stagnation characterized by bloating, poor appetite, and loose stools from yang deficiency.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon or one stick steeped in hot water after meals. May combine with ginger for enhanced warming effect.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Vasodilatory action of cinnamaldehyde improves peripheral blood flow, warming cold extremities and supporting cardiovascular function.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-3 cinnamon sticks simmered in 2 cups water for 15-20 minutes. Drink 1-2 cups daily, especially in cold weather.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Powerful antimicrobial and warming diaphoretic action helps the body expel wind-cold pathogens while the sweet warmth soothes sore throats and relieves chills.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 cinnamon sticks simmered with fresh ginger, honey, and lemon. Drink hot, 3-4 times daily during acute illness.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Warming aromatic that improves microcirculation and peripheral blood flow, particularly to the extremities.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1 cinnamon stick or 1/2 teaspoon powder steeped in hot water, 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Improves insulin sensitivity and reduces fasting blood glucose, addressing the metabolic insulin resistance central to PCOS.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 g cinnamon powder daily, or 500 mg extract twice daily with meals.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Improves insulin sensitivity and glucose uptake at the cellular level, reducing overall insulin requirements.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 g cinnamon daily with meals.

contraindication_associations.reason: Cinnamaldehyde may stimulate uterine contractions at high doses; coumarin content (especially in Cassia cinnamon) poses hepatotoxic risk that may affect maternal liver function during pregnancy
contraindication_associations.notes: Culinary amounts of cinnamon are considered safe; avoid concentrated cinnamon supplements or high-dose Cassia cinnamon during pregnancy

contraindication_associations.reason: Cassia cinnamon (Cinnamomum cassia) contains high levels of coumarin (up to 1% by weight), which is hepatotoxic and can cause dose-dependent liver damage through depletion of glutathione in hepatocytes
contraindication_associations.notes: Use Ceylon cinnamon (Cinnamomum verum) which contains negligible coumarin; strictly avoid Cassia cinnamon supplements if liver disease is present

contraindication_associations.reason: Cinnamon polyphenols (type-A procyanidins) potentiate insulin signaling by activating insulin receptor kinase and inhibiting phosphotyrosine phosphatase, potentially causing hypoglycemia when combined with antidiabetic medications
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor blood glucose when using supplemental cinnamon alongside insulin or oral hypoglycemic agents; culinary amounts pose minimal risk

description: The queen of spices, a lush tropical perennial whose aromatic seed pods are among the world's most expensive and revered flavorings, treasured across Ayurvedic, Unani, and Traditional Chinese Medicine for millennia. Cardamom carries a uniquely uplifting, sweet-spicy warmth that opens the breath, settles the stomach, and clears the mind without the aggressive heat of its ginger-family relatives.
habitat: Shady, moist understory of tropical evergreen forests at elevations of 600-1500 meters. Requires consistent rainfall, humidity, and dappled light beneath the forest canopy.
native_region: India (Western Ghats), Sri Lanka, Southeast Asia
energetic_quality: Warming, slightly drying, ascending. Lifts and disperses stagnant energy upward and outward, opening the lungs and clarifying the mind while gently warming the digestive center.
doctrine_of_signatures: The tightly sealed green pod protects precious, aromatic seeds within, suggesting the plant's capacity to contain, protect, and then release concentrated aromatic medicine. The upward-growing habit of the flower stalks signals cardamom's ascending, opening quality.
safety_notes: Generally very safe in culinary and moderate therapeutic doses. Extremely high doses may trigger gallstone movement in susceptible individuals. Avoid large therapeutic doses during pregnancy. May interact with anticoagulant medications at high supplemental doses.

parts.typical_compounds: 1,8-cineole (eucalyptol), alpha-terpinyl acetate, linalool, limonene, borneol, volatile oils, flavonoids
parts.therapeutic_properties: Carminative, digestive stimulant, anti-emetic, bronchodilator, breath freshener, detoxifying (mitigates caffeine), mood-uplifting
parts.notes: The seed pods are harvested just before ripening and dried carefully to preserve the volatile oils. Green cardamom is preferred for medicinal use. Chewing whole pods after meals is a traditional digestive practice across South Asia and the Middle East.

parts.typical_compounds: Fixed oils, starch, protein, mucilage, minerals
parts.therapeutic_properties: Nutritive, mild demulcent, digestive
parts.notes: The seeds within the pods contain the highest concentration of volatile oils. Freshly crushed seeds release the most potent aromatics. The pod husk itself has milder properties and is sometimes used in decoctions.

planet_associations.notes: Venus governs beauty, harmony, pleasure, and the refinement of the senses. Cardamom's exquisite aroma, its title as 'queen of spices,' and its gentle, harmonizing warmth that pleases without aggression all place it under Venus's gracious rulership.
Mercury governs the lungs and breath. Cardamom's eucalyptol-rich volatile oils open the airways and clarify respiration, supporting Mercury's domain of breath and communication.

zodiac_associations.notes: Libra, ruled by Venus, governs balance, harmony, and aesthetic refinement. Cardamom's elegant, balanced warmth and its capacity to harmonize digestion and uplift the senses embody Libran equilibrium.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Volatile oils relax smooth muscle spasms in the GI tract, stimulate bile flow, and dispel gas, relieving bloating, cramping, and post-meal heaviness.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 crushed pods steeped in hot water for 10 minutes, or chew 1-2 whole pods after meals.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Anti-emetic action through both aromatic and direct gastric mechanisms. Traditionally one of the first remedies reached for in cases of morning sickness and travel nausea.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Chew 1-2 whole pods slowly, or sip cardamom tea made from 3-4 crushed pods in hot water.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: High 1,8-cineole content opens bronchial passages, thins mucus, and clears respiratory congestion while the warming quality dispels cold-damp conditions in the lungs.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 5-6 crushed pods simmered with ginger in 2 cups water for 15 minutes. Drink hot, 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Antimicrobial volatile oils combat oral bacteria, freshen breath, and support gum health. Traditional use as a post-meal breath freshener doubles as oral hygiene practice.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Chew 1-2 whole pods thoroughly after meals, allowing the aromatic oils to coat the oral cavity.

contraindication_associations.reason: Cardamom essential oil compounds (1,8-cineole, alpha-terpinyl acetate) may have mild emmenagogue properties at high doses, theoretically stimulating uterine activity
contraindication_associations.notes: Culinary amounts are considered safe during pregnancy; avoid concentrated cardamom essential oil or high-dose supplements

contraindication_associations.reason: Cardamom extracts have demonstrated antiplatelet activity in vitro by inhibiting thromboxane synthesis and platelet aggregation pathways
contraindication_associations.notes: Unlikely to pose significant risk at culinary doses; exercise caution with concentrated extracts if taking anticoagulant medications

contraindication_associations.reason: Cardamom belongs to the Zingiberaceae family and may cross-react in individuals with known allergies to ginger or turmeric; contact dermatitis from cardamom essential oil has been documented
contraindication_associations.notes: Individuals with known Zingiberaceae allergies should introduce cardamom cautiously; discontinue if dermatitis or respiratory symptoms occur

description: A slender, creeping tropical vine whose elongated, cone-shaped fruit spikes were the original 'pepper' of ancient Indian and Greek medicine, predating black pepper in the Western pharmacopoeia. Long pepper carries a more complex, sweeter heat than its cousin black pepper, and holds a supreme position in Ayurvedic medicine as pippali, a rejuvenative spice that enhances bioavailability, kindles digestive fire, and clears cold congestion from the lungs.
habitat: Tropical and subtropical forests with warm, humid conditions and partial shade. Creeps along the forest floor and climbs supports in lowland to mid-elevation tropical regions.
energetic_quality: Hot, drying, penetrating. Kindles agni with a sweeter, more sustaining heat than black pepper, and is considered rejuvenative (rasayana) rather than merely stimulating.
doctrine_of_signatures: The elongated, catkin-like fruit spike with its tightly packed drupes suggests concentrated, sustained heat that releases gradually, mirroring long pepper's slower-building but longer-lasting warmth compared to black pepper's sharp burst.
safety_notes: Avoid during pregnancy. Large doses may irritate the gastric mucosa. Significantly enhances bioavailability of pharmaceuticals through piperine content; coordinate with prescribing physician. May increase pitta (heat) in constitutionally hot individuals.

parts.typical_compounds: Piperine, piperlongumine, piperidine alkaloids, volatile oils, starch, resinous matter
parts.therapeutic_properties: Bioenhancer, digestive stimulant, expectorant, rejuvenative (rasayana), bronchodilator, aphrodisiac, anti-inflammatory
parts.notes: The dried unripe fruit spikes are the primary medicinal part. In Ayurveda, pippali is one of the three ingredients in trikatu (three pungents) alongside black pepper and ginger, a foundational formula for kindling digestive fire.

parts.typical_compounds: Piperine, piperlongumine, sesamin, alkaloids, volatile oils
parts.therapeutic_properties: Analgesic, anti-inflammatory, hepatoprotective, deeper rejuvenative action
parts.notes: Long pepper root (pippalimula) is used as a separate medicine in Ayurveda with a deeper, more grounding action than the fruit. It supports the liver and is used in formulas for abdominal pain and liver conditions.

planet_associations.notes: Mars governs heat, penetration, and catalytic transformation. Long pepper's fiery, penetrating nature and its capacity to enhance the potency of other medicines through bioavailability enhancement embody Martian force.

The Sun governs vital force and rejuvenation. Long pepper's classification as a rasayana (rejuvenative) in Ayurveda, building sustained vitality rather than merely stimulating, connects it to solar nourishment.

zodiac_associations.notes: Aries embodies the initiating fire and catalytic heat that long pepper provides to sluggish digestion and cold, congested lungs.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Kindles agni (digestive fire) and enhances enzyme secretion, powerfully addressing cold-type digestive weakness characterized by bloating, low appetite, and sluggish metabolism.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 250-500 mg ground long pepper taken with honey before meals, or as part of trikatu formula (equal parts long pepper, black pepper, ginger).

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Powerfully expectorant and bronchodilatory, clearing cold, damp congestion from the lungs and sinuses. A primary Ayurvedic remedy for kapha-type asthma and chronic bronchitis.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 whole dried fruit spikes simmered with honey and ghee, or 250-500 mg powder mixed with honey, taken 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Intensely warming diaphoretic action breaks through cold, damp stagnation, stimulates immune response, and helps expel pathogens through sweat and expectoration.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-5 crushed fruit spikes steeped with ginger and tulsi in hot water. Drink hot, 3-4 times daily during acute illness.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Classified as vajikarana (aphrodisiac) in Ayurveda, long pepper warms the reproductive tissues and enhances vitality through sustained, rejuvenative heat rather than acute stimulation.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 250-500 mg ground long pepper mixed with warm milk and honey, taken nightly. Often combined with ashwagandha for enhanced effect.

contraindication_associations.reason: Piperlongumine and piperine alkaloids in Piper longum have demonstrated anti-implantation and abortifacient activity in animal studies through interference with progesterone signaling
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid therapeutic doses during pregnancy; long pepper has historical use as an emmenagogue in Ayurvedic medicine

contraindication_associations.reason: Piperine and piperlongumine stimulate gastric acid secretion and may irritate compromised gastric mucosa, potentially worsening peptic or duodenal ulcers
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid concentrated supplements if active ulcers are present; small culinary amounts are generally tolerated

contraindication_associations.reason: Piperine inhibits platelet aggregation via suppression of cyclooxygenase and thromboxane A2 pathways, increasing bleeding risk
contraindication_associations.notes: Use caution when combining with anticoagulant or antiplatelet medications; discontinue two weeks before planned surgery

description: A small, deciduous tree bearing translucent, pale green fruits that contain among the highest concentrations of vitamin C in the natural world, revered in Ayurveda as amalaki, the great rejuvenator and one of the three fruits in the legendary triphala formula. Amla uniquely nourishes all seven tissue layers (dhatus) in Ayurvedic understanding while balancing all three doshas, a nearly unparalleled breadth of action that has earned it the title 'nurse' or 'mother' among Indian medicinal plants.
habitat: Dry deciduous forests and mixed woodlands across tropical and subtropical lowlands to mid-elevations. Tolerates poor soils and seasonal drought once established.
energetic_quality: Cooling, moistening, nourishing. Uniquely balances all three doshas (vata, pitta, kapha) while deeply nourishing tissues and clearing excess heat without creating cold.
doctrine_of_signatures: The small, round, ribbed fruits cluster densely along the branches like jewels, suggesting the concentrated wealth of nutrients packed within each modest fruit. The sour-astringent-sweet-bitter-pungent taste profile spanning five of the six Ayurvedic tastes signals its ability to address virtually all bodily systems.
safety_notes: Generally very safe and well-tolerated. High vitamin C content may enhance iron absorption, which could be problematic in iron overload conditions. May potentiate blood-thinning medications. Extremely high doses may cause loose stools due to vitamin C content. Fresh fruit may be difficult to obtain outside of growing regions.

parts.typical_compounds: Ascorbic acid (vitamin C), ellagic acid, gallic acid, emblicanin A and B, phyllemblin, tannins (emblicanins), quercetin, rutin, mucilage, pectin
parts.therapeutic_properties: Antioxidant, rejuvenative (rasayana), immunomodulating, hepatoprotective, anti-inflammatory, hypoglycemic, hair and skin tonic, digestive
parts.notes: The fruit is the primary medicinal part, used fresh, dried, or as juice. It forms one-third of triphala, the most widely used Ayurvedic formula. The vitamin C in amla is remarkably heat-stable due to protective tannin complexes.

parts.typical_compounds: Fixed oils, phospholipids, linolenic acid, linoleic acid, phytosterols
parts.therapeutic_properties: Emollient, hair-strengthening, scalp-nourishing
parts.notes: Amla seed oil is cold-pressed and used extensively in Ayurvedic hair care to prevent graying, strengthen follicles, and nourish the scalp. Applied as a head massage oil.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Astringent, antimicrobial, wound-healing, antidiarrheal
parts.notes: The bark is occasionally used in traditional formulations for its strong astringent properties, particularly for diarrhea and wound healing, though it is far less commonly employed than the fruit.

planet_associations.notes: Venus governs beauty, nourishment, rejuvenation, and the tissues of the body. Amla's extraordinary capacity to nourish all seven tissue layers, restore youthful radiance to skin and hair, and function as a universal rasayana aligns with Venusian regenerative power.

The Moon governs nourishment, fluids, and cooling energy. Amla's cooling, moistening quality and its capacity to soothe pitta heat while deeply nourishing the body's fluid reserves connect it to lunar medicine.

zodiac_associations.notes: Taurus, ruled by Venus, governs sustenance, beauty, and the building of physical form. Amla's tissue-nourishing, beautifying, and deeply nutritive properties resonate with Taurus's earth-centered, generative nature.
Cancer governs nourishment, the stomach, and maternal care. Amla's role as the 'nurse' among plants and its digestive-supporting, deeply nurturing quality align with Cancer's maternal domain.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Extremely high vitamin C content and potent antioxidant tannins strengthen immune function at multiple levels, from enhancing white blood cell activity to protecting immune cells from oxidative damage.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 fresh fruits daily, or 3-6 grams dried amla powder. As triphala: 3-6 grams powder steeped in hot water before bed.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Nourishes rasa and rakta dhatus (plasma and blood tissues) from within, clearing pitta-type skin inflammation, supporting collagen synthesis through vitamin C, and restoring healthy complexion.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-6 grams dried amla powder daily internally. Externally, amla paste can be applied as a face mask.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Hepatoprotective tannins and antioxidants support liver detoxification pathways, reduce hepatic inflammation, and protect liver cells from oxidative damage.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 3-6 grams dried amla powder steeped in warm water, taken on an empty stomach in the morning.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Ellagic acid and emblicanins suppress COX and LOX inflammatory pathways while the cooling energetic quality directly counteracts pitta-type inflammatory heat.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 fresh fruits or 3-6 grams dried powder daily. Consistent long-term use provides the greatest anti-inflammatory benefit.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Powerful antioxidant that protects remaining beta cells and improves glycemic control in diabetic patients.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 500 mg-1 g amla powder daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Powerful hepatoprotective antioxidant that reduces liver enzyme elevation and supports liver tissue repair.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 500 mg-1 g amla powder daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Rich in vitamin C and bioflavonoids that strengthen capillary walls and support healthy blood clotting.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 500 mg-1 g amla powder daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: High vitamin C content (up to 600 mg per fruit) in concentrated supplemental doses may cause gastrointestinal distress; some Ayurvedic texts caution against high-dose use in early pregnancy
contraindication_associations.notes: Moderate dietary consumption is considered safe; avoid high-dose amla extract supplements during pregnancy without practitioner guidance

contraindication_associations.reason: Amla polyphenols (gallic acid, ellagic acid) and chromium content enhance insulin sensitivity and inhibit alpha-glucosidase, potentially causing hypoglycemia when combined with antidiabetic medications
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor blood glucose levels closely if taking alongside insulin or oral hypoglycemic agents; dose adjustments may be necessary

contraindication_associations.reason: High vitamin C content enhances absorption of anticoagulant medications and amla tannins have demonstrated antiplatelet activity, potentially increasing bleeding risk
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor INR if taking warfarin; avoid high-dose supplementation before surgical procedures

description: Frankincense is an aromatic oleo-gum resin harvested from trees of the Boswellia genus, prized for millennia in spiritual ceremonies and traditional medicine across the Middle East and North Africa. Its anti-inflammatory resin contains potent boswellic acids that have been extensively studied for joint health and respiratory support.
habitat: Dry, rocky hillsides and arid woodland regions with poor soil and limited rainfall.
energetic_quality: Warm and dry; elevating, purifying, and spiritually clarifying. Opens the crown and third eye, encouraging contemplative states.
doctrine_of_signatures: The golden, tear-shaped resin droplets weeping from wounded bark suggest the healing of grief and emotional wounds, while the ascending fragrance of the smoke points toward heavenly connection and prayer.
safety_notes: Generally well tolerated. May interact with anti-inflammatory and anticoagulant medications. Not recommended during pregnancy without supervision.

parts.typical_compounds: Boswellic acids (AKBA, KBA), incensole acetate, alpha-pinene, limonene
parts.therapeutic_properties: Potent anti-inflammatory, analgesic, expectorant, anxiolytic, and immunomodulatory.
parts.notes: The resin is the primary medicinal product, used as tears for burning, ground into capsules, or extracted into tinctures.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Mild astringent and anti-inflammatory; traditionally used in decoctions for digestive complaints.
parts.notes: Less commonly used than the resin but employed in some regional folk medicine traditions.

planet_associations.notes: Frankincense has been associated with the Sun since antiquity due to its golden color, warming nature, and use in solar devotional rites.

zodiac_associations.notes: As a solar herb, frankincense aligns with Leo's radiant, dignified, and heart-centered qualities.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Boswellic acids inhibit 5-lipoxygenase, reducing inflammatory mediators in osteoarthritis and rheumatoid conditions.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300-500 mg standardized extract (30-65% boswellic acids) taken 2-3 times daily with meals.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Systemic anti-inflammatory action through multiple pathways including leukotriene inhibition and NF-kB modulation.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 400-800 mg standardized extract daily in divided doses.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Traditional use for bronchial asthma and chronic bronchitis, supported by studies showing reduced bronchial inflammation.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300 mg extract three times daily, or inhale steam from resin dissolved in hot water.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Incensole acetate activates TRPV3 ion channels in the brain, producing anxiolytic and mildly antidepressant effects that facilitate contemplative practice.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Burn resin tears on charcoal during meditation or prayer; no standardized dose for this application.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Boswellic acids reduce neuroinflammation and may help protect myelin from immune-mediated degradation.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300-400 mg boswellia extract standardized to 60% boswellic acids, 3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Boswellic acids provide potent anti-inflammatory action, reducing the chronic pelvic inflammation of endometriosis.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300-400 mg boswellia extract, 3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Boswellic acids inhibit 5-lipoxygenase and reduce joint inflammation, with studies showing improved joint function in RA.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300-400 mg boswellia extract standardized to boswellic acids, 3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Boswellic acids reduce systemic inflammation and modulate autoimmune pathways relevant to lupus.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300-400 mg boswellia extract, 3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Boswellic acids reduce intestinal inflammation comparable to mesalamine in clinical trials for inflammatory bowel disease.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300-400 mg boswellia extract, 3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Boswellic acids reduce colonic inflammation with efficacy comparable to sulfasalazine in clinical trials.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300-400 mg boswellia extract, 3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Boswellic acids reduce joint inflammation and may help manage the autoimmune joint destruction of PsA.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300-400 mg boswellia extract, 3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Boswellic acids reduce autoimmune inflammation through 5-lipoxygenase inhibition and immune modulation.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 300-400 mg boswellia extract, 3 times daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Boswellic acids may stimulate uterine blood flow and contractions through leukotriene inhibition and prostaglandin modulation; insufficient safety data for supplemental use during pregnancy
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid oral frankincense supplements during pregnancy; topical use of diluted essential oil for aromatherapy is generally considered lower risk

contraindication_associations.reason: Boswellic acids modulate immune function by inhibiting 5-lipoxygenase and altering T-cell proliferation patterns, which may unpredictably affect autoimmune disease activity
contraindication_associations.notes: While often used as an anti-inflammatory, immune-modulating effects may be problematic in certain autoimmune conditions; consult rheumatologist before use

contraindication_associations.reason: Boswellic acids inhibit platelet aggregation and may potentiate the effects of anticoagulant medications through interference with arachidonic acid metabolism
contraindication_associations.notes: Discontinue frankincense supplements at least two weeks before surgery; monitor closely if taking warfarin or other anticoagulants

description: Myrrh is a dark, bitter resin harvested from thorny Commiphora trees native to the Horn of Africa and the Arabian Peninsula. Revered since ancient Egypt for embalming and purification, it remains a cornerstone antiseptic and wound-healing agent in traditional medicine.
habitat: Arid, rocky terrain and dry scrubland in semi-desert climates with minimal rainfall.
energetic_quality: Warm and dry with a descending, consolidating energy. Strongly astringent and protective; draws boundaries and seals wounds on physical and energetic levels.
doctrine_of_signatures: The dark, hardened resin that seals wounds in the tree's bark directly mirrors its capacity to close and protect wounds in human tissue, while its bitter taste signals detoxifying and purgative properties.
safety_notes: Avoid during pregnancy as it may stimulate uterine contractions. May lower blood sugar; use caution with diabetes medications. Large internal doses can be irritating to the kidneys.

parts.typical_compounds: Sesquiterpenes (furanoeudesma-1,3-diene, curzerene), commiphoric acids, eugenol, tannins
parts.therapeutic_properties: Antimicrobial, antifungal, astringent, vulnerary, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic.
parts.notes: The resin is the sole commercially significant medicinal part, used in tinctures, mouthwashes, capsules, and topical preparations.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Mildly astringent and antimicrobial; used in some folk traditions as a chewing stick for oral health.
parts.notes: The bark is of secondary importance compared to the resin.

planet_associations.notes: Myrrh's bitter, hot, and drying nature along with its wound-sealing and protective qualities align it with Mars and its martial, boundary-enforcing energy.
The dark color, preservative quality, and association with death rites connect myrrh to Saturn's domain of endings, boundaries, and consolidation.

zodiac_associations.notes: Myrrh's associations with death, transformation, purification, and deep healing resonate with Scorpio's themes of regeneration.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Topical application promotes tissue repair and prevents infection through broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Apply diluted tincture (1:5) directly to wounds 2-3 times daily, or use as a mouth rinse for oral lesions.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Astringent and antimicrobial properties reduce gingival inflammation, treat mouth ulcers, and combat oral pathogens.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Dilute 10-20 drops of tincture in water as a mouthwash, used 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Demonstrates antifungal activity against Candida species and dermatophytes in vitro and traditional use.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Apply diluted tincture topically to affected areas twice daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Sesquiterpene compounds modulate inflammatory pathways, particularly effective when combined with frankincense.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 200-500 mg powdered resin or standardized extract, 2-3 times daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Commiphora myrrha resin contains furanosesquiterpenes (curzerene, furanoeudesma-1,3-diene) that are potent uterine stimulants, historically documented as emmenagogues and abortifacients
contraindication_associations.notes: Strictly contraindicated during pregnancy in all forms including oral supplements, tinctures, and essential oil; even topical use should be avoided

contraindication_associations.reason: Myrrh resin constituents inhibit platelet aggregation and may potentiate anticoagulant drugs; guggulsterone-related compounds interfere with thromboxane synthesis
contraindication_associations.notes: Do not use alongside warfarin, heparin, or antiplatelet medications; discontinue well in advance of any surgical procedure

contraindication_associations.reason: Myrrh resin has demonstrated hypoglycemic activity in animal studies through enhancement of insulin secretion from pancreatic beta cells, risking additive hypoglycemia with diabetes medications
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor blood glucose if using myrrh supplements alongside antidiabetic medications; adjust doses as needed

contraindication_associations.reason: Myrrh resin contains terpenoid compounds that undergo renal excretion and may accumulate in patients with impaired kidney function, potentially causing nephrotoxic effects
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid high-dose or prolonged use in patients with reduced renal function; monitor kidney function markers if use is necessary

description: Cat's Claw is a large woody vine native to the Amazon rainforest, named for the curved thorns along its stem that resemble feline claws. It has been used for centuries by indigenous Amazonian peoples as a powerful immune-stimulating and anti-inflammatory medicine.
habitat: Tropical rainforest understory, climbing through the canopy in moist, humid lowland forests.
energetic_quality: Cool and dry; clearing and dispersing. Moves stagnation in the blood and lymph, opens blocked channels, and supports immune vigilance.
doctrine_of_signatures: The claw-like thorns that grip and climb suggest the plant's ability to grasp and hold onto health, while the vine's persistent, penetrating growth through dense forest mirrors its capacity to reach deep into the body to address chronic conditions.
safety_notes: Avoid during pregnancy and while trying to conceive. May interact with immunosuppressant drugs, anticoagulants, and antihypertensives. Not recommended for autoimmune conditions without professional guidance.

parts.typical_compounds: Oxindole alkaloids (isopteropodine, pteropodine), quinovic acid glycosides, procyanidins, tannins
parts.therapeutic_properties: Immunomodulatory, anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, antiviral, and antimutagenic.
parts.notes: Inner bark is the most commonly used part, prepared as decoctions or standardized extracts.

parts.typical_compounds: Oxindole alkaloids (higher concentration), tannins, quinovic acid glycosides
parts.therapeutic_properties: Strong immune stimulant and anti-inflammatory; traditionally considered more potent than bark.
parts.notes: Root bark harvesting is less sustainable than stem bark; many preparations now favor stem bark to protect wild populations.

planet_associations.notes: The expansive, vigorous growth of the vine and its immune-enhancing, protective qualities align with Jupiter's themes of abundance, protection, and vitality.

zodiac_associations.notes: Cat's Claw's Jupiterian nature and Amazonian origins connect it to Sagittarius and its associations with exploration, expansion, and resilience.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Oxindole alkaloids enhance phagocytosis and increase T-cell and natural killer cell activity.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 grams of bark simmered in water for 15-20 minutes, taken 1-3 times daily; or 250-350 mg standardized extract.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Quinovic acid glycosides and alkaloids inhibit TNF-alpha and NF-kB inflammatory signaling.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 250-350 mg standardized extract taken 1-3 times daily with food.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant actions reduce joint swelling and pain in osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Decoction of 1-2 grams bark, or 100 mg freeze-dried extract daily for joint support.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Anti-inflammatory and mucosal-protective properties help heal gastric and intestinal ulceration.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Mild decoction (0.5-1 gram bark) taken before meals, 2 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Oxindole alkaloids modulate immune function, helping to temper the autoimmune response without suppressing overall immunity.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 g dried bark simmered for 15 minutes, taken 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Pentacyclic oxindole alkaloids modulate TNF-alpha production and NF-kB pathway, reducing autoimmune joint destruction.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 g dried bark simmered 15 minutes, 2-3 times daily, or 60 mg standardized extract daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Immune-modulating alkaloids help regulate overactive immune responses characteristic of lupus.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 g dried bark simmered 15 minutes, 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating effects support intestinal healing and reduce inflammatory bowel symptoms.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 g dried bark simmered 15 minutes, 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Immune-modulating and anti-inflammatory effects support intestinal healing in inflammatory bowel disease.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 g dried bark simmered 15 minutes, 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Immune-modulating alkaloids help regulate the autoimmune inflammation affecting both skin and joints.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 g dried bark simmered 15 minutes, 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Pentacyclic oxindole alkaloids modulate TNF-alpha and NF-kB, helping temper autoimmune inflammatory cascades.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 g dried bark simmered 15 minutes, 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Immune-modulating and anti-proliferative effects support the body's anti-cancer defenses.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 g dried bark simmered 15 minutes, 2-3 times daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Uncaria tomentosa oxindole alkaloids (rhynchophylline, isopteropodine) have been traditionally used as a contraceptive and may stimulate uterine contractions, posing risk of miscarriage
contraindication_associations.notes: Strictly avoid during pregnancy and when trying to conceive; historical use as a contraceptive in indigenous Amazonian medicine underscores reproductive risk

contraindication_associations.reason: Cat's claw pentacyclic oxindole alkaloids potently stimulate immune function by enhancing phagocytosis, increasing T-helper cell proliferation, and modulating NF-kB signaling, which may exacerbate autoimmune disease activity
contraindication_associations.notes: Contraindicated in autoimmune conditions including lupus, multiple sclerosis, and rheumatoid arthritis; immune stimulation may trigger or worsen flare-ups

contraindication_associations.reason: Cat's claw inhibits platelet aggregation through suppression of thromboxane A2 and platelet-activating factor; may potentiate anticoagulant and antiplatelet medications
contraindication_associations.notes: Do not combine with warfarin, heparin, aspirin, or clopidogrel; discontinue at least two weeks before any surgical procedure

contraindication_associations.reason: Oxindole alkaloids undergo extensive hepatic metabolism via cytochrome P450 enzymes; may inhibit CYP3A4 and alter clearance of co-administered drugs in patients with hepatic impairment
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor liver function if using cat's claw with hepatically metabolized medications; reduce dose or avoid in significant liver impairment

description: Pau d'Arco is a majestic tropical hardwood tree known for its stunning pink-purple flowers that bloom before the leaves emerge each season. The inner bark has been used by indigenous South American peoples for centuries as a potent antifungal, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory remedy.
habitat: Tropical and subtropical forests, cerrado woodlands, and gallery forests across Central and South America.
energetic_quality: Cool and dry; strongly purifying and eliminative. Clears heat and dampness from the blood, supports detoxification pathways, and strengthens resistance.
doctrine_of_signatures: The incredibly dense, rot-resistant heartwood signals the tree's capacity to resist decay and infection, while the brilliant flowers emerging from bare branches symbolize renewal and vitality arising from purification.
safety_notes: High doses of lapachol may cause nausea and anticoagulant effects. Avoid during pregnancy. May interact with blood-thinning medications. Use standardized preparations to ensure safety.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Antifungal, antibacterial, antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and immune-stimulating.
parts.notes: Inner bark (phloem) is the primary medicinal part, typically prepared as decoctions or dried into capsules.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Mild antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties; less potent than bark.
parts.notes: Leaves are occasionally used in folk preparations but are considered significantly less active than inner bark.

planet_associations.notes: The intensely antimicrobial, aggressive action against pathogens and the dense, hard wood reflect Mars's combative and protective nature.

zodiac_associations.notes: Pau d'Arco's direct, forceful antimicrobial action and its pioneering role in immune defense connect it to Aries' bold, initiating energy.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Naphthoquinones (lapachol, beta-lapachone) demonstrate strong antifungal activity against Candida species and other fungi.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Simmer 2-3 teaspoons dried inner bark in 2 cups water for 20 minutes; drink 1-3 cups daily for 2-4 weeks.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Stimulates immune cell production and activity, supporting the body's natural defenses against opportunistic infections.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Standard bark decoction, 1-2 cups daily as a tonic during periods of immune vulnerability.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Naphthoquinones and flavonoids modulate inflammatory pathways and reduce oxidative stress.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 500-1000 mg dried bark powder in capsules, taken 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Traditional use against intestinal parasites, supported by in vitro studies showing antiparasitic activity of lapachol.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Strong decoction of 3 teaspoons bark in 2 cups water, taken twice daily for up to 2 weeks.

contraindication_associations.reason: Lapachol and beta-lapachone, primary naphthoquinone compounds in Tabebuia impetiginosa, have demonstrated embryotoxic and teratogenic effects in animal studies at therapeutic doses
contraindication_associations.notes: Strictly avoid during pregnancy; lapachol has shown fetal resorption effects in animal models

contraindication_associations.reason: Lapachol possesses documented anticoagulant activity resembling vitamin K antagonism, inhibiting vitamin K-dependent clotting factor synthesis and prolonging prothrombin time
contraindication_associations.notes: Do not use with warfarin or other vitamin K antagonists; monitor INR closely if pau d'arco use is unavoidable; discontinue before surgery

contraindication_associations.reason: Naphthoquinones (lapachol, beta-lapachone) can generate reactive oxygen species during hepatic metabolism, causing oxidative stress in already compromised hepatocytes
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid high doses in patients with liver disease; monitor liver enzymes during prolonged use

description: Yerba Mate is an evergreen holly tree whose leaves are dried and brewed into a stimulating, nutrient-rich beverage central to the social and cultural life of southern South America. It provides a balanced, sustained energy lift alongside a rich array of vitamins, minerals, and polyphenols.
habitat: Subtropical forests with red laterite soils, moderate rainfall, and partial shade in the understory.
energetic_quality: Warm and dry; stimulating yet grounding. Promotes alertness and mental clarity without the jittery peaks of coffee, and fosters communal connection through the ritual of sharing.
doctrine_of_signatures: The communal sharing of the gourd and bombilla (metal straw) among friends signals the plant's capacity to connect people, stimulate dialogue, and warm social bonds, while the persistent green leaves suggest enduring vitality.
safety_notes: Contains caffeine; avoid excessive consumption, especially in those sensitive to stimulants. Chronic consumption of very hot mate is associated with increased esophageal cancer risk. Moderate intake is generally safe for most adults.

parts.typical_compounds: Caffeine, theobromine, chlorogenic acid, saponins, rutin, quercetin
parts.therapeutic_properties: Central nervous system stimulant, thermogenic, antioxidant, diuretic, and cholesterol-lowering.
parts.notes: Leaves (and small stems) are the sole medicinal and beverage part, dried by smoke or air and then aged.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Milder stimulant effect; contributes minerals and smoothness to blends.
parts.notes: Stems are included in many commercial mate blends to mellow the flavor and reduce caffeine intensity.

planet_associations.notes: Yerba Mate's mental-stimulating, communicative, and socially connecting properties align it strongly with Mercury's domain of intellect and exchange.
The generosity of the shared mate circle and the plant's abundant nutritional profile connect it to Jupiter's expansive, beneficent nature.

zodiac_associations.notes: The social, mentally stimulating, and communicative nature of mate drinking aligns with Gemini's airy, talkative, and intellectually curious character.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Caffeine and theobromine provide sustained mental and physical energy without the crash associated with coffee.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Steep 2-3 grams of dried leaf in hot (not boiling) water; consume 2-4 cups daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Xanthine alkaloids combined with L-theanine-like calming saponins promote focused attention and mental acuity.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-3 cups of traditionally prepared mate throughout the morning.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Saponins and chlorogenic acid contribute to mild vasodilation and cholesterol reduction, supporting cardiovascular health.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Regular moderate consumption (2-3 cups daily); excessive intake may counteract benefits due to caffeine.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Bitter compounds and saponins stimulate bile flow and digestive secretions, relieving sluggish digestion.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: One cup after meals to promote digestion.

contraindication_associations.reason: Caffeine content (approximately 85 mg per cup) crosses the placenta and is associated with increased risk of low birth weight and miscarriage at high intake levels; polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons from traditional processing may be carcinogenic
contraindication_associations.notes: Limit to 1-2 cups per day during pregnancy to keep caffeine under 200 mg daily; choose unsmoked varieties to reduce PAH exposure

contraindication_associations.reason: Caffeine and theobromine content stimulate cardiac beta-adrenergic receptors, increasing heart rate and myocardial oxygen demand; may trigger atrial fibrillation or tachyarrhythmias in susceptible individuals
contraindication_associations.notes: Individuals with arrhythmias, heart failure, or coronary artery disease should limit or avoid yerba mate; monitor cardiac symptoms

contraindication_associations.reason: Caffeine induces acute blood pressure elevation through sympathetic nervous system activation, catecholamine release, and adenosine receptor blockade
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor blood pressure response; individuals with uncontrolled hypertension should limit intake or avoid entirely

contraindication_associations.reason: Caffeine can exacerbate anxiety disorders, panic disorder, and insomnia through central nervous system stimulation and adenosine A2A receptor antagonism; may interfere with psychiatric medications including lithium and MAOIs
contraindication_associations.notes: Individuals with anxiety, panic disorder, or bipolar disorder should limit or avoid yerba mate; consult psychiatrist regarding potential drug interactions

description: Guarana is a climbing plant of the Amazon basin whose seeds contain the highest natural concentration of caffeine found in any plant. The Guarani people have used it for centuries as a tonic for endurance, hunger suppression, and mental sharpness.
habitat: Tropical rainforest, often found climbing along riverbanks and in secondary growth areas of the Amazon basin.
energetic_quality: Hot and dry; intensely stimulating and activating. Drives energy upward and outward, sharpens the mind, and accelerates metabolic fire.
doctrine_of_signatures: The seed's remarkable resemblance to a human eye, with its dark pupil surrounded by a white aril, suggests its capacity to sharpen vision, enhance perception, and promote wakefulness and alertness.
safety_notes: High caffeine content requires caution in those with anxiety, insomnia, heart conditions, or caffeine sensitivity. Avoid combining with other stimulants. Not recommended for children or during pregnancy. May interact with stimulant medications.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Potent central nervous system stimulant, thermogenic, cognitive enhancer, appetite suppressant, and antioxidant.
parts.notes: Seeds are the primary medicinal part, traditionally ground into a paste with water and dried into sticks that are grated as needed.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Milder stimulant and antioxidant properties than seed.
parts.notes: Leaves are used in some traditional preparations but are far less common commercially than the seeds.

planet_associations.notes: Guarana's intense, fiery stimulant energy, its capacity to drive action and endurance, and its hot, activating nature strongly align with Mars.

zodiac_associations.notes: The explosive, fast-acting stimulant nature of guarana matches Aries' impulsive, energetic, and action-oriented character.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Very high caffeine content bound with tannins provides prolonged, steady energy release compared to coffee.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 50-200 mg guarana seed extract (standardized to 10-12% caffeine), 1-2 times daily. Start low.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Improves reaction time, attention, and memory performance through caffeine-mediated adenosine receptor antagonism.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 75-150 mg standardized extract in the morning or early afternoon.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Caffeine content produces cerebral vasoconstriction, providing relief from tension and vascular headaches.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 50-100 mg standardized extract at onset of headache; combine with analgesic herbs if needed.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Stimulant and vasodilatory actions improve peripheral blood flow and metabolic rate.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 75-150 mg standardized extract daily.
