contraindication_associations.notes: May mask ulcer pain due to antispasmodic effects while worsening underlying pathology; avoid in active peptic ulcer disease

description: Woad is best known in the West as the source of blue war paint used by ancient Britons, but in Traditional Chinese Medicine its root and leaves (known as Ban Lan Gen and Da Qing Ye) rank among the most important antiviral and fever-clearing herbs. It bridges two great herbal traditions with its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds.
habitat: Dry stony slopes, waste ground, roadsides, and cultivated fields in temperate regions with well-drained calcareous soils
native_region: Central and Western Asia, spreading throughout Europe
energetic_quality: Very cold and bitter; powerfully heat-clearing and toxin-resolving in Chinese medical terms
doctrine_of_signatures: The blue dye produced from its leaves connects to cooling and reducing heat and inflammation; the deep indigo color suggests its capacity to reach into the blood level to clear toxins and fever
safety_notes: Generally safe at recommended doses. Contains glucosinolates that may affect thyroid function with prolonged high-dose use. Avoid during pregnancy. Not recommended for those with cold, deficient digestive patterns in Chinese medical terms.

parts.typical_compounds: Indirubin, indigo, tryptanthrin, isatin, sinigrin, glucosinolates
parts.therapeutic_properties: Antiviral, antibacterial, antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, immune-stimulating
parts.notes: Known as Ban Lan Gen in TCM; the dried root is one of China's most widely consumed herbal medicines during cold and flu season

parts.typical_compounds: Indigo, indirubin, tryptanthrin, isatan B, flavonoids
parts.therapeutic_properties: Antiviral, heat-clearing, toxin-resolving, antipyretic
parts.notes: Known as Da Qing Ye in TCM; the leaves contain the indigo precursors used for dyeing and are a separate medicine from the root with overlapping but distinct properties

planet_associations.notes: The cold, bitter nature of Woad and its deep blue-black dye connect it to Saturn's cold, contracting, and boundary-setting qualities; Saturn governs the clearing of deep-seated chronic conditions
Woad's intensely cooling nature acts in opposition to Mars's heat; it is used to extinguish the fever, inflammation, and hot toxins that represent excessive Martial energy

zodiac_associations.notes: Saturn-ruled Aquarius governs circulation and the blood; Woad's action of clearing heat and toxins from the blood level aligns with this sign's domain

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Ban Lan Gen is one of China's most popular remedies during flu season; demonstrated antiviral activity against influenza, SARS-related coronaviruses, and other respiratory pathogens in vitro
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 9-15g dried root in decoction daily; commonly available as granules or prepared tea in Chinese pharmacies

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Indirubin and tryptanthrin inhibit inflammatory cytokines and have shown particular activity against inflammation of the throat and upper respiratory tract
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 9-15g root decocted for 20-30 minutes, taken in divided doses throughout the day

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Used in TCM for skin eruptions with heat signs including erysipelas, herpes, and eruptive febrile diseases
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 9-15g dried leaves decocted; can also be applied as a cooled wash for skin eruptions

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Stimulates innate immune responses while simultaneously controlling excessive inflammatory reactions; supports balanced immune function
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 9-15g root in decoction daily during vulnerable periods; often combined with Astragalus for deeper immune support

contraindication_associations.reason: Indirubin and indigo compounds have demonstrated anti-proliferative and cytotoxic activity that may harm developing fetal tissues
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during pregnancy; cytotoxic compounds pose risk to embryonic and fetal development

contraindication_associations.reason: Indigo and indirubin are hepatically metabolized and high doses have been associated with elevated transaminases and drug-induced liver injury
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid in patients with pre-existing hepatic impairment; monitor liver function tests during use

contraindication_associations.reason: Isatin derivatives may inhibit platelet function and prolong coagulation time, increasing hemorrhagic risk
contraindication_associations.notes: Use cautiously with anticoagulant medications; monitor coagulation parameters

contraindication_associations.reason: Indigo dye compounds are known contact sensitizers; ingestion may trigger systemic allergic reactions in sensitized individuals
contraindication_associations.notes: Perform patch test before topical application; discontinue if hypersensitivity reaction develops

description: Red Buckeye is a small understory tree native to the southeastern United States, producing striking clusters of red tubular flowers that attract hummingbirds and have been used in Native American and folk medicine traditions. While closely related to the European Horse Chestnut, it has its own distinct medicinal profile centered on its flowers' affinity for the circulatory system and emotional heart.
habitat: Rich bottomland forests, moist ravines, shaded stream banks, and woodland understories throughout the southeastern United States
energetic_quality: Cool and moist; gently astringent and tonifying to venous circulation with a calming influence on the emotional heart
doctrine_of_signatures: The vivid red flowers suggest an affinity for the blood and circulatory system, while the eye-like marking on the seed (the 'buck's eye') historically connected it to protection and clear sight
safety_notes: All parts of the plant contain aesculin and other saponins that are toxic if ingested in large quantities. Seeds are poisonous and must be properly processed before any use. Flower preparations are used in low doses. Not for use during pregnancy. Native Americans leached the toxins from seeds through extended water processing.

parts.typical_compounds: Flavonoids, quercetin, kaempferol, anthocyanins, aescin (low concentration)
parts.therapeutic_properties: Circulatory tonic, mild venotonic, emotionally calming, anti-inflammatory
parts.notes: The red flower clusters are the most safely used medicinal part; employed in flower essence traditions and low-dose herbal preparations

parts.therapeutic_properties: Astringent, venotonic, antipyretic, narcotic (in large doses)
parts.notes: Bark was used by various Native American peoples as a wash for sores and in small internal doses for various complaints; requires expert preparation

planet_associations.notes: Jupiter governs the arterial system and overall vitality; the expansive red flower clusters and the tree's generous canopy reflect Jupiterian qualities of abundance and circulation

zodiac_associations.notes: Jupiter-ruled Sagittarius connects to the circulatory and hepatic actions of Red Buckeye; the tree's forest habitat and spreading growth pattern echo Sagittarian expansiveness

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Flower essence and low-dose preparations support venous tone and circulation, particularly in the lower extremities
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Flower essence: 2-4 drops under tongue 3-4 times daily; tincture of flowers: 5-10 drops 2-3 times daily

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Calming influence on functional heart palpitations related to anxiety and emotional stress; works on the emotional dimension of cardiac disturbance
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Flower essence: 2-4 drops as needed during episodes; combine with deep breathing practices

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Used in flower essence therapy for deep emotional anxiety and fear, particularly related to vulnerability and feeling unprotected
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-4 drops flower essence under the tongue 3-4 times daily or as needed

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Astringent and venotonic properties help address edema related to poor venous return, particularly in the lower limbs
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Very low doses under practitioner guidance only due to toxicity of bark preparations; safer to use flower preparations for mild cases

contraindication_associations.reason: Contains esculin and aesculin glycosides that are directly cytotoxic and teratogenic; all parts of the plant are poisonous and unsafe during pregnancy
contraindication_associations.notes: Absolutely contraindicated during pregnancy; Red Buckeye is considered a poisonous plant and all parts should be avoided

contraindication_associations.reason: Esculin and saponin glycosides (aescin) are directly hepatotoxic, causing dose-dependent hepatocellular necrosis and cholestatic injury in compromised livers
contraindication_associations.notes: Strictly contraindicated in any degree of hepatic impairment; esculin toxicity may cause fatal liver failure

contraindication_associations.reason: Toxic glycosides are renally excreted; impaired clearance increases systemic toxicity and may cause direct nephrotoxic damage
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid in patients with any degree of renal impairment; toxic metabolites accumulate with reduced kidney function

contraindication_associations.reason: Aescin saponins inhibit platelet aggregation and damage capillary endothelium, significantly increasing hemorrhagic risk
contraindication_associations.notes: Contraindicated with anticoagulant therapy; toxic doses can cause hemolytic anemia and disseminated intravascular coagulation

contraindication_associations.reason: Saponin glycosides are severely irritating to gastrointestinal mucosa, causing mucosal erosion, hemorrhagic gastritis, and vomiting
contraindication_associations.notes: All parts of Red Buckeye are toxic when ingested; gastrointestinal damage is a primary symptom of poisoning

description: Linden, also called Lime Flower, is a majestic deciduous tree whose fragrant blossoms produce one of the most beloved and gentle herbal teas in European tradition, cherished for its calming, heart-soothing, and diaphoretic properties. It is the quintessential nervine relaxant for children and the elderly, combining absolute safety with genuine therapeutic effectiveness.
habitat: Mixed deciduous forests, parks, avenues, and village squares throughout Europe; thrives in deep, fertile, well-drained soils in temperate climates
energetic_quality: Cool and moist; deeply relaxing, softening, and soothing with a sweet, gentle quality that opens constricted tissues
doctrine_of_signatures: The heart-shaped leaves directly point to the plant's affinity for the emotional and physical heart; the wing-like bract attached to each flower cluster suggests the lifting and lightening of heavy, burdened spirits
safety_notes: One of the safest herbs available; suitable for children, the elderly, and during pregnancy. Very rarely, old or improperly stored flowers may develop mild narcotic properties. Excessive long-term consumption of very large quantities has been anecdotally linked to rare cardiac effects, but this is poorly documented.

parts.typical_compounds: Tiliroside, quercetin, kaempferol, chlorogenic acid, mucilage, volatile oils (farnesol, geraniol, linalool), tannins
parts.therapeutic_properties: Nervine relaxant, anxiolytic, diaphoretic, hypotensive, antispasmodic, demulcent
parts.notes: Flowers with their attached bracts are harvested shortly after opening when most fragrant; must be dried quickly and stored carefully to preserve medicinal quality

parts.therapeutic_properties: Demulcent, cholagogue, hepatoprotective, emollient
parts.notes: Inner bark (sapwood) is used in some European traditions as a gentle liver remedy and was historically prepared as a nutritive survival food

parts.therapeutic_properties: Mild diaphoretic, demulcent, nutritive
parts.notes: Young leaves are edible and mildly mucilaginous; less medicinally potent than flowers but contribute to overall tree medicine preparations

planet_associations.notes: Linden's sweetness, beauty, heart affinity, and gentle soothing nature make it profoundly Venusian; it was traditionally planted along lovers' lanes and associated with conjugal love
The tree's majestic stature, generous shade, longevity (Lindens live for centuries), and expansive canopy reflect Jupiterian benevolence and abundance

zodiac_associations.notes: Venus-ruled Libra governs harmony and relationship; Linden's association with love, partnership, and its harmonizing effect on the nervous and cardiovascular systems align with this sign
The deeply nurturing, maternal safety of Linden tea and its suitability for children and the vulnerable resonate with Cancer's protective, caring nature

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: One of the gentlest and most effective nervine relaxants; calms agitation, worry, and restlessness without sedating
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried flowers per cup, steeped covered 10-15 minutes; drink freely, 3-5 cups daily as needed

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Mild hypotensive action through relaxation of vascular smooth muscle; particularly effective for hypertension with a nervous or stress-related component
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-3 cups of strong infusion daily as part of a broader cardiovascular support protocol

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Classic European diaphoretic; promotes therapeutic sweating when taken hot at the onset of fever, helping to resolve acute infections
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Strong infusion (1 tablespoon per cup), drunk hot and frequently at the first sign of fever; combine with elderflower for enhanced effect

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Gentle enough for nightly use without habituation; especially suited for children's sleep difficulties and age-related sleep disturbances
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 cups of warm infusion 30-60 minutes before bed; safe for long-term nightly use

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Gentle nervine and antispasmodic that calms nighttime anxiety and promotes restful, undisturbed sleep.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried flowers per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Drink before bed.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Cardiovascular tonic that reduces blood pressure and supports healthy arterial function through mild vasodilation.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried flowers per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Drink 2-3 cups daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Insufficient safety data for medicinal doses during pregnancy; flavonoid glycosides may influence uterine smooth muscle tone
contraindication_associations.notes: Occasional use as tea is generally considered low risk, but concentrated extracts and medicinal doses should be avoided

contraindication_associations.reason: Tiliroside and other flavonoids have mild cardioactive and hypotensive properties that may potentiate cardiac medications and alter heart rhythm
contraindication_associations.notes: Use cautiously with cardiac glycosides, beta-blockers, or antiarrhythmic medications; monitor heart rate and blood pressure

contraindication_associations.reason: Linden flower tiliroside has demonstrated hypoglycemic activity in pharmacological studies, potentially enhancing the effect of antidiabetic medications
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor blood glucose if using linden tea regularly alongside diabetes medications

contraindication_associations.reason: Linden pollen and flowers contain allergenic proteins; individuals with tree pollen allergies may experience cross-reactive hypersensitivity
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid if known allergy to Tilia species or related tree pollens; discontinue if respiratory or skin symptoms develop

description: An Amazonian holly tree cultivated for millennia by the Kichwa people of Ecuador, who rise before dawn to brew its leaves as a communal tea for dream sharing and forest preparation. Guayusa provides smooth, sustained energy through a unique combination of caffeine, theobromine, and L-theanine that promotes alert focus without jitteriness. It is the night watchman's ally, sharpening awareness while keeping the spirit calm and connected.
habitat: Upper Amazon rainforest understory, cultivated in forest gardens (chakras) at elevations of 200-2000 meters in rich, well-drained soil.
energetic_quality: Stimulating yet centering, clarifying, socially connecting. Awakens the mind while keeping it rooted in calm awareness.
doctrine_of_signatures: As an evergreen that never drops its leaves, Guayusa suggests unwavering wakefulness and the persistence of consciousness. Its cultivation in forest gardens rather than monocultures reflects its medicine of community and interconnection.
safety_notes: Contains caffeine — avoid in caffeine-sensitive individuals or late in the day. Generally well-tolerated. Moderate consumption during pregnancy is debated as with all caffeinated beverages.

parts.typical_compounds: Caffeine, theobromine, L-theanine, chlorogenic acid, quercetin, catechins
parts.therapeutic_properties: Stimulant, antioxidant, nootropic, digestive aid, anti-inflammatory, mood elevator
parts.notes: Leaves are brewed as a tea, traditionally in large pots shared communally at dawn. Contains more caffeine than green tea but with a smoother profile due to theobromine and L-theanine content. Also prepared as cold brew or tincture.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Digestive tonic, astringent, mild stimulant
parts.notes: Bark is occasionally used in traditional preparations but is secondary to the leaf. Provides more grounding, tonifying qualities.

planet_associations.notes: Mercury governs communication, mental clarity, and alertness — the core qualities Guayusa promotes. Its traditional use for dawn dream-sharing circles reflects Mercury's gift for translating vision into language.
Jupiter rules expansion, community, and abundance. Guayusa's communal preparation and its role in fostering social connection reflect Jupiterian generosity.

zodiac_associations.notes: Gemini, ruled by Mercury, governs communication and mental agility — qualities enhanced by Guayusa's clean, focused stimulation.
Sagittarius rules the dawn, exploration, and communal wisdom traditions — reflecting Guayusa's role in pre-dawn dream-sharing ceremonies.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Provides sustained energy through synergistic caffeine-theobromine-L-theanine complex. Cleaner stimulation than coffee with less crash.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-3 teaspoons dried leaf per cup, steeped 5-10 minutes. Drink 1-3 cups daily, preferably in the morning and early afternoon.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: L-theanine combined with caffeine enhances focus, working memory, and cognitive performance. Traditional use for mental sharpness during forest navigation.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 cups strong infusion before mental work. Steep 7-10 minutes for maximum L-theanine extraction.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Bitter compounds and chlorogenic acid stimulate digestive secretions and promote healthy gut motility.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1 cup infusion after meals to support digestion.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Traditionally consumed before dawn to enhance dream recall and facilitate lucid dreaming. The Kichwa believe it allows communication with the spirit world through dreams.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1 strong cup before bed or upon waking in the early morning hours. Set dream intention before consumption.

contraindication_associations.reason: Contains significant caffeine which crosses the placental barrier and may affect fetal heart rate and development
contraindication_associations.notes: Limit to 1-2 cups daily during pregnancy, equivalent to moderate caffeine intake guidelines. Consult healthcare provider.

contraindication_associations.reason: Caffeine and theobromine are cardiac stimulants that may exacerbate arrhythmias, tachycardia, or hypertension
contraindication_associations.notes: Use cautiously in individuals with cardiac arrhythmias or uncontrolled hypertension; moderate consumption generally safe for mild conditions

contraindication_associations.reason: Caffeine may lower seizure threshold in susceptible individuals, particularly at higher doses
contraindication_associations.notes: Moderate consumption may be acceptable; discuss with neurologist if using anticonvulsant medications

description: A small Amazonian shrub whose root bark is prepared as potent eye drops by indigenous peoples of the Amazon for sharpening physical vision and opening spiritual sight. Sananga produces an intense burning sensation followed by remarkable clarity of vision, both physical and energetic. Used by hunters to enhance perception in the forest and by healers to diagnose and treat spiritual conditions, it is considered a powerful cleanser of panema (negative energy).
habitat: Understory of tropical rainforest, preferring shaded, humid conditions with rich organic soil in lowland Amazonia.
energetic_quality: Intensely clarifying, purifying, vision-sharpening. Burns away energetic fog and opens the inner eye to perceive truth.
doctrine_of_signatures: The milky latex of the plant suggests its affinity for the eyes and vision. The intense, tear-inducing experience of its application mirrors the cleansing power of tears themselves — a purification through temporary suffering.
safety_notes: Causes intense burning and tearing upon application. Must be prepared by experienced practitioners to ensure sterility and proper dilution. Risk of corneal damage if improperly prepared. Not for use with contact lenses. Contraindicated in glaucoma and eye infections.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Analgesic, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, vision-enhancing, energetic cleanser
parts.notes: Root bark is shaved and steeped in water to create the eye drop solution. Must be freshly prepared and strained through fine cloth. The alkaloid profile is related to ibogaine but present in much smaller quantities.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, fever-reducing, wound healing
parts.notes: Stem bark is used in poultices for wounds and as a decoction for fevers. Secondary to root bark for the primary eye medicine application.

planet_associations.notes: The Sun governs sight, clarity, and illumination. Sananga's ability to sharpen both physical and spiritual vision aligns with solar medicine of revelation and truth.
Mars rules the warrior's sharp perception and the courage to see difficult truths. The intense burning of Sananga reflects martial initiation through fire.

zodiac_associations.notes: Aries governs the head, eyes, and direct perception. Sananga's focus on the eyes and its bold, fiery application reflect Arian medicine.
Scorpio rules deep perception, the ability to see beyond surface appearances, and purification through intensity — the essence of Sananga's spiritual action.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Traditional use for improving visual acuity, treating cataracts, glaucoma (early stage), and general eye health. Anti-inflammatory alkaloids reduce ocular inflammation.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 drops per eye of freshly prepared dilute root bark infusion. Applied by experienced practitioner only. Used in sessions spaced 1-2 weeks apart.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Opens the third eye and cleanses panema (negative energy). Used before ayahuasca ceremonies and healing sessions to enhance spiritual perception.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Ceremonial application by trained practitioner, typically 1-2 drops per eye before ceremony.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Sharpens mental focus and perceptual clarity. Hunters traditionally use it to improve detection of game and forest navigation.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Applied before hunting or activities requiring heightened perception. Effects last several hours.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Coronaridine and related alkaloids have analgesic properties. Traditional reports of pain relief following Sananga sessions, particularly headaches and chronic pain conditions.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Ceremonial application. Pain relief effects may last hours to days after session.

contraindication_associations.reason: Improperly prepared solutions risk corneal damage, infection, or chemical burns; contraindicated in active eye infections, post-surgical eyes, and advanced glaucoma
contraindication_associations.notes: Only use preparations made by experienced practitioners with sterile technique. Never apply to infected or surgically compromised eyes.

contraindication_associations.reason: Ibogaine-related alkaloids may have teratogenic effects; the intense stress response from application may affect fetal wellbeing
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid during pregnancy; the intense pain response triggers significant adrenaline release

contraindication_associations.reason: The intense pain response causes significant sympathetic nervous system activation including tachycardia and blood pressure spikes
contraindication_associations.notes: Screen for cardiovascular conditions before application; the acute stress response may be dangerous for cardiac patients

description: A distinctive North American tree with mitten-shaped leaves and aromatic root bark that once flavored root beer and was a cornerstone of early colonial medicine. Sassafras was one of the first North American exports to Europe, prized as a blood purifier and spring tonic. Its warming, circulatory medicine helps clear stagnant conditions, particularly of the liver and blood, while its pleasant flavor makes it one of the most palatable woodland medicines.
habitat: Deciduous forests, woodland edges, abandoned fields, and disturbed areas of eastern North America. Prefers well-drained, sandy, acidic soils in partial shade.
energetic_quality: Warming, dispersing, cleansing. Moves stagnant blood and fluids, clears accumulated toxins, and renews vitality.
doctrine_of_signatures: The three distinct leaf shapes (ovate, bilobed, trilobed) on a single tree suggest adaptability and the plant's ability to address multiple body systems. The bright red-orange color of the inner bark and root signals its affinity for the blood.
safety_notes: Safrole, the primary aromatic compound, is classified as a potential carcinogen and is banned in food products by the FDA. Use in moderation and avoid long-term continuous use. Not recommended during pregnancy.

parts.typical_compounds: Safrole, camphor, eugenol, tannins, mucilage, resin
parts.therapeutic_properties: Blood purifier, diaphoretic, diuretic, anti-rheumatic, circulatory stimulant, carminative
parts.notes: Root bark is the primary medicinal part. Traditionally prepared as a spring tonic tea or decoction. The root bark is harvested in autumn or early spring when sap is concentrated. Use in moderation due to safrole content.

parts.typical_compounds: Mucilage, tannins, volatile oils (low safrole), flavonoids
parts.therapeutic_properties: Demulcent, thickening agent, nutritive, mild diuretic
parts.notes: Young leaves are dried and powdered to make filé powder, a traditional Cajun/Creole thickener and flavoring for gumbo. Much lower safrole content than root bark. Also used as a poultice for wounds.

planet_associations.notes: Jupiter rules the liver, blood purification, and expansion. Sassafras as a spring tonic reflects Jupiter's generous renewal and its role in clearing hepatic stagnation.
Mars governs the blood, heat, and circulatory drive. Sassafras' warming, blood-moving properties reflect martial circulatory medicine.

zodiac_associations.notes: Sagittarius, ruled by Jupiter, governs the liver and the impulse toward renewal and exploration. Sassafras embodies the Sagittarian spring tonic — expansive, cleansing, and vitalizing.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Traditional spring blood purifier and liver tonic. Stimulates hepatic function and promotes elimination of metabolic waste products accumulated over winter.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1 teaspoon dried root bark simmered in 1 cup water for 15-20 minutes. Drink 1 cup daily for 2-3 weeks in spring. Avoid continuous long-term use.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Warming volatile oils stimulate peripheral blood flow and help clear circulatory stagnation. Traditional remedy for cold hands and feet.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1 cup warm decoction 1-2 times daily during cold months.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Anti-rheumatic properties attributed to blood-cleansing and anti-inflammatory action. Traditional Appalachian remedy for arthritis and rheumatism.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1 cup decoction daily for 2-3 weeks. Combine with topical application of sassafras oil (diluted) to affected joints.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Diaphoretic action promotes sweating and helps break fevers. Warming properties help clear cold, damp conditions of respiratory infections.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1 cup warm decoction 2-3 times daily during acute cold or flu. Add honey and lemon for throat soothing.

contraindication_associations.reason: Safrole has shown embryotoxic effects in animal studies; emmenagogue properties may stimulate uterine contractions
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid all internal use during pregnancy and breastfeeding

contraindication_associations.reason: Safrole is hepatotoxic and potentially hepatocarcinogenic; compromised liver function increases risk of accumulation and damage
contraindication_associations.notes: Contraindicated in individuals with liver disease, hepatitis, or compromised liver function. Avoid concurrent use with hepatotoxic drugs.

contraindication_associations.reason: Diuretic properties may stress compromised kidneys; safrole metabolites are renally excreted
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid in individuals with kidney impairment; safrole metabolites may accumulate with reduced renal clearance

description: A gentle, cooling mint with a sweeter and less intense flavor than its hybrid offspring peppermint, Spearmint has been used as a digestive and calming herb across Mediterranean and Middle Eastern traditions for thousands of years. Recent research has revealed its remarkable anti-androgenic properties, making it valuable for hormonal balance in conditions of androgen excess. Its medicine is one of gentle cooling, soothing irritation and excess heat throughout the body.
habitat: Moist, rich soils along streams, garden beds, and disturbed areas. Naturalizes readily in temperate climates worldwide. Prefers partial shade to full sun.
energetic_quality: Cooling, soothing, gently dispersing. Calms excess heat and irritation without the intense cold of peppermint.
doctrine_of_signatures: The pointed, spear-shaped leaves (from which it derives its name) suggest precise, directed action. Its cool nature amid vigorous growth reflects the ability to bring calm without suppressing vitality.
safety_notes: Generally very safe, even for children and pregnant women in moderate tea doses. High-dose supplementation for hormonal effects should be monitored. May reduce iron absorption if consumed with meals.

parts.typical_compounds: Carvone, limonene, rosmarinic acid, flavonoids, menthol (trace), luteolin
parts.therapeutic_properties: Digestive carminative, anti-androgenic, cooling, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, mild nervine
parts.notes: Leaves are used fresh or dried as tea, tincture, or culinary herb. The tea is pleasant and widely consumed across Middle Eastern cultures. Clinical studies have demonstrated anti-androgenic effects with regular consumption.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Aromatic, mild digestive, nervine
parts.notes: Flowering tops are included in herbal tea blends for added aromatic and medicinal value.

planet_associations.notes: Venus governs beauty, hormonal balance, and the soothing of inflammation. Spearmint's anti-androgenic and skin-clearing properties reflect Venusian medicine of beauty and hormonal harmony.
Mercury rules the digestive system and communication. Spearmint's carminative properties and its classification in the Lamiaceae family reflect Mercurial digestive medicine.

zodiac_associations.notes: Libra, ruled by Venus, governs balance and harmony. Spearmint's hormonal balancing and gentle cooling properties reflect the Libran desire for equilibrium.
Virgo governs digestion, purification, and health maintenance. Spearmint's daily digestive tonic use reflects Virgoan attentiveness to bodily health.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Clinical trials show significant reduction in free testosterone and increase in LH and FSH in women with hyperandrogenism. Effective for hirsutism and hormonal acne.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2 cups spearmint tea daily (1-2 teaspoons dried leaf per cup, steeped 10 minutes). Consistent use for at least 1-3 months for hormonal effects.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Carminative oils relax smooth muscle of the digestive tract, relieving gas, bloating, and digestive discomfort. One of the gentlest digestive herbs, safe for all ages.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried leaf per cup, steeped 10 minutes. Drink after meals or as needed for digestive comfort.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Gentle anti-emetic and carminative action soothes nausea without the intensity of peppermint. Safe for pregnancy-related nausea.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1 cup tea sipped slowly during episodes of nausea. Can be consumed multiple times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Anti-androgenic effects help clear hormonal acne by reducing sebum production driven by excess androgens. Internal and external use both beneficial.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2 cups tea daily internally. Cooled tea can also be used as a facial rinse. Allow 1-3 months for skin clearing.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Clinical studies show spearmint tea significantly reduces free testosterone and increases LH and FSH, addressing the androgenic component of PCOS.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2 cups spearmint tea daily (1-2 teaspoons dried leaf per cup, steeped 10 minutes).

contraindication_associations.reason: High consumption may stress compromised kidneys through diuretic action and essential oil metabolism
contraindication_associations.notes: Moderate tea consumption (1-2 cups daily) generally safe; avoid concentrated supplements with kidney impairment

contraindication_associations.reason: Anti-androgenic effects may be undesirable in individuals with already low androgen levels, particularly men or postmenopausal women
contraindication_associations.notes: Men and individuals with low testosterone should avoid high-dose regular supplementation; moderate culinary use is fine

description: A naturally occurring hybrid of spearmint and watermint, Peppermint is one of the most widely used medicinal herbs in the world, with a sharp, cooling menthol flavor that awakens the senses and soothes the gut. Its essential oil is clinically proven for irritable bowel syndrome, tension headaches, and respiratory congestion. Peppermint's medicine is one of crisp clarity and immediate relief, cooling inflamed tissues and dispersing stagnant energy with brisk efficiency.
habitat: Moist, rich soils in garden cultivation, stream banks, and wet meadows worldwide. As a sterile hybrid, it spreads only vegetatively through runners.
energetic_quality: Strongly cooling, dispersing, clearing. Opens blocked channels and brings immediate relief to congested, overheated conditions.
doctrine_of_signatures: The intensely cool sensation upon tasting peppermint directly announces its cooling, anti-inflammatory medicine. Its vigorous spreading growth habit reflects how its medicine quickly disperses throughout the body.
safety_notes: Generally very safe as tea. Peppermint essential oil should not be applied near the face of infants or young children due to risk of breathing difficulties. May worsen GERD by relaxing the lower esophageal sphincter. Enteric-coated capsules recommended for IBS to bypass the stomach.

parts.typical_compounds: Menthol, menthone, rosmarinic acid, luteolin, eriocitrin, flavonoids
parts.therapeutic_properties: Carminative, antispasmodic, analgesic, decongestant, cooling anti-inflammatory, mild nervine, antimicrobial
parts.notes: Leaves are the primary medicinal part. Used as tea, tincture, essential oil (topical and internal enteric-coated capsules), and steam inhalation. One of the most clinically studied herbs with strong evidence for IBS and tension headaches.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Aromatic, mild antispasmodic, cooling
parts.notes: Flowering tops are harvested with leaves for maximum essential oil content. Included in herbal tea blends.

planet_associations.notes: Mercury rules the digestive system, communication between body systems, and mental clarity — all primary actions of peppermint. Its quick, dispersing action reflects Mercury's speed.
Venus governs soothing, cooling, and anti-inflammatory action. Peppermint's ability to cool inflamed tissues reflects Venusian medicine of comfort and relief.

zodiac_associations.notes: Virgo, ruled by Mercury, governs the intestines and digestive precision. Peppermint's IBS and digestive specificity embody Virgoan attention to gut health.
Gemini rules the lungs, nervous system, and rapid communication. Peppermint's respiratory and nervine actions reflect Geminian medicine.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Clinically proven antispasmodic action on smooth muscle of the GI tract. Relieves gas, bloating, cramps, and general digestive discomfort. First-line herbal remedy for IBS.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried leaf per cup, steeped 10 minutes covered. Drink after meals. For IBS: 0.2-0.4 ml enteric-coated peppermint oil capsules, 3 times daily before meals.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: 10% peppermint oil in ethanol applied to temples is clinically equivalent to acetaminophen for tension headaches. Menthol activates cold receptors and inhibits pain signaling.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Apply diluted peppermint essential oil (10% in carrier oil) to temples and forehead at onset of headache. Reapply after 15-30 minutes if needed.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Menthol vapors act as a decongestant and expectorant, opening airways and thinning mucus. Antimicrobial action helps combat respiratory infections.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Add 3-5 drops essential oil or a handful of fresh leaves to a bowl of steaming water. Inhale with towel over head for 5-10 minutes, 2-3 times daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Antispasmodic and carminative action relieves nausea. Peppermint aromatherapy studied for post-operative nausea with positive results.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1 cup peppermint tea sipped slowly. Inhaling peppermint essential oil from a tissue also effective for acute nausea.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Antispasmodic and carminative actions relieve abdominal cramping, bloating, and intestinal discomfort common in celiac disease.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried leaf per cup, steeped 10 minutes. Drink 2-3 cups daily between meals.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Antispasmodic action relieves intestinal cramping and reduces abdominal pain associated with Crohn's flares.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried leaf per cup, steeped 10 minutes. Drink 2-3 cups daily between meals.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Enteric-coated peppermint oil capsules are clinically proven to reduce IBS symptoms including pain, bloating, and urgency.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 0.2-0.4 ml enteric-coated peppermint oil capsules, 2-3 times daily before meals.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Relieves pregnancy-related nausea and digestive discomfort. Generally considered safe in moderate amounts during pregnancy.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1 teaspoon dried leaf per cup, steeped 10 minutes. Drink 1-2 cups daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Peppermint oil in concentrated form may relax the uterine muscles; menthol in large doses has emmenagogue properties
contraindication_associations.notes: Peppermint tea in moderate amounts is generally considered safe during pregnancy; avoid concentrated essential oil internally

contraindication_associations.reason: Menthol may reduce milk supply; peppermint has been traditionally used to help with weaning by decreasing lactation
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid regular peppermint tea consumption while establishing or maintaining breastfeeding. Occasional use is unlikely to be problematic.

contraindication_associations.reason: Menthol is metabolized hepatically; compromised liver function may lead to accumulation of volatile oil compounds
contraindication_associations.notes: Avoid peppermint oil capsules with liver impairment; mild tea use generally acceptable

description: One of the most prized medicines in traditional Chinese and Tibetan pharmacology, Cordyceps is an extraordinary parasitic fungus that colonizes caterpillar larvae high in the Tibetan Plateau. It dramatically enhances oxygen utilization, ATP production, and endurance, making it legendary among athletes and those seeking to restore depleted vitality. Its medicine bridges the animal and fungal kingdoms, transforming the caterpillar's vital essence into concentrated energy medicine for the lungs and kidneys.
habitat: Alpine meadows and grasslands of the Tibetan Plateau and Himalayan highlands above 3,500 meters. Wild specimens grow on ghost moth caterpillar larvae. Cultivated forms (Cordyceps militaris) grow on grain substrates.
energetic_quality: Warming, vitalizing, tonifying. Deeply restores depleted kidney and lung energy, rekindling the foundational fire of vitality.
doctrine_of_signatures: The transformation of caterpillar into fungus embodies the medicine of alchemical transformation — converting one form of vital energy into another. Its growth at extreme altitude reflects its capacity to enhance oxygen utilization and high-altitude performance.
safety_notes: Generally safe in recommended doses. Wild cordyceps is extremely expensive and often adulterated. Cultivated Cordyceps militaris is a sustainable and clinically validated alternative. May interact with immunosuppressive and anticoagulant medications.

parts.typical_compounds: Cordycepin, adenosine, beta-glucans, polysaccharides, sterols, cordycepic acid (D-mannitol)
parts.therapeutic_properties: Adaptogenic, energy-enhancing, lung tonic, kidney tonic, anti-fatigue, immune modulating, anti-inflammatory, libido-enhancing
parts.notes: The entire stroma-caterpillar complex is used traditionally. Cultivated Cordyceps militaris fruiting bodies are the sustainable modern alternative with comparable or superior cordycepin content. Hot water or dual extraction recommended.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Energy-enhancing, immune-modulating, adaptogenic
parts.notes: Mycelium culture (CS-4 strain) has been extensively studied and provides a more affordable and sustainable option than wild-harvested specimens.

planet_associations.notes: Mars rules physical vitality, endurance, and the active fire of the body. Cordyceps' ability to dramatically enhance energy, oxygen utilization, and athletic performance reflects martial medicine.
The Sun governs core vitality, the heart, and sovereign energy. Cordyceps' deep tonification of the body's fundamental energy reserves reflects solar medicine.

zodiac_associations.notes: Aries, ruled by Mars, governs physical energy, courage, and the warrior's stamina. Cordyceps' energy-enhancing and endurance-promoting effects embody Arian vitality.
Capricorn governs endurance, structure, and kidney vitality in traditional Chinese medicine. Cordyceps' kidney-tonifying and stamina-building properties reflect Capricornian medicine.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Enhances cellular ATP production and oxygen utilization. Clinical studies show significant improvement in exercise tolerance, VO2 max, and subjective energy levels.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-3g daily of Cordyceps militaris powder or extract. For athletic performance: 3-6g daily. Allow 1-2 weeks for effects to build.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Traditional lung tonic in Chinese medicine. Improves oxygen absorption and utilization. Clinically studied for COPD, asthma, and respiratory function improvement.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-3g daily as decoction or capsule. For chronic respiratory conditions: consistent use for 3-6 months.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Traditional kidney yang tonic in Chinese medicine. Clinical studies demonstrate improved sexual function and testosterone support in both men and women.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-3g daily. For libido enhancement: consistent use for 4-8 weeks. Traditionally combined with other kidney-tonifying herbs.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Beta-glucans and cordycepin modulate immune function, enhancing NK cell activity and adaptive immune response.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 2-4 ml dual-extracted tincture twice daily. Consistent use for immune building.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Enhances mitochondrial ATP production and oxygen utilization, directly addressing the energy deficit in CFS.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-3 g dried powder or 500-1000 mg extract daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Enhances adrenal function and mitochondrial energy production, rebuilding depleted energy reserves.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-3 g dried powder or 500-1000 mg extract daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Immunomodulatory polysaccharides help rebalance immune function and reduce autoimmune-driven inflammation.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-3 g dried powder or 500-1000 mg extract daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Clinical trials demonstrate improved kidney function, reduced proteinuria, and increased creatinine clearance.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-3 g dried powder or 500-1000 mg extract daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Immune-stimulating properties may aggravate autoimmune conditions by enhancing immune responses against self-tissue
contraindication_associations.notes: Use with practitioner guidance in autoimmune conditions; monitor for symptom flares

contraindication_associations.reason: Cordycepin has demonstrated antiplatelet and anticoagulant effects in laboratory studies
contraindication_associations.notes: Use with caution alongside anticoagulant medications. Discontinue 2 weeks before surgical procedures.

contraindication_associations.reason: May influence hormone levels including testosterone; potential concern for hormone-sensitive conditions
contraindication_associations.notes: Consult oncologist before use with hormone-sensitive cancers. Paradoxically, cordycepin shows anti-tumor properties in research.

description: An aromatic Mediterranean evergreen revered since antiquity as the herb of remembrance, Rosemary stimulates circulation, sharpens memory, and uplifts the spirits with its penetrating, camphoraceous fragrance. Greek students wore rosemary garlands during examinations to enhance recall, and Ophelia famously declared 'rosemary, that's for remembrance.' Modern research confirms its nootropic properties, with rosmarinic acid and 1,8-cineole demonstrating measurable improvements in cognitive performance and memory consolidation.
habitat: Rocky limestone hillsides, coastal cliffs, and dry scrubland throughout the Mediterranean region. Drought-resistant, sun-loving, and adapted to poor, alkaline soils. Widely cultivated as a culinary and ornamental herb.
energetic_quality: Warming, stimulating, clarifying. Pierces through mental fog and circulatory stagnation with focused, penetrating warmth.
doctrine_of_signatures: Its needle-like leaves, packed with aromatic oils, suggest penetrating, focused action — like needles stimulating acupuncture points. The persistent, evergreen nature of rosemary reflects the enduring quality of memory it strengthens.
safety_notes: Very safe as a culinary herb and moderate tea. Essential oil should not be ingested undiluted. High doses may cause seizures in susceptible individuals. Avoid therapeutic doses during pregnancy. May interact with blood-thinning and diabetes medications.

parts.typical_compounds: Rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid, 1,8-cineole, camphor, ursolic acid, luteolin, apigenin
parts.therapeutic_properties: Nootropic, circulatory stimulant, antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, digestive tonic, carminative, hepatoprotective
parts.notes: Leaves are the primary medicinal and culinary part. Used as tea, tincture, essential oil (inhalation and topical), culinary herb, and hair rinse. The simple act of inhaling rosemary essential oil has demonstrated cognitive enhancement in clinical studies.

parts.therapeutic_properties: Aromatic, mild nervine, uplifting, antioxidant
parts.notes: Delicate blue-purple flowers are included with leaves in preparations. Used in flower essences for memory and mental clarity. Attractive to bees.

planet_associations.notes: The Sun rules vitality, clarity of consciousness, and the warming life force. Rosemary's stimulating, clarifying, and warming properties reflect solar medicine of illuminated awareness.
Mercury governs memory, cognition, and the nervous system. Rosemary's renowned nootropic and memory-enhancing properties directly reflect Mercury's domain.

zodiac_associations.notes: Aries governs the head and cerebral function. Rosemary's affinity for the brain, memory, and mental stimulation reflects Arian medicine directed at the head.
Leo, ruled by the Sun, governs vitality and the warming heart. Rosemary's circulatory and heart-tonic properties reflect Leonine solar warmth.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Clinical studies demonstrate that inhaling rosemary essential oil significantly improves memory, alertness, and cognitive performance. 1,8-cineole levels in blood correlate directly with cognitive enhancement.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Inhale rosemary essential oil from a diffuser or tissue for 5-10 minutes before cognitive tasks. Alternatively, drink 1-2 cups rosemary tea daily.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Carnosic acid and volatile oils stimulate peripheral blood flow, warming cold extremities and improving delivery of oxygen and nutrients to tissues.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried rosemary per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes covered. Drink 2-3 cups daily. Topical: infused oil massaged into cold extremities.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Anti-inflammatory and circulatory-stimulating properties relieve tension headaches. The aromatic volatile oils have analgesic and antispasmodic effects on cranial vasculature.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: Dilute 2-3 drops essential oil in carrier oil and apply to temples and neck. Alternatively, inhale from a tissue or diffuser.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Bitter and aromatic compounds stimulate bile flow, digestive secretions, and peristalsis. Traditional Mediterranean after-meal digestive herb.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1 cup rosemary tea after meals. Combines well with other digestive herbs like peppermint and ginger.

ailment_associations.efficacy_notes: Circulatory stimulant that improves peripheral blood flow and warms cold extremities through gentle vasodilation.
ailment_associations.dosage_notes: 1-2 teaspoons dried herb per cup, steeped 10-15 minutes. Drink 2-3 cups daily.

contraindication_associations.reason: Emmenagogue properties may stimulate uterine contractions; camphor and volatile oils have potential teratogenic effects at high doses
contraindication_associations.notes: Culinary use is safe during pregnancy; avoid therapeutic doses (concentrated tea, tinctures, essential oil) during pregnancy

contraindication_associations.reason: Camphor and 1,8-cineole can lower seizure threshold; high-dose essential oil has been documented to cause seizures
contraindication_associations.notes: Contraindicated in epilepsy at therapeutic doses; culinary amounts are generally safe. Essential oil is the highest risk form.

contraindication_associations.reason: Circulatory stimulant properties may raise blood pressure in some individuals, particularly with concentrated preparations
contraindication_associations.notes: Monitor blood pressure with regular therapeutic use; culinary amounts unlikely to affect blood pressure significantly
